requirements.description: The system SHALL provide directional and scale reveal variants. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** an element has `.reveal-left`, `.reveal-right`, or `.reveal-scale` - **THEN** each SHALL have independent opacity and transform transitions using `var(--ease-out)` - **AND** `.reveal-left` SHALL enter from the left - **AND** `.reveal-right` SHALL enter from the right - **AND** `.reveal-scale` SHALL enter via scale-up animation - **AND** all variants SHALL be observed by the IntersectionObserver in core.js - **GIVEN** any `.reveal` or reveal-variant element - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the element SHALL have `will-change: opacity, transform` - **AND** the compositor SHALL promote the element to a GPU layer before the transition fires

scenarios.name: Directional reveal variants
scenarios.then: each SHALL have independent opacity and transform transitions using `var(--ease-out)`

scenarios.name: GPU-accelerated reveals
scenarios.then: the element SHALL have `will-change: opacity, transform`

requirements.name: Noise Texture
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide configurable noise texture overlay via CSS custom properties. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** `.noise-overlay` is used with `var(--noise-opacity)` - **THEN** the noise texture SHALL render at the defined opacity - **AND** dark theme SHALL use a higher opacity value for visibility on dark backgrounds

scenarios.name: Noise opacity token
scenarios.then: the noise texture SHALL render at the defined opacity

requirements.name: Neutral-to-Brand Color Ratio
requirements.description: The system SHALL maintain approximately 88% neutral / 12% brand color ratio. - **GIVEN** any page in the marketing website - **WHEN** the page is visually assessed - **THEN** the majority of surface area SHALL use neutral colors (--bg, --surface, --fg, --muted, --border) - **AND** brand colors (--accent, --teal, --green, --lime) SHALL be reserved for accent elements, CTAs, and highlights

scenarios.name: Neutral-dominant interface
scenarios.given: any page in the marketing website
scenarios.then: the majority of surface area SHALL use neutral colors (--bg, --surface, --fg, --muted, --border)

requirements.name: Light and Dark Themes
requirements.description: The system SHALL support light and dark themes via the `data-theme` attribute on the `<html>` element. - **GIVEN** `<html data-theme="light">` or no `data-theme` attribute - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** light-theme color values from `:root` SHALL be applied - **GIVEN** `<html data-theme="dark">` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** dark-theme color values from `[data-theme="dark"]` SHALL override `:root` values - **AND** shadow tokens SHALL switch to dark-mode shadows - **AND** noise texture opacity SHALL increase

scenarios.name: Light theme (default)
scenarios.then: light-theme color values from `:root` SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Dark theme
scenarios.then: dark-theme color values from `[data-theme="dark"]` SHALL override `:root` values

requirements.name: Typography
requirements.description: The system SHALL use Inter as the primary font family and JetBrains Mono for monospace. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** headings and body text render - **THEN** `--font-display` and `--font-body` SHALL resolve to Inter with system font fallbacks - **AND** `--font-mono` SHALL resolve to JetBrains Mono with monospace fallbacks

scenarios.name: Display and body text
scenarios.then: `--font-display` and `--font-body` SHALL resolve to Inter with system font fallbacks

requirements.name: Type Scale
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a complete type scale with responsive clamping. - **GIVEN** `<h1>` and `<h2>` elements - **WHEN** the page renders at any viewport width - **THEN** `h1` SHALL use `--fs-h1` (clamped between 36px and 68px) - **AND** `h2` SHALL use `--fs-h2` (clamped between 28px and 46px) - **AND** `h3` SHALL use `--fs-h3` (20px) - **GIVEN** `.lead`, `.body`, `.meta`, and `.eyebrow` elements - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** `.lead` SHALL use 18px, `.body` SHALL use 16px, `.meta` SHALL use 13px - **AND** `.eyebrow` SHALL use 12px uppercase monospace with teal color

scenarios.name: Heading sizes
scenarios.when: the page renders at any viewport width
scenarios.then: `h1` SHALL use `--fs-h1` (clamped between 36px and 68px)

scenarios.name: Body and utility sizes
scenarios.then: `.lead` SHALL use 18px, `.body` SHALL use 16px, `.meta` SHALL use 13px

requirements.name: 8px Baseline Grid
requirements.description: The system SHALL use an 8px baseline grid spacing system. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** any layout spacing is applied - **THEN** spacing SHALL use `var(--gap-*)` tokens: `--gap-xs` (8px), `--gap-sm` (12px), `--gap-md` (20px), `--gap-lg` (32px), `--gap-xl` (56px), `--gap-2xl` (96px)

scenarios.name: Gap tokens
scenarios.then: spacing SHALL use `var(--gap-*)` tokens: `--gap-xs` (8px), `--gap-sm` (12px), `--gap-md` (20px), `--gap-lg` (32px), `--gap-xl` (56px), `--gap-2xl` (96px)

requirements.name: Shared Components
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide shared component styles via utility classes in `core.css`. - **GIVEN** an element with `.btn-primary`, `.btn-secondary`, or `.btn-ghost` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** `.btn-primary` SHALL render as a gradient blue button with glow effect - **AND** `.btn-secondary` SHALL render as an outlined button with backdrop blur - **AND** `.btn-ghost` SHALL render as a transparent text button - **AND** all buttons SHALL have hover lift, active press, and focus-visible states - **GIVEN** an element with `.card` or `.glass-card` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** `.card` SHALL render with surface background, border, border-radius, and shadow - **AND** `.glass-card` SHALL render with backdrop blur and semi-transparent background - **AND** both SHALL have hover lift and border-color transition - **GIVEN** elements with `.grid-2`, `.grid-3`, `.grid-4`, or `.grid-5` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** child elements SHALL be arranged in responsive CSS Grid columns matching the class name - **AND** grids SHALL collapse to single column at mobile breakpoints - **GIVEN** FAQ markup with `.faq-item`, `.faq-question`, `.faq-answer` - **WHEN** a question is clicked - **THEN** the answer SHALL expand with a `grid-template-rows` transition - **AND** the icon SHALL rotate 45 degrees

scenarios.name: Button variants
scenarios.then: `.btn-primary` SHALL render as a gradient blue button with glow effect

scenarios.name: Card components
scenarios.then: `.card` SHALL render with surface background, border, border-radius, and shadow

scenarios.name: Grid layouts
scenarios.then: child elements SHALL be arranged in responsive CSS Grid columns matching the class name

scenarios.name: FAQ accordion
scenarios.then: the answer SHALL expand with a `grid-template-rows` transition

requirements.name: No Hardcoded Colors
requirements.description: The system SHALL NOT use hardcoded hex colors in page content outside the design system, except within `<style>` blocks, SVG `fill` attributes, and `core.css` token definitions. - **GIVEN** any page HTML content outside `<style>` blocks and SVG fills - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** hardcoded hex colors SHALL be flagged as warnings - **GIVEN** `core.css` defines CSS custom properties using OKLCH and hex values - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** hex values within `core.css` token definitions SHALL NOT be flagged

scenarios.name: Validation enforces variable usage
scenarios.given: any page HTML content outside `<style>` blocks and SVG fills
scenarios.then: hardcoded hex colors SHALL be flagged as warnings

scenarios.name: core.css is exempt from hex validation
scenarios.given: `core.css` defines CSS custom properties using OKLCH and hex values
scenarios.then: hex values within `core.css` token definitions SHALL NOT be flagged

requirements.name: Email Dispatch Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a POST endpoint at `/api/email` that accepts JSON with notification type, recipient data, and template context, and sends an email via the Resend API. - **WHEN** a POST request is received at `/api/email` with `{ "type": "contact", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com" }, "utm": {} }` - **THEN** an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Contact Form Submission — OptiFlow OS" - **AND** the email body SHALL be HTML with OptiFlow brand styling (logo, accent colors, footer) - **AND** the response SHALL be HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "type": "contact" }` - **WHEN** a POST request is received with `{ "type": "demo-booking", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com", "demoDate": "2026-07-15" } }` - **THEN** an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Demo Booking — OptiFlow OS" - **WHEN** a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }` - **THEN** an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Newsletter Signup — OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: Send team notification for contact form
scenarios.when: a POST request is received at `/api/email` with `{ "type": "contact", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com" }, "utm": {} }`
scenarios.then: an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Contact Form Submission — OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: Send team notification for demo booking
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with `{ "type": "demo-booking", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com", "demoDate": "2026-07-15" } }`
scenarios.then: an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Demo Booking — OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: Send team notification for newsletter
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }`
scenarios.then: an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Newsletter Signup — OptiFlow OS"

requirements.name: Subscriber Welcome Email
requirements.description: The system SHALL send a welcome confirmation email to the subscriber when a newsletter signup is received. - **WHEN** a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }` - **THEN** a welcome email SHALL be sent to `sub@example.com` with subject "Welcome to OptiFlow OS" - **AND** the welcome email SHALL include the OptiFlow OS brand logo, a brief introduction, and a link to the website - **AND** the welcome email SHALL include an unsubscribe notice - **WHEN** a POST request is received with type other than "newsletter" - **THEN** no subscriber-facing email SHALL be sent

scenarios.name: Welcome email sent to subscriber
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }`
scenarios.then: a welcome email SHALL be sent to `sub@example.com` with subject "Welcome to OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: No welcome email for non-newsletter types
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with type other than "newsletter"
scenarios.then: no subscriber-facing email SHALL be sent

requirements.name: HTML Email Templates
requirements.description: The system SHALL send HTML-formatted emails with OptiFlow brand styling for all notification types. - **WHEN** any team notification email is sent - **THEN** the email body SHALL contain the OptiFlow OS logo with alt text - **AND** SHALL use the brand accent color (`#1B4D81`) for headers and buttons - **AND** SHALL include a footer with company name "OptiFlow Tech Solutions" and location "Surat, India" - **AND** SHALL use table-based layout with inline styles for email client compatibility - **WHEN** an HTML email is sent - **THEN** the email SHALL include a plain-text alternative via the `text` field in the Resend API payload - **AND** the plain-text SHALL contain all substantive information from the HTML version

scenarios.name: Team notification email template
scenarios.when: any team notification email is sent
scenarios.then: the email body SHALL contain the OptiFlow OS logo with alt text

scenarios.name: HTML email renders with fallback
scenarios.then: the email SHALL include a plain-text alternative via the `text` field in the Resend API payload

requirements.name: Email Notification Logging
requirements.description: The system SHALL log every email attempt (both success and failure) to Cloudflare KV store for auditing and debugging. - **WHEN** an email is successfully sent via Resend API - **THEN** a KV entry SHALL be written with key `notif:<ISO-timestamp>-<random>` - **AND** the entry SHALL include: `timestamp`, `type`, `to`, `subject`, `success: true` - **WHEN** the Resend API call fails - **THEN** a KV entry SHALL be written with key `notif:<ISO-timestamp>-<random>` - **AND** the entry SHALL include: `timestamp`, `type`, `to`, `subject`, `success: false`, `error` - **WHEN** KV storage is unavailable during logging - **THEN** the email dispatch SHALL still succeed or fail independently - **AND** a console warning SHALL be logged

scenarios.name: Successful email logged
scenarios.when: an email is successfully sent via Resend API
scenarios.then: a KV entry SHALL be written with key `notif:<ISO-timestamp>-<random>`

scenarios.name: Failed email logged
scenarios.then: a KV entry SHALL be written with key `notif:<ISO-timestamp>-<random>`

scenarios.name: KV write failure does not fail the request
scenarios.when: KV storage is unavailable during logging
scenarios.then: the email dispatch SHALL still succeed or fail independently

requirements.name: Request Validation
requirements.description: The system SHALL validate incoming JSON request bodies on the email dispatch endpoint. - **WHEN** a POST request is received at `/api/email` without the `type` field - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Missing required field: type" }` - **WHEN** a POST request is received with an unknown `type` value - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Unknown notification type: <type>" }` - **WHEN** a POST request is received with malformed JSON - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Invalid JSON body" }`

scenarios.name: Missing type field
scenarios.when: a POST request is received at `/api/email` without the `type` field
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Missing required field: type" }`

scenarios.name: Unsupported notification type
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with an unknown `type` value
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Unknown notification type: <type>" }`

scenarios.name: Invalid JSON body
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with malformed JSON
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Invalid JSON body" }`

requirements.name: Graceful Degradation
requirements.description: The system SHALL ensure that email delivery failures never propagate as errors to the caller that submitted the notification request. - **WHEN** the Resend API is unreachable - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "emailSent": false }` - **WHEN** the Resend API returns HTTP 4xx or 5xx - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "emailSent": false }` - **AND** the error details SHALL be included in the log entry

scenarios.name: Resend API network error
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "emailSent": false }`

scenarios.name: Resend API non-2xx response
scenarios.when: the Resend API returns HTTP 4xx or 5xx
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "emailSent": false }`

requirements.name: Notification Log Schema
requirements.description: The system SHALL use a versioned, structured schema for notification log KV records. - **WHEN** a notification log is stored in KV - **THEN** the key SHALL be `notif:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}` - **AND** the value SHALL include `"schema": "notification.v1"` and `"id"` matching the UUID - **WHEN** the email worker writes notification logs - **THEN** logs SHALL be written to the `NOTIFICATIONS` KV namespace (not `SUBMISSIONS`) - **WHEN** KV write fails during logging - **THEN** the email dispatch SHALL still succeed or fail independently - **AND** a console warning SHALL be logged - **AND** error log entries SHALL exclude sensitive fields

scenarios.name: Notification key format
scenarios.when: a notification log is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the key SHALL be `notif:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}`

scenarios.name: KV namespace separation
scenarios.when: the email worker writes notification logs
scenarios.then: logs SHALL be written to the `NOTIFICATIONS` KV namespace (not `SUBMISSIONS`)

scenarios.name: KV failure non-blocking
scenarios.then: the email dispatch SHALL still succeed or fail independently

requirements.name: Self-Service Toolbar
requirements.description: The FAQ page SHALL display a self-service toolbar with quick-access cards linking to Video Tutorials, SOP Library resources, Guided Setup documentation, and a Troubleshooting section. The toolbar SHALL appear between the hero/search section and the FAQ categories. - **WHEN** a user visits the FAQ page - **THEN** four self-service cards SHALL be visible in a 4-column grid (stacked on mobile) - **AND** each card SHALL have an icon, title, short description, and CTA link - **WHEN** the user toggles dark mode - **THEN** toolbar cards SHALL use theme-appropriate background and text colors via CSS variables

scenarios.name: Toolbar renders with four cards
scenarios.when: a user visits the FAQ page
scenarios.then: four self-service cards SHALL be visible in a 4-column grid (stacked on mobile)

scenarios.name: Toolbar adapts to dark mode
scenarios.then: toolbar cards SHALL use theme-appropriate background and text colors via CSS variables

requirements.name: FAQ Feedback Buttons
requirements.description: Each FAQ answer SHALL display "Was this helpful?" feedback buttons (Yes/No) after the answer text. Voting SHALL persist in localStorage per question. After voting, a thank-you message SHALL replace the buttons. - **WHEN** a user clicks "Yes" on a previously unvoted FAQ item - **THEN** the vote SHALL be stored in localStorage - **AND** the feedback buttons SHALL be replaced with a "Thanks for your feedback!" message - **WHEN** a user clicks "No" on a previously unvoted FAQ item - **THEN** the vote SHALL be stored in localStorage - **AND** a follow-up prompt SHALL appear: "What were you looking for?" with a link to contact/demo - **WHEN** a user re-visits a question they previously voted on - **THEN** the feedback buttons SHALL not appear; the thank-you message SHALL show instead

scenarios.name: User votes Yes
scenarios.when: a user clicks "Yes" on a previously unvoted FAQ item
scenarios.then: the vote SHALL be stored in localStorage

scenarios.name: User votes No
scenarios.when: a user clicks "No" on a previously unvoted FAQ item
scenarios.then: the vote SHALL be stored in localStorage

scenarios.name: Already-voted question
scenarios.when: a user re-visits a question they previously voted on
scenarios.then: the feedback buttons SHALL not appear; the thank-you message SHALL show instead

requirements.name: Related Questions
requirements.description: Each FAQ answer SHALL display 2-3 related question links at the bottom of the answer body. Clicking a related question SHALL scroll to and expand that question. - **WHEN** a user expands an FAQ item - **THEN** related question links SHALL appear below the answer text and feedback section - **WHEN** a user clicks a related question link - **THEN** the target FAQ item SHALL expand - **AND** the page SHALL scroll smoothly to that item

scenarios.name: Related questions display
scenarios.when: a user expands an FAQ item
scenarios.then: related question links SHALL appear below the answer text and feedback section

scenarios.name: Clicking a related question
scenarios.when: a user clicks a related question link
scenarios.then: the target FAQ item SHALL expand

requirements.name: Troubleshooting Widget
requirements.description: The FAQ page SHALL include a guided troubleshooting widget with three cascading selectors: Issue Category, Specific Problem, and Resolution. Selecting a resolution SHALL display an action link to the appropriate resource. - **WHEN** a user selects Issue Category → Specific Problem → Resolution in sequence - **THEN** a resolution panel SHALL appear with a description and a CTA link (e.g., "Book Demo", "Contact Support", "View Pricing") - **WHEN** a user changes the Issue Category selector - **THEN** the Specific Problem and Resolution selectors SHALL reset to default - **AND** any visible resolution panel SHALL hide

scenarios.name: User completes the troubleshooting flow
scenarios.when: a user selects Issue Category → Specific Problem → Resolution in sequence
scenarios.then: a resolution panel SHALL appear with a description and a CTA link (e.g., "Book Demo", "Contact Support", "View Pricing")

scenarios.name: User changes category
scenarios.when: a user changes the Issue Category selector
scenarios.then: the Specific Problem and Resolution selectors SHALL reset to default

requirements.name: Escalation Section
requirements.description: The FAQ page SHALL display a "Still need help?" escalation section immediately before the bottom CTA, surfacing all contact options: demo booking, contact form, WhatsApp, and email. - **WHEN** a user scrolls to the escalation section - **THEN** contact cards for Demo, Contact, WhatsApp, and Email SHALL be visible - **AND** each card SHALL link to the appropriate destination

scenarios.name: Escalation section renders
scenarios.when: a user scrolls to the escalation section
scenarios.then: contact cards for Demo, Contact, WhatsApp, and Email SHALL be visible

requirements.name: Enhanced Search
requirements.description: The FAQ search SHALL display a live match count and highlight matching search terms in results and expanded answers. - **WHEN** a user types a search query with 2+ characters - **THEN** the search results SHALL display "X matches found" - **WHEN** a user clicks a search result - **THEN** the matching term SHALL be visually highlighted in the expanded answer using `<mark>` elements

scenarios.name: Search shows match count
scenarios.when: a user types a search query with 2+ characters
scenarios.then: the search results SHALL display "X matches found"

scenarios.name: Search highlights terms
scenarios.when: a user clicks a search result
scenarios.then: the matching term SHALL be visually highlighted in the expanded answer using `<mark>` elements

requirements.name: Feature Showcase Page Renders
requirements.description: The system SHALL render a Feature Showcase page at `/feature-showcase/` with 6 curated feature sections, each showing a Problem → Fix → Outcome transformation. - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/` directory - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `feature-showcase.html` SHALL exist with `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->` and `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->` - **GIVEN** `site.json` contains an entry for `feature-showcase/index.html` - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** `dist/feature-showcase/index.html` SHALL be generated

scenarios.name: Page source exists
scenarios.then: `feature-showcase.html` SHALL exist with `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->` and `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->`

scenarios.name: Build output
scenarios.then: `dist/feature-showcase/index.html` SHALL be generated

requirements.name: Showcase Sections
requirements.description: The page SHALL contain exactly 6 feature showcase sections, each labeled with a `data-screen-label` attribute. - **GIVEN** the assembled Feature Showcase page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** exactly 6 `<section class="feature-section">` elements SHALL be present for: Task Management, Attendance, SOPs, Reports, Checklists, and Leave Management - **AND** each section SHALL have a `data-screen-label` attribute (e.g., "01 Task Management", "02 Attendance", etc.) - **AND** a Hero section SHALL precede the feature sections with `data-screen-label="00 Hero"`

scenarios.name: Six feature sections
scenarios.then: exactly 6 `<section class="feature-section">` elements SHALL be present for: Task Management, Attendance, SOPs, Reports, Checklists, and Leave Management

requirements.name: Problem → Fix → Outcome Flow
requirements.description: Each feature showcase section SHALL follow a Problem → Fix → Outcome narrative structure. - **GIVEN** any feature showcase section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.showcase-problem` element SHALL describe a real operational problem with quantified impact - **GIVEN** any feature showcase section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 2-3 `.card` elements SHALL describe how OptiFlow solves that problem - **GIVEN** any feature showcase section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 3 `.showcase-metric` stat cards SHALL display quantified outcomes (e.g., completion rate, time saved, cost reduction)

scenarios.name: Problem card present
scenarios.then: a `.showcase-problem` element SHALL describe a real operational problem with quantified impact

scenarios.name: Solution cards
scenarios.then: 2-3 `.card` elements SHALL describe how OptiFlow solves that problem

scenarios.name: Outcome metrics
scenarios.then: 3 `.showcase-metric` stat cards SHALL display quantified outcomes (e.g., completion rate, time saved, cost reduction)

requirements.name: Dual-Device Visuals
requirements.description: Each feature showcase section SHALL include a visual comparison showing desktop and mobile views of the feature in action. - **GIVEN** any feature showcase section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.dashboard-preview` element SHALL represent the desktop view - **AND** a `.phone-frame` element SHALL represent the mobile view - **AND** both SHALL be visible side by side within a `.showcase-visual` container

scenarios.name: Desktop and mobile mockups
scenarios.then: a `.dashboard-preview` element SHALL represent the desktop view

requirements.name: Sticky Sub-Navigation
requirements.description: The page SHALL include a sticky feature sub-navigation bar below the main nav that highlights the currently visible section. - **GIVEN** the assembled Feature Showcase page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.feature-nav` element SHALL be present with 6 `.feature-tab` buttons - **AND** the active tab SHALL update via IntersectionObserver as sections scroll into view - **GIVEN** the page has scrolled past the sub-nav's initial position - **WHEN** the sub-nav intersects the viewport boundary - **THEN** the `.stuck` class SHALL be applied for shadow effect

scenarios.name: Sub-nav present
scenarios.then: a `.feature-nav` element SHALL be present with 6 `.feature-tab` buttons

scenarios.name: Stuck state
scenarios.given: the page has scrolled past the sub-nav's initial position
scenarios.when: the sub-nav intersects the viewport boundary
scenarios.then: the `.stuck` class SHALL be applied for shadow effect

requirements.name: Feature Showcase Metadata
requirements.description: The page SHALL use build-time placeholders for SEO metadata that resolve from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** the page source file `src/pages/feature-showcase.html` - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `<title>` SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` - **AND** `<meta name="description">` SHALL contain `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` - **GIVEN** the assembled page - **WHEN** rendered - **THEN** `og:image`, `og:type`, and `twitter:card` meta tags SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Placeholders used
scenarios.then: `<title>` SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}`

scenarios.name: OG and Twitter tags
scenarios.then: `og:image`, `og:type`, and `twitter:card` meta tags SHALL be present

requirements.name: Full Dark Mode Support
requirements.description: All page-specific components SHALL have complete `[data-theme="dark"]` style overrides. - **GIVEN** the page's `<style>` block - **WHEN** `<html data-theme="dark">` is active - **THEN** dark overrides SHALL exist for at minimum: `.showcase-problem`, `.showcase-metric`, `.feature-nav`, `.showcase-visual`, `.dashboard-preview`, `.phone-frame`, `.card`, and `.showcase-cta`

scenarios.name: Dark mode overrides
scenarios.then: dark overrides SHALL exist for at minimum: `.showcase-problem`, `.showcase-metric`, `.feature-nav`, `.showcase-visual`, `.dashboard-preview`, `.phone-frame`, `.card`, and `.showcase-cta`

requirements.name: No Duplicated Core Logic
requirements.description: Page-specific scripts SHALL NOT duplicate reveal, stagger, sticky-cta, or scroll-to-top logic already handled by `core.js`. - **GIVEN** the page's `<script>` block - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** it SHALL contain only `IntersectionObserver` logic for the feature sub-navigation - **AND** it SHALL NOT contain scroll-reveal, stagger, sticky-CTA, or scroll-to-top observers

scenarios.name: Only sub-nav logic is page-specific
scenarios.then: it SHALL contain only `IntersectionObserver` logic for the feature sub-navigation

requirements.name: Form Submission Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a POST endpoint at `/api/form-submit` that accepts JSON request bodies containing form field data, form metadata, and UTM parameters. - **WHEN** a POST request is received at `/api/form-submit` with valid JSON body containing `formName`, `fields`, and `honeyPot` set to empty string - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "message": "Submission received" }` - **AND** an email notification SHALL be sent via Resend - **AND** structured JSON SHALL be logged to the worker console - **WHEN** a POST request is received with missing `formName` or `fields` in the JSON body - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Missing required fields: formName, fields" }` - **AND** no email notification SHALL be sent - **WHEN** the `honeyPot` field contains any non-empty value - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true }` (to not reveal detection) - **AND** the submission SHALL be silently discarded - **AND** a "spam_detected" log entry SHALL be written

scenarios.name: Valid submission accepted
scenarios.when: a POST request is received at `/api/form-submit` with valid JSON body containing `formName`, `fields`, and `honeyPot` set to empty string
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "message": "Submission received" }`

scenarios.name: Missing required fields
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with missing `formName` or `fields` in the JSON body
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Missing required fields: formName, fields" }`

scenarios.name: Honey-pot detection
scenarios.when: the `honeyPot` field contains any non-empty value
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true }` (to not reveal detection)

requirements.name: Server-Side Field Validation
requirements.description: The system SHALL validate submitted form fields against per-form field schemas before accepting the submission. - **WHEN** a submission with `formName: "contact"` is received - **THEN** `fields.name`, `fields.email`, `fields.phone`, and `fields.company` SHALL be required and non-empty - **AND** `fields.email` SHALL match regex `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/` - **AND** `fields.phone` SHALL match regex `/^\+?\d{7,15}$/` - **AND** on validation failure, HTTP 422 SHALL be returned with per-field error messages - **WHEN** a submission with `formName: "demo-booking"` is received - **THEN** `fields.name`, `fields.email`, `fields.phone`, `fields.company` SHALL be required - **AND** `fields.demoDate` if present SHALL be a valid ISO date string - **AND** on validation failure, HTTP 422 SHALL be returned - **WHEN** a submission with `formName: "newsletter"` is received - **THEN** `fields.email` SHALL be required and valid - **AND** on validation failure, HTTP 422 SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Contact form field validation
scenarios.then: `fields.name`, `fields.email`, `fields.phone`, and `fields.company` SHALL be required and non-empty

scenarios.name: Demo booking form field validation
scenarios.then: `fields.name`, `fields.email`, `fields.phone`, `fields.company` SHALL be required

scenarios.name: Newsletter form field validation
scenarios.then: `fields.email` SHALL be required and valid

requirements.name: Rate Limiting
requirements.description: The system SHALL enforce a per-IP rate limit of 5 submissions per 10-minute sliding window. - **WHEN** a client IP has submitted fewer than 5 requests in the last 10 minutes - **THEN** the request SHALL be processed normally - **WHEN** a client IP has submitted 5 or more requests in the last 10 minutes - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 429 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Too many requests. Please try again later." }` - **AND** the Retry-After header SHALL be set

scenarios.name: Under rate limit
scenarios.when: a client IP has submitted fewer than 5 requests in the last 10 minutes
scenarios.then: the request SHALL be processed normally

scenarios.name: Rate limit exceeded
scenarios.when: a client IP has submitted 5 or more requests in the last 10 minutes
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 429 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Too many requests. Please try again later." }`

requirements.name: Email Notification
requirements.description: The system SHALL delegate email notification delivery to the email-notifications Worker at `/api/email` for every valid form submission. - **WHEN** a valid "contact" form submission is received - **THEN** the form-submit Worker SHALL POST to `/api/email` with `{ "type": "contact", "fields": <form fields>, "utm": <utm params> }` - **AND** the form-submit Worker SHALL NOT call the Resend API directly - **WHEN** a valid "demo-booking" form submission is received - **THEN** the form-submit Worker SHALL POST to `/api/email` with `{ "type": "demo-booking", "fields": <form fields>, "utm": <utm params> }` - **WHEN** a valid "newsletter" form submission is received - **THEN** the form-submit Worker SHALL POST to `/api/email` with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": <form fields>, "utm": <utm params> }` - **WHEN** the POST to `/api/email` fails or returns `emailSent: false` - **THEN** the submission SHALL still be accepted (HTTP 200) - **AND** a warning SHALL be logged to the worker console - **AND** the response body SHALL include `"emailSent": false` - **WHEN** the POST to `/api/email` returns HTTP 200 with `emailSent: true` - **THEN** the response body SHALL include `"emailSent": true`

scenarios.name: Email dispatched for contact form
scenarios.when: a valid "contact" form submission is received
scenarios.then: the form-submit Worker SHALL POST to `/api/email` with `{ "type": "contact", "fields": <form fields>, "utm": <utm params> }`

scenarios.name: Email dispatched for demo booking
scenarios.when: a valid "demo-booking" form submission is received
scenarios.then: the form-submit Worker SHALL POST to `/api/email` with `{ "type": "demo-booking", "fields": <form fields>, "utm": <utm params> }`

scenarios.name: Email dispatched for newsletter
scenarios.when: a valid "newsletter" form submission is received
scenarios.then: the form-submit Worker SHALL POST to `/api/email` with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": <form fields>, "utm": <utm params> }`

scenarios.name: Email Worker failure degrades gracefully
scenarios.when: the POST to `/api/email` fails or returns `emailSent: false`
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL still be accepted (HTTP 200)

scenarios.name: Email Worker returns success
scenarios.when: the POST to `/api/email` returns HTTP 200 with `emailSent: true`
scenarios.then: the response body SHALL include `"emailSent": true`

requirements.name: Structured Logging
requirements.description: The system SHALL log each submission as a structured JSON object with timestamp, form name, field keys (not values for PII), IP, UTM params, and spam status. - **WHEN** a valid submission is processed - **THEN** a JSON log entry SHALL be written with keys: `timestamp`, `formName`, `fieldKeys`, `ip`, `utm`, `spam`, `emailSent` - **AND** field values SHALL be excluded from the log for PII protection - **WHEN** a honey-pot detection occurs - **THEN** a JSON log entry SHALL be written with `spam: true` and no field data

scenarios.name: Log entry for valid submission
scenarios.then: a JSON log entry SHALL be written with keys: `timestamp`, `formName`, `fieldKeys`, `ip`, `utm`, `spam`, `emailSent`

scenarios.name: Log entry for spam detection
scenarios.then: a JSON log entry SHALL be written with `spam: true` and no field data

requirements.name: Cross-Platform Compatibility
requirements.description: The system SHALL function as both a Cloudflare Worker and a Netlify Function using a shared validation and notification library. - **WHEN** deployed to Cloudflare Pages via `functions/api/form-submit.js` - **THEN** the worker SHALL handle POST requests and return valid JSON responses - **WHEN** deployed to Netlify via `netlify/functions/form-submit.js` - **THEN** the function SHALL handle POST requests and return valid JSON responses

scenarios.name: Cloudflare Worker deployment
scenarios.then: the worker SHALL handle POST requests and return valid JSON responses

scenarios.name: Netlify Function deployment
scenarios.then: the function SHALL handle POST requests and return valid JSON responses

requirements.name: KV Data Schema
requirements.description: The system SHALL use versioned data schemas and structured key naming for all KV records. - **WHEN** a valid submission is stored in KV - **THEN** the key SHALL be `sub:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}` - **AND** the value SHALL include `"schema": "submission.v1"` and `"id"` matching the UUID - **WHEN** a submission is stored - **THEN** the `ip` field SHALL be SHA-256 hashed before storage - **WHEN** the worker is deployed - **THEN** it SHALL bind to `SUBMISSIONS`, `NOTIFICATIONS`, and `AUDIT` KV namespaces

scenarios.name: Submission key format
scenarios.when: a valid submission is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the key SHALL be `sub:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}`

scenarios.name: Hashed IP storage
scenarios.then: the `ip` field SHALL be SHA-256 hashed before storage

scenarios.name: KV namespace bindings
scenarios.then: it SHALL bind to `SUBMISSIONS`, `NOTIFICATIONS`, and `AUDIT` KV namespaces

requirements.name: Newsletter Subscriber Persistence
requirements.description: The system SHALL persist newsletter subscribers with email-based deduplication. - **WHEN** a newsletter signup is accepted - **THEN** a record SHALL be stored at `subscriber:{sha256(email)}` - **AND** the value SHALL include `email`, `subscribedAt`, `source`, and `firstSeen` - **WHEN** an existing subscriber re-subscribes - **THEN** `subscribedAt` SHALL be updated while preserving `firstSeen`

scenarios.name: Subscriber stored
scenarios.then: a record SHALL be stored at `subscriber:{sha256(email)}`

scenarios.name: Duplicate subscriber
scenarios.then: `subscribedAt` SHALL be updated while preserving `firstSeen`

requirements.name: Audit Logging on Write
requirements.description: The system SHALL write an audit entry for each successful form submission. - **WHEN** a submission is accepted and stored - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_write"` and the submission key in `resource` - **AND** KV audit write failure SHALL NOT fail the submission

scenarios.name: Audit entry written
scenarios.when: a submission is accepted and stored
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_write"` and the submission key in `resource`

requirements.name: Shared hook utilities
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `hooks/_utils.mjs` module exporting common utilities used by all hook scripts: `ROOT`, `ASSETS`, `SRC_PAGES`, `SRC_PARTIALS` path constants; `readText(fp)` file reader; `fail(msg)` and `ok(msg)` logging helpers; `walkDir(dir, exts)` recursive file walker; and `findDesignMd()` DESIGN.md locator. - **WHEN** any hook script in `hooks/` is executed - **THEN** it SHALL import path constants and helpers from `hooks/_utils.mjs` instead of defining its own `ROOT`, `readText`, `fail`, `ok`, `walkSrc`, or `findDesignMd` functions - **WHEN** `walkDir('/path/to/dir', ['.html', '.css'])` is called - **THEN** only files ending in `.html` or `.css` SHALL be returned - **AND** subdirectories SHALL be traversed recursively - **AND** directories that don't exist SHALL return an empty array

scenarios.name: All hooks import shared utilities
scenarios.when: any hook script in `hooks/` is executed
scenarios.then: it SHALL import path constants and helpers from `hooks/_utils.mjs` instead of defining its own `ROOT`, `readText`, `fail`, `ok`, `walkSrc`, or `findDesignMd` functions

scenarios.name: walkDir filters by extension
scenarios.then: only files ending in `.html` or `.css` SHALL be returned

requirements.name: Commit message format enforcement
requirements.description: The pre-commit hook SHALL validate that the commit message follows the Conventional Commits format: `type(scope): description` where type is one of `feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore|perf|ci|style`. - **WHEN** `git commit -m "feat: add post-merge hook"` is executed - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the commit message check - **WHEN** `git commit -m "added post-merge hook"` is executed - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with an error listing the allowed types - **WHEN** `git commit -m "fix(hooks): resolve globalResults ordering"` is executed - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the commit message check - **WHEN** `git commit --amend` is executed - **THEN** the commit message check SHALL be skipped

scenarios.name: Valid commit message passes
scenarios.when: `git commit -m "feat: add post-merge hook"` is executed
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the commit message check

scenarios.name: Invalid commit message fails
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with an error listing the allowed types

scenarios.name: Scoped commit message passes
scenarios.when: `git commit -m "fix(hooks): resolve globalResults ordering"` is executed
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the commit message check

scenarios.name: Amend commits bypass message check
scenarios.then: the commit message check SHALL be skipped

requirements.name: Branch name convention enforcement
requirements.description: The pre-commit hook SHALL validate that the current branch name matches one of the allowed patterns: `main`, `staging`, `develop`, `feature/*`, `fix/*`, `chore/*`, `docs/*`. - **WHEN** a commit is made on branch `feature/new-hooks` - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the branch name check - **WHEN** a commit is made on branch `random-stuff` - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with an error listing allowed branch prefixes - **WHEN** a commit is made on branch `main` - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the branch name check without requiring a prefix

scenarios.name: Valid branch name passes
scenarios.when: a commit is made on branch `feature/new-hooks`
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the branch name check

scenarios.name: Invalid branch name fails
scenarios.when: a commit is made on branch `random-stuff`
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with an error listing allowed branch prefixes

scenarios.name: Main branch passes
scenarios.when: a commit is made on branch `main`
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL pass the branch name check without requiring a prefix

requirements.name: Post-merge dependency drift detection
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a post-merge hook at `hooks/post-merge.mjs` that detects changes to `package-lock.json` since the merge and runs `npm install` if needed. - **WHEN** a merge does NOT change `package-lock.json` - **THEN** the post-merge hook SHALL exit silently with code 0 - **WHEN** a merge changes `package-lock.json` - **THEN** the post-merge hook SHALL run `npm install` - **AND** SHALL report success or failure - **WHEN** `npm install` fails during post-merge (e.g., network error) - **THEN** the hook SHALL warn the user but exit with code 0 - **WHEN** a merge changes `site.json` or `assets/css/core.css` - **THEN** the post-merge hook SHALL warn the user to run `npm run build`

scenarios.name: No lockfile change skips install
scenarios.then: the post-merge hook SHALL exit silently with code 0

scenarios.name: Lockfile changed runs install
scenarios.then: the post-merge hook SHALL run `npm install`

scenarios.name: Install failure warns but does not block
scenarios.when: `npm install` fails during post-merge (e.g., network error)
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL warn the user but exit with code 0

scenarios.name: Site data or CSS changed triggers rebuild warning
scenarios.then: the post-merge hook SHALL warn the user to run `npm run build`

requirements.name: Pre-push validation gate
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a pre-push hook at `hooks/pre-push.mjs` that runs lint and validate checks before allowing a push to proceed. - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and `npm run lint:all && npm run validate` both pass - **THEN** the push SHALL proceed - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and linting finds errors - **THEN** the hook SHALL exit with code 1 - **AND** the push SHALL be blocked - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and validation finds errors - **THEN** the hook SHALL exit with code 1 - **AND** the push SHALL be blocked

scenarios.name: Clean lint and validate allows push
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and `npm run lint:all && npm run validate` both pass
scenarios.then: the push SHALL proceed

scenarios.name: Lint failure blocks push
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and linting finds errors
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Validate failure blocks push
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and validation finds errors
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL exit with code 1

requirements.name: Hook lifecycle integration
requirements.description: The orchestration engine's `hook-engine.mjs` SHALL include post-merge and pre-push events in the lifecycle phase mappings. - **WHEN** the orchestration engine runs the `apply` phase - **THEN** the lifecycle SHALL include `post-merge` in addition to existing `pre-build`, `post-build`, and `theme-change` - **WHEN** `executeLifecycle` processes global hooks - **THEN** the `globalResults` array SHALL contain results in deterministic order matching project hooks behavior

scenarios.name: Apply phase includes post-merge
scenarios.when: the orchestration engine runs the `apply` phase
scenarios.then: the lifecycle SHALL include `post-merge` in addition to existing `pre-build`, `post-build`, and `theme-change`

scenarios.name: globalResults ordering is fixed
scenarios.then: the `globalResults` array SHALL contain results in deterministic order matching project hooks behavior

requirements.name: Shared Form Submission
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `submitForm()` function in `core.js` that handles form submission with a state machine (idle → submitting → success → error → idle). - **WHEN** a user submits a validated form with a configured endpoint - **THEN** the form SHALL enter `submitting` state showing a loading indicator - **AND** the form data SHALL be POSTed to the endpoint - **AND** a honey-pot field SHALL be injected if not present - **AND** on 2xx response, if the response body contains `success: true`, the form SHALL transition to `success` state - **AND** if the response body contains `success: false`, the form SHALL transition to `error` state with the API error message - **WHEN** a form submission fails (network error or non-2xx response) - **THEN** the form SHALL transition to `error` state showing the error message - **AND** a retry button SHALL be displayed - **AND** clicking retry SHALL re-enter `submitting` state and re-POST - **WHEN** a form is in `error` state and the user clicks retry - **THEN** the form SHALL re-submit - **AND** on failure, the form SHALL return to `error` state with the new error message

scenarios.name: Successful submission
scenarios.when: a user submits a validated form with a configured endpoint
scenarios.then: the form SHALL enter `submitting` state showing a loading indicator

scenarios.name: Submission error
scenarios.when: a form submission fails (network error or non-2xx response)
scenarios.then: the form SHALL transition to `error` state showing the error message

scenarios.name: Form returns to idle on retry
scenarios.when: a form is in `error` state and the user clicks retry
scenarios.then: the form SHALL re-submit

requirements.name: Endpoint Configuration
requirements.description: Forms SHALL configure their submission target via `data-endpoint` attribute on the `<form>` element. The `data-netlify` attribute provides a Netlify Forms fallback. - **WHEN** a form has `data-netlify="true"` attribute - **THEN** the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` - **AND** `form-name` SHALL be sent as a hidden field with the form's `name` attribute value - **AND** the honey-pot field value SHALL be included in the payload - **WHEN** a form has `data-endpoint` set to a URL (not Netlify Forms) - **THEN** the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/json` - **AND** form data SHALL be sent as a JSON object with `formName`, `fields`, `honeyPot`, and `utm` keys - **WHEN** a form has no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` attribute - **THEN** the submission SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with JSON content type - **AND** the form SHALL NOT log a warning or show an error

scenarios.name: Netlify Forms endpoint
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`

scenarios.name: JSON API endpoint
scenarios.when: a form has `data-endpoint` set to a URL (not Netlify Forms)
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/json`

scenarios.name: Missing endpoint defaults to API
scenarios.when: a form has no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` attribute
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with JSON content type

requirements.name: UTM Parameter Capture
requirements.description: The system SHALL capture UTM parameters (`utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`, `utm_content`, `utm_term`) from the current URL and include them in form submission payloads. - **WHEN** the URL contains `?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=launch` - **THEN** the form payload SHALL include `utm_source: "linkedin"`, `utm_medium: "cpc"`, `utm_campaign: "launch"` - **WHEN** the URL contains no UTM parameters - **THEN** the form payload SHALL NOT include any `utm_*` fields

scenarios.name: UTM params present
scenarios.then: the form payload SHALL include `utm_source: "linkedin"`, `utm_medium: "cpc"`, `utm_campaign: "launch"`

scenarios.name: No UTM params
scenarios.when: the URL contains no UTM parameters
scenarios.then: the form payload SHALL NOT include any `utm_*` fields

requirements.name: Form State Styling
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide CSS classes for form submission states applied to the form element. - **WHEN** a form has class `form-submitting` - **THEN** the submit button SHALL be disabled - **AND** the submit button SHALL display a loading indicator (CSS spinner) - **AND** all form inputs SHALL be disabled - **WHEN** a form has class `form-success` - **THEN** form fields SHALL be hidden - **AND** a success message SHALL be displayed in their place - **WHEN** a form has class `form-error` - **THEN** the error message SHALL be displayed above the submit button - **AND** a retry button SHALL be shown

scenarios.name: Submitting state
scenarios.then: the submit button SHALL be disabled

scenarios.name: Success state
scenarios.then: form fields SHALL be hidden

scenarios.name: Error state
scenarios.then: the error message SHALL be displayed above the submit button

requirements.name: Honey-Pot Spam Detection
requirements.description: All forms using `submitForm()` SHALL include an invisible honey-pot field that bots auto-fill but humans never see or interact with. - **WHEN** a form with `data-endpoint` or `data-netlify` is submitted - **THEN** `submitForm()` SHALL append a hidden `<input>` with name `_hp`, `tabindex="-1"`, and `autocomplete="off"` if one does not already exist - **AND** the honey-pot field SHALL be visually hidden via CSS (`position: absolute; left: -9999px`) - **WHEN** a form already contains `<input name="_hp" ...>` in the HTML source - **THEN** `submitForm()` SHALL NOT inject a duplicate honey-pot field

scenarios.name: Honey-pot field injected
scenarios.when: a form with `data-endpoint` or `data-netlify` is submitted
scenarios.then: `submitForm()` SHALL append a hidden `<input>` with name `_hp`, `tabindex="-1"`, and `autocomplete="off"` if one does not already exist

scenarios.name: Honey-pot field present in source
scenarios.when: a form already contains `<input name="_hp" ...>` in the HTML source
scenarios.then: `submitForm()` SHALL NOT inject a duplicate honey-pot field

requirements.name: Default API Endpoint
requirements.description: When no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` attribute are present, `submitForm()` SHALL default to POSTing to `/api/form-submit`. - **WHEN** a form has neither `data-endpoint` nor `data-netlify` - **THEN** `submitForm()` SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with `Content-Type: application/json` - **AND** the payload SHALL include `formName` (from the form's `name` attribute), `fields` (object of all named inputs), `honeyPot` (value of `_hp` field), and `utm` (captured UTM params)

scenarios.name: Default endpoint used
scenarios.then: `submitForm()` SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with `Content-Type: application/json`

requirements.name: Privacy Policy Page
requirements.description: The system SHALL render a Privacy Policy page at `/privacy-policy/` with a hero section, sticky side navigation, and 8 content sections presented as bordered content cards. - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/privacy-policy.html` source file - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** `dist/privacy-policy/index.html` SHALL be generated - **AND** the page SHALL contain a `<title>` matching the `site.json` entry - **AND** the page SHALL contain the shared navigation and footer includes - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a hero section SHALL display an "LEGAL & COMPLIANCE" eyebrow, the page title "Privacy Policy", a lead paragraph, and version/effective/updated metadata - **AND** the hero SHALL have a centered layout with a radial gradient background effect - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 9 content sections SHALL be present: Introduction, Information Collection, Data Usage, Data Storage, Cookies, Third-Party Services, Your Rights, Security, Contact - **AND** each section SHALL have a unique `id` attribute for side navigation linking - **AND** each section SHALL contain an inline SVG icon in the heading

scenarios.name: Page assembles correctly
scenarios.then: `dist/privacy-policy/index.html` SHALL be generated

scenarios.name: Hero section renders
scenarios.then: a hero section SHALL display an "LEGAL & COMPLIANCE" eyebrow, the page title "Privacy Policy", a lead paragraph, and version/effective/updated metadata

scenarios.name: Content sections render
scenarios.then: 9 content sections SHALL be present: Introduction, Information Collection, Data Usage, Data Storage, Cookies, Third-Party Services, Your Rights, Security, Contact

requirements.name: Side Navigation
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL include a sticky side navigation that highlights the currently visible section based on scroll position. - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy or terms page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<aside class="side-nav">` element SHALL be present - **AND** it SHALL contain links to all content sections on the page - **AND** it SHALL have a "On this page" header in monospace uppercase - **GIVEN** the side navigation is rendered - **WHEN** the user scrolls to a content section - **THEN** the corresponding side nav link SHALL have class `active` - **AND** only one link SHALL be active at any time - **AND** the active link SHALL use `--accent-soft` background - **GIVEN** the side navigation is rendered - **WHEN** the page scrolls - **THEN** the side nav SHALL remain visible at `top: calc(var(--nav-h) + 24px)` - **GIVEN** the viewport is 1024px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the side nav SHALL be hidden via `display: none`

scenarios.name: Side nav renders
scenarios.given: the assembled privacy-policy or terms page
scenarios.then: a `<aside class="side-nav">` element SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Active section tracking
scenarios.when: the user scrolls to a content section
scenarios.then: the corresponding side nav link SHALL have class `active`

scenarios.name: Sticky positioning
scenarios.then: the side nav SHALL remain visible at `top: calc(var(--nav-h) + 24px)`

scenarios.name: Hidden on tablet
scenarios.then: the side nav SHALL be hidden via `display: none`

requirements.name: Reading Progress Bar
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL display a fixed reading progress bar at the top of the viewport. - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy or terms page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<div class="progress-bar">` SHALL be present with `position: fixed` and `z-index: 101` - **AND** the bar SHALL have a gradient background from `--accent` to `--teal` - **GIVEN** the page is scrollable - **WHEN** the user scrolls - **THEN** the progress bar width SHALL be `(scrollTop / scrollableHeight) * 100` percent - **AND** the scroll listener SHALL use `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Progress bar renders
scenarios.given: the assembled privacy-policy or terms page
scenarios.then: a `<div class="progress-bar">` SHALL be present with `position: fixed` and `z-index: 101`

scenarios.name: Progress updates on scroll
scenarios.then: the progress bar width SHALL be `(scrollTop / scrollableHeight) * 100` percent

requirements.name: Content Cards
requirements.description: Legal page sections SHALL be rendered as content cards with border, background surface, and consistent internal spacing. - **GIVEN** any content section on a legal page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the section SHALL have class `content-card` with background `var(--surface)`, border `var(--border)`, and border-radius `var(--radius-lg)` - **AND** the heading SHALL have a bottom border separating it from the body text - **AND** paragraph text SHALL use `line-height: 1.7` - **GIVEN** a content card containing informational sub-sections - **WHEN** the card renders - **THEN** `.info-block` elements SHALL render with a subtle background and border - **AND** each info block SHALL have a bold heading and muted body text

scenarios.name: Card styling
scenarios.given: any content section on a legal page
scenarios.then: the section SHALL have class `content-card` with background `var(--surface)`, border `var(--border)`, and border-radius `var(--radius-lg)`

scenarios.name: Info blocks within cards
scenarios.given: a content card containing informational sub-sections
scenarios.then: `.info-block` elements SHALL render with a subtle background and border

requirements.name: Cookie Classification Grid
requirements.description: The Privacy Policy page SHALL classify cookies used by the Platform in a 4-column grid of cards. - **GIVEN** the Cookies section of the privacy policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.cookie-grid` SHALL display exactly 4 `.cookie-card` items: Essential, Analytics, Performance, Preferences - **AND** each card SHALL contain an SVG icon, heading, and description

scenarios.name: Four cookie types displayed
scenarios.given: the Cookies section of the privacy policy page

requirements.name: Company Data Placeholders

requirements.name: Print Styles
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL include print-specific CSS to produce clean printed output. - **GIVEN** the page is printed via `@media print` - **WHEN** the print stylesheet applies - **THEN** navigation, mobile drawer, hamburger, progress bar, side nav, footer, breadcrumbs, and skip link SHALL be hidden - **AND** content cards SHALL render without borders or shadows - **AND** links SHALL render in black with underline - **AND** paragraph and list items SHALL enforce orphans:3 and widows:3

scenarios.then: navigation, mobile drawer, hamburger, progress bar, side nav, footer, breadcrumbs, and skip link SHALL be hidden

requirements.name: Dark Mode Support
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL render correctly in both light and dark themes. - **GIVEN** the page's `<style>` block - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.content-card`, `.side-nav a`, `.info-block`, `.hero-section::before`, `.rights-grid .card` - **AND** all color values in dark mode rules SHALL use `color-mix()`, `var()`, or CSS custom properties

scenarios.name: Dark mode overrides
scenarios.then: `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.content-card`, `.side-nav a`, `.info-block`, `.hero-section::before`, `.rights-grid .card`

requirements.name: Responsive Layout
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL adapt layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports. - **GIVEN** viewport is 1024px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the content layout SHALL collapse to single column - **AND** the side nav SHALL be hidden - **AND** cookie, rights, and security grids SHALL display 2 columns - **GIVEN** viewport is 768px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** content cards SHALL reduce padding to 28px - **AND** cookie, rights, security grids SHALL collapse to single column - **AND** data flow arrows SHALL rotate 90 degrees to vertical - **GIVEN** viewport is 480px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** hero metadata SHALL stack vertically - **AND** content cards SHALL reduce padding to 20px 16px - **AND** headings SHALL reduce font size - **AND** the sticky CTA SHALL expand to full width

scenarios.name: Tablet layout (≤1024px)
scenarios.then: the content layout SHALL collapse to single column

scenarios.name: Mobile layout (≤768px)
scenarios.then: content cards SHALL reduce padding to 28px

scenarios.name: Small mobile (≤480px)
scenarios.then: hero metadata SHALL stack vertically

requirements.name: Shared Component Compliance
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL use the project's shared components and SHALL NOT duplicate shared logic. - **GIVEN** the privacy-policy or terms page has a form (if applicable) - **WHEN** the page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no duplicate `fetch()`, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL exist for form submission - **GIVEN** the privacy-policy page scripts - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** no duplicate sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **AND** scroll event listeners SHALL use `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Uses shared form logic
scenarios.given: the privacy-policy or terms page has a form (if applicable)
scenarios.then: no duplicate `fetch()`, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL exist for form submission

scenarios.name: Uses shared scroll/animation logic
scenarios.then: no duplicate sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

requirements.name: Page Inventory
requirements.description: The system SHALL render exactly 14 marketing pages as defined in the `site.json` pages array. The FAQ page SHALL now include interactive self-service components beyond its original static Q&A format. The demo booking and contact pages SHALL use the shared `submitForm()` function from core.js for real form submission instead of `alert()`. - **GIVEN** `site.json` contains entries for Home, Problem-Solutions, Product-Overview, Features, Feature-Showcase, Why-OptiFlow, Pricing, Newsletter, FAQ, Contact, Demo-Booking, Privacy-Policy, Terms, and Competitive-Positioning - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** all 14 pages SHALL be generated in `dist/` with corresponding directory structures - **AND** only pages defined in `site.json` SHALL be generated - **AND** the FAQ page SHALL include self-service toolbar, feedback buttons, troubleshooting widget, and escalation section - **GIVEN** the assembled demo-booking page - **WHEN** a user fills and submits the booking form - **THEN** the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()` - **AND** the form SHALL display submitting/success/error states - **AND** the calendar slot data SHALL be included in the payload - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page - **WHEN** a user fills and submits the contact form - **THEN** the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()` - **AND** the form SHALL display submitting/success/error states

scenarios.name: All pages present
scenarios.given: `site.json` contains entries for Home, Problem-Solutions, Product-Overview, Features, Feature-Showcase, Why-OptiFlow, Pricing, Newsletter, FAQ, Contact, Demo-Booking, Privacy-Policy, Terms, and Competitive-Positioning
scenarios.then: all 14 pages SHALL be generated in `dist/` with corresponding directory structures

scenarios.name: Demo booking form submits to endpoint
scenarios.when: a user fills and submits the booking form
scenarios.then: the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()`

scenarios.name: Contact form submits to endpoint
scenarios.when: a user fills and submits the contact form
scenarios.then: the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()`

requirements.name: Unique Metadata
requirements.description: Each page SHALL have a unique `<title>`, `<meta name="description">`, OpenGraph tags, and canonical URL. All SEO metadata in page source files SHALL use build-time placeholders that resolve from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** any page source file in `src/pages/` - **WHEN** the file is inspected - **THEN** the `<title>` tag SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` as its content - **AND** the title SHALL NOT be hardcoded in the source file - **GIVEN** any page source file in `src/pages/` - **WHEN** the file is inspected - **THEN** the `<meta name="description">` tag SHALL use `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` as its content attribute - **AND** the `<meta property="og:description">` tag SHALL use `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` as its content attribute - **AND** the description SHALL NOT be hardcoded in the source file - **GIVEN** a page entry in `site.json` with `title` and `description` fields - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the `<title>` tag SHALL match the page's `title` value - **AND** the `<meta name="description">` SHALL match the page's `description` value (120-160 characters) - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL contain `og:title` and `og:description` meta tags - **AND** it SHALL contain `og:image` pointing to the OptiFlow logo - **AND** it SHALL contain `og:type` set to "website" - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL contain a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag

scenarios.name: Title uses placeholder
scenarios.then: the `<title>` tag SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` as its content

scenarios.name: Description uses placeholder
scenarios.then: the `<meta name="description">` tag SHALL use `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` as its content attribute

scenarios.name: Title and description
scenarios.given: a page entry in `site.json` with `title` and `description` fields
scenarios.then: the `<title>` tag SHALL match the page's `title` value

scenarios.name: OpenGraph tags
scenarios.then: it SHALL contain `og:title` and `og:description` meta tags

scenarios.name: Twitter card
scenarios.then: it SHALL contain a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag

requirements.name: Sticky Navigation
requirements.description: Each page SHALL include a sticky navigation bar with glass effect. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a fixed-position `<header class="topnav">` SHALL be present - **AND** it SHALL contain the OptiFlow logo, desktop nav links, theme toggle, and CTA buttons - **AND** the nav SHALL acquire a glass blur background on scroll past 20px

requirements.name: Shared Footer
requirements.description: Each page SHALL include a shared footer with company info, product links, and contact details. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<footer class="pagefoot">` SHALL be present - **AND** it SHALL contain branded columns (Product, Solutions, Resources, Contact) - **AND** it SHALL display company info: phone, email, and location from `site.json`

requirements.name: Scroll-Reveal Animations
requirements.description: Each page SHALL use scroll-reveal animations for content sections. - **GIVEN** page sections marked with `.reveal` class - **WHEN** a section scrolls into the viewport - **THEN** it SHALL transition from translateY(28px) and opacity 0 to visible state - **AND** staggered children SHALL animate sequentially via `--delay` values

scenarios.when: a section scrolls into the viewport
scenarios.then: it SHALL transition from translateY(28px) and opacity 0 to visible state

requirements.name: Source File Naming
requirements.description: Source page files SHALL be named to match their purpose. The newsletter page source SHALL be `newsletter.html` (not `blog.html`). The feature showcase page source SHALL be `feature-showcase.html`. - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/` directory - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `feature-showcase.html` SHALL exist - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/` directory - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `newsletter.html` SHALL exist - **AND** `blog.html` SHALL NOT exist - **GIVEN** the SRC_MAP in `assemble.mjs` - **WHEN** the newsletter entry is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL map `newsletter/index.html` → `newsletter.html` - **GIVEN** the SRC_MAP in `assemble.mjs` - **WHEN** the feature showcase entry is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL map `feature-showcase/index.html` → `feature-showcase.html`

scenarios.name: Build mapping for newsletter

scenarios.name: Build mapping for feature showcase
scenarios.when: the feature showcase entry is inspected
scenarios.then: it SHALL map `feature-showcase/index.html` → `feature-showcase.html`

requirements.name: Page Structure
requirements.description: The system SHALL maintain a consistent page structure on every page. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** the content SHALL follow the order: navigation (`<header>`) → main content (`<main>`) → footer (`<footer>`) - **AND** a skip-to-content link SHALL precede the navigation - **AND** the main content area SHALL have `id="content"`

scenarios.then: the content SHALL follow the order: navigation (`<header>`) → main content (`<main>`) → footer (`<footer>`)

requirements.name: Shared Logic Deduplication
requirements.description: Page-specific scripts SHALL NOT duplicate logic already handled by `core.js`. Sticky CTA visibility, scroll-to-top, scroll reveal, stagger animation, FAQ accordion, counter animation, AND form submission SHALL be handled exclusively by core.js. Page-specific CSS SHALL NOT redefine stagger animation keyframes or transition rules already provided by core.css `.stagger-3`/`.stagger-4`/`.stagger-5` utility classes. - **GIVEN** the newsletter page source at `src/pages/newsletter.html` - **WHEN** the `<style>` block is inspected - **THEN** no `.stagger > *` or `.stagger.visible > *:nth-child()` animation rules SHALL be present - **AND** all stagger animations SHALL use core.css `.stagger-N` utility classes - **GIVEN** the home, pricing, privacy-policy, and terms pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **GIVEN** the home and pricing pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no scroll-to-top scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **GIVEN** the product-overview and newsletter pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no reveal or stagger IntersectionObserver SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **GIVEN** the demo-booking and contact pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no `fetch()` calls, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL be present for form submission - **AND** form submission SHALL be handled exclusively by core.js `submitForm()`

scenarios.name: No duplicate stagger CSS in newsletter page
scenarios.then: no `.stagger > *` or `.stagger.visible > *:nth-child()` animation rules SHALL be present

scenarios.name: No duplicate sticky CTA
scenarios.given: the home, pricing, privacy-policy, and terms pages
scenarios.then: no sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

scenarios.name: No duplicate scroll-to-top
scenarios.then: no scroll-to-top scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

scenarios.name: No duplicate reveal or stagger
scenarios.then: no reveal or stagger IntersectionObserver SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

scenarios.name: No duplicate form submission logic
scenarios.then: no `fetch()` calls, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL be present for form submission

requirements.name: Performance Standards
requirements.description: All page-specific scroll listeners SHALL use `{passive: true}`. Mousemove listeners SHALL be throttled via `requestAnimationFrame`. Animations SHALL pause when the page is hidden. - **GIVEN** any page-specific scroll listener - **WHEN** the listener is registered - **THEN** the options object SHALL include `{passive: true}` - **GIVEN** mousemove listeners on home or why-optiflow pages - **WHEN** the mouse moves rapidly - **THEN** the callback SHALL fire at most once per animation frame

scenarios.name: Scroll listeners are passive
scenarios.then: the options object SHALL include `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Mousemove listeners are throttled
scenarios.given: mousemove listeners on home or why-optiflow pages
scenarios.then: the callback SHALL fire at most once per animation frame

requirements.name: Problem & Solutions Narrative Page

scenarios.then: `dist/problem-solutions/index.html` SHALL contain exactly 10 `<section>` elements labeled via `data-screen-label` attributes: 01 Hero, 02 Pain Points, 02B Chaos Map, 03 Trust, 04 Why Growth Feels Hard, 05 WhatsApp & Excel, 06 The Cost of Doing Nothing, 07 The Solution, 08 People vs Process, 09 Before & After, 10 CTA

scenarios.name: Navigation and footer included
scenarios.then: the `<header class="topnav">` navigation SHALL be present with Solutions marked as the active nav link

scenarios.then: `<title>` SHALL match the entry's title value

requirements.name: Pain-Point Rotator
requirements.description: The page SHALL include an auto-cycling carousel of pain-point statements that rotates every 3 seconds and pauses when the page is hidden. - **GIVEN** the pain-points section with 8 `.pp-item` elements - **WHEN** the page loads - **THEN** exactly one `.pp-item` SHALL have class `active` at any time - **AND** the active item SHALL cycle to the next every `3000ms` - **GIVEN** the pain-point rotator interval is running - **WHEN** the browser tab is hidden (`visibilitychange` event fires with `document.hidden === true`) - **THEN** the rotator SHALL skip the current cycle (no class change occurs) - **AND** when the tab becomes visible again, the rotator SHALL resume normally - **GIVEN** the user has `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` - **WHEN** the pain-points section renders - **THEN** all `.pp-item` elements SHALL be visible (opacity 1, no animation) - **AND** only the element with class `active` SHALL be displayed; others SHALL have `display: none`

scenarios.name: Carousel cycles on timer
scenarios.then: exactly one `.pp-item` SHALL have class `active` at any time

scenarios.name: Pauses when page hidden
scenarios.given: the pain-point rotator interval is running
scenarios.when: the browser tab is hidden (`visibilitychange` event fires with `document.hidden === true`)
scenarios.then: the rotator SHALL skip the current cycle (no class change occurs)

scenarios.name: Reduced motion disables carousel
scenarios.then: all `.pp-item` elements SHALL be visible (opacity 1, no animation)

scenarios.name: Chaos resolves to OptiFlow OS
