scenarios.then: the item SHALL expand to show all field key-value pairs

requirements.name: Admin Submissions Pagination
requirements.description: The admin submissions endpoint SHALL support offset-based pagination via query parameters. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions` is called without page params - **THEN** page 1 with 20 items per page SHALL be returned - **AND** the response SHALL include `pagination: { page: 1, perPage: 20, total: N, pages: ceil(N/20) }` - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?per_page=200` is called - **THEN** `per_page` SHALL be capped to 100 (the maximum allowed) - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?formName=contact` is called - **THEN** only submissions with matching formName SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Default page
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions` is called without page params
scenarios.then: page 1 with 20 items per page SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Per-page capped to max
scenarios.then: `per_page` SHALL be capped to 100 (the maximum allowed)

scenarios.name: Form type filter
scenarios.then: only submissions with matching formName SHALL be returned

requirements.name: Admin Data Export
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose an export endpoint at `/api/admin/submissions/export` with CSV and JSON format support, secured by Bearer token. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv` is called with valid Bearer token - **THEN** the response SHALL be `text/csv` with `Content-Disposition: attachment` header - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=json` is called - **THEN** the response SHALL be a JSON array of submission objects - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv&formName=contact` is called - **THEN** only matching submissions SHALL be included - **WHEN** an export is performed - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_export"`

scenarios.name: CSV export
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv` is called with valid Bearer token
scenarios.then: the response SHALL be `text/csv` with `Content-Disposition: attachment` header

scenarios.name: JSON export
scenarios.then: the response SHALL be a JSON array of submission objects

scenarios.name: Filtered export
scenarios.then: only matching submissions SHALL be included

scenarios.name: Audit on export
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_export"`

requirements.name: Subscriber Metrics
requirements.description: The admin stats endpoint SHALL include newsletter subscriber count. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/stats` is called - **THEN** the response SHALL include `stats.subscribers` with count of `subscriber:*` KV keys

scenarios.name: Subscribers in stats
scenarios.then: the response SHALL include `stats.subscribers` with count of `subscriber:*` KV keys

requirements.name: Admin Audit Logging
requirements.description: The system SHALL write audit entries on admin authentication events. - **WHEN** admin login succeeds - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login"` - **WHEN** admin login fails - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login_failed"`

scenarios.name: Successful login audit
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login"`

scenarios.name: Failed login audit
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login_failed"`

requirements.name: Admin Tab Accessibility
requirements.description: The admin dashboard tablist SHALL follow the WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with `aria-controls` attributes. - **WHEN** the admin dashboard renders the tab bar - **THEN** each tab button SHALL have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the corresponding `role="tabpanel"` element - **AND** each `<div role="tabpanel">` SHALL have an `id` matching the `aria-controls` value

scenarios.name: Tab aria-controls
scenarios.when: the admin dashboard renders the tab bar
scenarios.then: each tab button SHALL have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the corresponding `role="tabpanel"` element

requirements.name: Page Assembly
requirements.description: The system SHALL assemble all pages from `src/pages/`, `src/partials/`, and shared assets into the `dist/` directory via `npm run build`. - **GIVEN** a clean project with no `dist/` directory - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** all 12 pages SHALL be generated under `dist/` with clean URLs (directory-based, `/page-name/index.html`) - **AND** the home page SHALL be generated at `dist/index.html` - **GIVEN** an existing `dist/` directory - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** the existing `dist/` SHALL be removed and recreated from scratch

scenarios.name: Full build from clean state
scenarios.given: a clean project with no `dist/` directory
scenarios.then: all 12 pages SHALL be generated under `dist/` with clean URLs (directory-based, `/page-name/index.html`)

scenarios.name: Rebuild overwrites previous output
scenarios.then: the existing `dist/` SHALL be removed and recreated from scratch

requirements.name: SEO Meta Injection
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject canonical URLs, OpenGraph URL/site name/locale/dimensions, Twitter image, and robots meta tags into every assembled page. - **GIVEN** a page with file path `features/index.html` in site.json - **WHEN** the build pipeline assembles the page - **THEN** `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/features/">` SHALL be injected into `<head>` - **GIVEN** any page being assembled - **WHEN** the build pipeline processes the page - **THEN** `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:site_name" content="OptiFlow OS">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_IN">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:image:width" content="512">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:image:height" content="512">` SHALL be injected - **GIVEN** any page being assembled - **WHEN** the build pipeline processes the page - **THEN** `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` SHALL be injected into `<head>` - **GIVEN** any page being assembled - **WHEN** the build pipeline processes the page - **THEN** `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta name="twitter:image:alt" content="OptiFlow OS Logo">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Canonical URL injection
scenarios.given: a page with file path `features/index.html` in site.json
scenarios.when: the build pipeline assembles the page
scenarios.then: `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/features/">` SHALL be injected into `<head>`

scenarios.name: OG URL and site name injection
scenarios.when: the build pipeline processes the page
scenarios.then: `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Robots meta injection
scenarios.when: the build pipeline processes the page
scenarios.then: `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` SHALL be injected into `<head>`

scenarios.name: Twitter image injection
scenarios.when: the build pipeline processes the page
scenarios.then: `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` SHALL be injected

requirements.name: BreadcrumbList Structured Data Injection
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject BreadcrumbList JSON-LD structured data into every assembled page. - **GIVEN** a page at `/problem-solutions/` - **WHEN** the build pipeline injects JSON-LD - **THEN** a BreadcrumbList schema SHALL be present with items for Home and Solutions

scenarios.name: BreadcrumbList generation
scenarios.then: a BreadcrumbList schema SHALL be present with items for Home and Solutions

requirements.name: Article Structured Data on Newsletter
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject Article JSON-LD structured data into the newsletter page. - **GIVEN** the newsletter page - **WHEN** the build pipeline injects JSON-LD - **THEN** an Article schema SHALL be present with headline, description, publisher, and datePublished fields

scenarios.name: Article schema on newsletter
scenarios.then: an Article schema SHALL be present with headline, description, publisher, and datePublished fields

requirements.name: Placeholder Variable Replacement
requirements.description: The system SHALL replace `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` variables in source pages with values from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** a source page contains `{{PHONE}}`, `{{EMAIL}}`, `{{YEAR}}`, `{{SITE_NAME}}` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** each placeholder SHALL be replaced with its corresponding value from `site.json` - **AND** no `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` variables SHALL remain in the output - **GIVEN** a source page contains `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` and `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** these SHALL be replaced with the page's title and description from the `site.json` pages array

scenarios.name: Canonical data injection
scenarios.then: each placeholder SHALL be replaced with its corresponding value from `site.json`

scenarios.name: Page-specific metadata injection
scenarios.then: these SHALL be replaced with the page's title and description from the `site.json` pages array

requirements.name: Partial Injection
requirements.description: The system SHALL inject navigation and footer partials via `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->` and `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->` comments. - **GIVEN** a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/nav.html` - **AND** the active page indicator SHALL be resolved based on the page's `active` field in `site.json` - **AND** active state SHALL be resolved for both desktop nav AND mobile drawer links - **GIVEN** a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/footer.html`

scenarios.name: Navigation injection
scenarios.given: a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->`
scenarios.then: the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/nav.html`

scenarios.name: Footer injection
scenarios.given: a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->`
scenarios.then: the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/footer.html`

requirements.name: Asset Copying
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL copy shared assets (CSS, JS, images) from `assets/` into `dist/assets/`, and SHALL minify CSS and JS during production build. - **GIVEN** the `assets/` directory contains `css/core.css`, `js/core.js`, and image files - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** all files and subdirectories from `assets/` SHALL be recursively copied to `dist/assets/` - **AND** each page SHALL reference assets via `/assets/css/core.css` and `/assets/js/core.js` - **GIVEN** a design specification directory exists in the project root - **WHEN** the build runs - **THEN** PNG logo files SHALL be copied into `dist/assets/img/` - **WHEN** assets are copied in production build mode - **THEN** `core.css` and `core.js` SHALL be minified before writing to `dist/assets/` - **AND** source files in `assets/` SHALL remain unminified

scenarios.name: Shared assets copy
scenarios.given: the `assets/` directory contains `css/core.css`, `js/core.js`, and image files
scenarios.then: all files and subdirectories from `assets/` SHALL be recursively copied to `dist/assets/`

scenarios.name: Logo image detection
scenarios.given: a design specification directory exists in the project root
scenarios.then: PNG logo files SHALL be copied into `dist/assets/img/`

scenarios.name: CSS and JS minification
scenarios.when: assets are copied in production build mode
scenarios.then: `core.css` and `core.js` SHALL be minified before writing to `dist/assets/`

requirements.name: Validation
requirements.description: The system SHALL validate all generated pages via `npm run validate`, checking internal links, hardcoded hex colors, dark mode coverage, SEO tags, and data consistency. - **GIVEN** a generated page contains internal links (`href="/path/"`) - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** each internal link SHALL be verified as resolvable against the `dist/` directory - **AND** broken links SHALL be reported as errors - **GIVEN** a generated page contains inline hex colors outside `<style>` blocks and SVG `fill` attributes - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** each hardcoded hex color SHALL be reported as a warning - **GIVEN** a generated page does NOT contain `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** a warning SHALL be reported for that page - **GIVEN** a generated page - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** the system SHALL verify `<title>`, meta description, `<h1>`, and OG tags exist - **AND** missing tags SHALL be reported as errors or warnings - **GIVEN** a generated page - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** the system SHALL check for stale phone numbers and locations - **AND** inconsistencies SHALL be reported as errors

scenarios.name: Internal link validation
scenarios.given: a generated page contains internal links (`href="/path/"`)
scenarios.then: each internal link SHALL be verified as resolvable against the `dist/` directory

scenarios.name: Hardcoded color detection
scenarios.given: a generated page contains inline hex colors outside `<style>` blocks and SVG `fill` attributes
scenarios.then: each hardcoded hex color SHALL be reported as a warning

scenarios.name: Dark mode coverage check
scenarios.given: a generated page does NOT contain `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors
scenarios.then: a warning SHALL be reported for that page

scenarios.name: SEO tag presence check
scenarios.then: the system SHALL verify `<title>`, meta description, `<h1>`, and OG tags exist

scenarios.name: Data consistency check
scenarios.then: the system SHALL check for stale phone numbers and locations

requirements.name: Validation Exit Code
requirements.description: The system SHALL exit with a non-zero code when validation errors are present, and the post-build hook SHALL propagate this exit code. - **GIVEN** validation finds one or more errors - **WHEN** `npm run validate` completes - **THEN** the process SHALL exit with code 1 - **GIVEN** one or more E2E tests fail - **WHEN** `npm test` completes - **THEN** the process SHALL exit with code 1 - **GIVEN** validation finds only warnings and no errors - **WHEN** `npm run validate` completes - **THEN** the process SHALL exit with code 0 - **GIVEN** validation exits with code 1 (errors found) - **WHEN** the post-build hook runs - **THEN** the build SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Errors cause failure
scenarios.given: validation finds one or more errors
scenarios.then: the process SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Test failures cause CI failure
scenarios.given: one or more E2E tests fail
scenarios.then: the process SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Warnings-only success
scenarios.given: validation finds only warnings and no errors
scenarios.then: the process SHALL exit with code 0

scenarios.name: Post-build propagates validate exit code
scenarios.then: the build SHALL exit with code 1

requirements.name: Clean URLs
requirements.description: The system SHALL produce clean, directory-based URLs without `.html` extensions. - **GIVEN** a page mapped to `features/index.html` in the page map - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the output SHALL be written to `dist/features/index.html` - **AND** the URL path SHALL be `/features/`

scenarios.name: Directory-based output
scenarios.given: a page mapped to `features/index.html` in the page map
scenarios.then: the output SHALL be written to `dist/features/index.html`

requirements.name: CI/CD Pipeline
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on pull requests and pushes to main. - **GIVEN** a pull request is opened against `main` - **WHEN** the CI workflow triggers - **THEN** the build SHALL run via `npm run build` - **AND** validation SHALL run via `npm run validate` - **AND** lint checks SHALL run via `npm run lint:all` - **AND** E2E tests SHALL run via `npm test` - **AND** all steps SHALL complete within 10 minutes - **GIVEN** the project is configured for deployment - **WHEN** `npm run deploy:netlify` is executed - **THEN** the site SHALL be deployed to Netlify - **WHEN** `npm run deploy:cloudflare` is executed - **THEN** the site SHALL be deployed to Cloudflare Pages

scenarios.name: PR validation
scenarios.given: a pull request is opened against `main`
scenarios.then: the build SHALL run via `npm run build`

scenarios.name: Deploy scripts
scenarios.given: the project is configured for deployment
scenarios.when: `npm run deploy:netlify` is executed `npm run deploy:cloudflare` is executed
scenarios.then: the site SHALL be deployed to Netlify the site SHALL be deployed to Cloudflare Pages

requirements.name: Pipeline Configuration
requirements.description: The system SHALL have a deploy step in the build pipeline config that invokes the actual deploy script. - **GIVEN** the build pipeline config at `orchestrate/pipeline-config/build.yaml` - **WHEN** the deploy step is executed - **THEN** it SHALL run `npm run deploy` - **AND** it SHALL NOT be a placeholder echo command

scenarios.name: Pipeline deploy step
scenarios.then: it SHALL run `npm run deploy`

requirements.name: Development Server
requirements.description: The system SHALL support `npm run dev` for local development (build + serve). - **GIVEN** the project source files - **WHEN** `npm run dev` is executed - **THEN** the build SHALL run first - **AND** a local HTTP server SHALL serve the `dist/` directory

scenarios.name: Dev workflow
scenarios.then: the build SHALL run first

requirements.name: Test Command
requirements.description: The system SHALL include an `npm test` script that runs the full Playwright E2E test suite. - **GIVEN** the project has test files in `tests/e2e/` - **WHEN** `npm test` is executed - **THEN** Playwright SHALL run all test specs across configured browser projects - **AND** the dev server SHALL be started automatically via `webServer` config

scenarios.name: Test command execution
scenarios.given: the project has test files in `tests/e2e/`
scenarios.then: Playwright SHALL run all test specs across configured browser projects

requirements.name: Post-Merge Hook
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a post-merge hook that runs after `git merge` or `git pull` to detect dependency drift and warn of rebuild-requiring changes. - **WHEN** a merge changes `package-lock.json` - **THEN** `npm install` SHALL run automatically via the post-merge hook - **WHEN** a merge changes `site.json` or `assets/css/core.css` - **THEN** the hook SHALL warn the user to run `npm run build`

scenarios.name: Package lockfile change triggers install
scenarios.then: `npm install` SHALL run automatically via the post-merge hook

scenarios.name: Site data change warns about rebuild
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL warn the user to run `npm run build`

requirements.name: Pre-Push Hook
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a pre-push hook that runs lint and validate checks before allowing a `git push`. - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and linting returns errors - **THEN** the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1 - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and validation returns errors - **THEN** the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1 - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and both lint and validate pass - **THEN** the push SHALL proceed with exit code 0

scenarios.name: Push blocked on lint failure
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and linting returns errors
scenarios.then: the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1

scenarios.name: Push blocked on validate failure
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and validation returns errors
scenarios.then: the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1

scenarios.name: Clean push proceeds
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and both lint and validate pass
scenarios.then: the push SHALL proceed with exit code 0

requirements.name: Preconnect Resource Hints Injection
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject `<link rel="preconnect">` hints into every assembled page's `<head>` for Google Fonts and Plausible analytics. - **WHEN** a page is assembled - **THEN** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be present in `<head>` - **AND** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>` SHALL be present - **AND** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://plausible.io">` SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Preconnect hints present
scenarios.then: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be present in `<head>`

requirements.name: GitHub Actions CI Workflow
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a GitHub Actions workflow at `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that runs on every pull request to `main`. - **WHEN** a pull request is opened or updated against `main` - **THEN** the CI workflow SHALL execute in order: `npm run build`, `npm run validate`, `npm run lint:all`, `npm test`, `npm audit --production` - **AND** all steps SHALL complete within 10 minutes - **WHEN** any CI step fails - **THEN** the workflow SHALL exit with a non-zero status - **AND** the PR SHALL be marked as failing required checks

scenarios.name: PR triggers CI pipeline
scenarios.when: a pull request is opened or updated against `main`
scenarios.then: the CI workflow SHALL execute in order: `npm run build`, `npm run validate`, `npm run lint:all`, `npm test`, `npm audit --production`

scenarios.name: Failed step blocks merge
scenarios.then: the workflow SHALL exit with a non-zero status

requirements.name: Dependency Vulnerability Scanning
requirements.description: The system SHALL scan dependencies for known vulnerabilities via `npm audit --production` as a CI step. - **WHEN** `npm audit --production` finds a vulnerability with severity HIGH or CRITICAL - **THEN** the audit step SHALL fail - **AND** the vulnerability details SHALL be displayed in CI output - **WHEN** `npm audit --production` finds only LOW or MODERATE severity or no vulnerabilities - **THEN** the audit step SHALL pass

scenarios.name: Vulnerabilities detected
scenarios.when: `npm audit --production` finds a vulnerability with severity HIGH or CRITICAL
scenarios.then: the audit step SHALL fail

scenarios.name: No vulnerabilities
scenarios.when: `npm audit --production` finds only LOW or MODERATE severity or no vulnerabilities
scenarios.then: the audit step SHALL pass

requirements.name: ESLint Scope Coverage
requirements.description: ESLint SHALL cover all JavaScript files including `functions/` Worker scripts. - **WHEN** `npm run lint:js` is executed - **THEN** all `.js` files in `functions/` SHALL be included in ESLint's scope - **AND** any lint violations SHALL be reported

scenarios.name: functions/ files are linted
scenarios.then: all `.js` files in `functions/` SHALL be included in ESLint's scope

requirements.name: Pre-Commit Hook Automation
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a git pre-commit hook for automated quality checks before commit. The pre-commit hook SHALL validate: 1. No debug statements in source files 2. No hardcoded secrets 3. Contact data consistency (phone placeholders, location correctness) 4. No merge conflict markers 5. Commit message follows Conventional Commits format (`feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore|perf|ci|style`) 6. Branch name matches allowed patterns (`main|staging|develop|feature/*|fix/*|chore/*|docs/*`) - **WHEN** `git commit` is executed - **THEN** `npm run precommit` SHALL run automatically via husky - **AND** the commit SHALL be blocked if precommit checks fail - **WHEN** `git commit -m "updated stuff"` is executed - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with a message listing allowed commit types - **WHEN** a commit is made on an unlisted branch name - **THEN** the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with a message listing allowed branch patterns - **WHEN** `git commit --amend` is executed - **THEN** the commit message format check SHALL be skipped - **AND** all other checks SHALL still run

scenarios.name: Pre-commit check runs automatically
scenarios.then: `npm run precommit` SHALL run automatically via husky

scenarios.name: Commit message format validated
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with a message listing allowed commit types

scenarios.name: Branch name validated
scenarios.when: a commit is made on an unlisted branch name
scenarios.then: the pre-commit hook SHALL fail with a message listing allowed branch patterns

scenarios.name: Amend commits skip message check
scenarios.then: the commit message format check SHALL be skipped

requirements.name: Pipeline Configs for Orchestration
requirements.description: The system SHALL include `security.yaml` and `audit.yaml` pipeline configs for the orchestration engine. - **WHEN** the orchestration engine references `security.yaml` - **THEN** a valid pipeline config SHALL exist at `orchestrate/pipeline-configs/security.yaml` - **AND** it SHALL include GATE_SECURITY step - **WHEN** the orchestration engine references `audit.yaml` - **THEN** a valid pipeline config SHALL exist at `orchestrate/pipeline-configs/audit.yaml` - **AND** it SHALL include a11y, SEO, and design compliance steps

scenarios.name: Security pipeline config exists
scenarios.then: a valid pipeline config SHALL exist at `orchestrate/pipeline-configs/security.yaml`

scenarios.name: Audit pipeline config exists
scenarios.then: a valid pipeline config SHALL exist at `orchestrate/pipeline-configs/audit.yaml`

requirements.name: Pre-Existing HTML Validation Fixes
requirements.description: The system SHALL resolve all pre-existing HTML validation errors in contact and product-overview pages. - **WHEN** `npx html-validate "dist/contact/index.html"` is executed - **THEN** zero errors SHALL be reported - **WHEN** `npx html-validate "dist/product-overview/index.html"` is executed - **THEN** zero errors SHALL be reported

scenarios.name: No html-validate errors in contact page
scenarios.then: zero errors SHALL be reported

scenarios.name: No html-validate errors in product-overview page
scenarios.then: zero errors SHALL be reported

requirements.name: Page Structure
requirements.description: The system SHALL render a Competitive Positioning page with 6 sections: Hero, Competitor Quadrant Grid, Feature Comparison Matrix, Cost Comparison, Trust/Why OptiFlow Stands Out, and CTA. - **WHEN** the page is assembled and rendered - **THEN** the `<main>` tag SHALL contain exactly 6 `<section>` elements in order: Hero, Quadrant Grid, Feature Matrix, Cost Comparison, Why Stands Out, CTA - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `<header class="topnav">` navigation SHALL be present with Resources marked as active nav link - **AND** the `<footer class="pagefoot">` SHALL be present

scenarios.name: All 6 sections render
scenarios.when: the page is assembled and rendered
scenarios.then: the `<main>` tag SHALL contain exactly 6 `<section>` elements in order: Hero, Quadrant Grid, Feature Matrix, Cost Comparison, Why Stands Out, CTA

scenarios.name: Navigation and footer included
scenarios.then: the `<header class="topnav">` navigation SHALL be present with Resources marked as active nav link

requirements.name: Competitor Quadrant Grid
requirements.description: The page SHALL display a 2x2 quadrant grid comparing OptiFlow OS against 4 competitor categories. - **WHEN** the quadrant section renders - **THEN** a `.quadrant-grid` SHALL contain exactly 4 `.quadrant-card` elements - **AND** cards SHALL represent: WhatsApp & Excel Chaos, Generic SaaS Tools, Traditional ERPs, Manual Paper Systems - **AND** each card SHALL include: category name, 3 key weaknesses, and an "OptiFlow advantage" line - **WHEN** the quadrant grid renders - **THEN** a center `.quadrant-center` element SHALL display "OptiFlow OS" with "All 4 solved in one platform" subtitle - **AND** the center node SHALL use accent gradient styling - **WHEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` is active - **THEN** quadrant cards SHALL use `var(--surface)` background with `var(--border)` border - **AND** advantage text SHALL use `var(--teal)` color

scenarios.name: 4 quadrants displayed
scenarios.then: a `.quadrant-grid` SHALL contain exactly 4 `.quadrant-card` elements

scenarios.name: OptiFlow OS center node
scenarios.then: a center `.quadrant-center` element SHALL display "OptiFlow OS" with "All 4 solved in one platform" subtitle

scenarios.name: Dark mode quadrant cards
scenarios.then: quadrant cards SHALL use `var(--surface)` background with `var(--border)` border

requirements.name: Feature Comparison Matrix
requirements.description: The page SHALL include a responsive feature comparison table with OptiFlow OS and 4 competitor categories as columns. - **WHEN** the feature matrix section renders - **THEN** a `.comparison-table` SHALL have 5 columns: Feature, OptiFlow OS, WhatsApp/Excel, Generic SaaS, Traditional ERP - **AND** the table SHALL include at least 10 feature rows covering: task management, attendance, SOPs, real-time dashboards, mobile access, checklists, reports, multi-location, role-based access, and pricing model - **AND** OptiFlow OS column SHALL use check icons (`.check`) and competitor columns SHALL use cross icons (`.cross`) as appropriate - **WHEN** viewport width is less than 768px - **THEN** the comparison table SHALL be horizontally scrollable via `overflow-x: auto` - **AND** the first column (Feature) SHALL remain sticky-left

scenarios.name: Feature matrix structure
scenarios.then: a `.comparison-table` SHALL have 5 columns: Feature, OptiFlow OS, WhatsApp/Excel, Generic SaaS, Traditional ERP

scenarios.name: Responsive scroll behavior
scenarios.then: the comparison table SHALL be horizontally scrollable via `overflow-x: auto`

requirements.name: Cost Comparison Section
requirements.description: The page SHALL display a cost comparison contrasting OptiFlow's flat annual pricing against per-user pricing models. - **WHEN** the cost section renders - **THEN** the section SHALL display 4 `.cost-card` elements comparing: OptiFlow OS (flat annual), WhatsApp/Excel (hidden costs), Generic SaaS (per-user/per-month), and Traditional ERP (implementation + license) - **AND** each card SHALL show an annual cost estimate range in ₹ - **AND** OptiFlow OS card SHALL use `.card` with green border accent - **AND** competitor cards SHALL use muted styling - **WHEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` is active - **THEN** cost cards SHALL use appropriate dark background and maintain readable ₹ figures

scenarios.name: Cost comparison cards
scenarios.then: the section SHALL display 4 `.cost-card` elements comparing: OptiFlow OS (flat annual), WhatsApp/Excel (hidden costs), Generic SaaS (per-user/per-month), and Traditional ERP (implementation + license)

scenarios.name: Dark mode cost cards
scenarios.then: cost cards SHALL use appropriate dark background and maintain readable ₹ figures

requirements.name: Why OptiFlow Stands Out Section
requirements.description: The page SHALL include a trust-building section highlighting OptiFlow's unique advantages over competitors. - **WHEN** the stand-out section renders - **THEN** a `.grid-3` SHALL contain 3 `.card` elements with: "Built for Indian MSMEs", "Flat Pricing — No Per-User Tax", and "Implementation + Support Included" - **AND** each card SHALL include an icon, heading, and description paragraph

scenarios.name: Stand-out cards render
scenarios.then: a `.grid-3` SHALL contain 3 `.card` elements with: "Built for Indian MSMEs", "Flat Pricing — No Per-User Tax", and "Implementation + Support Included"

requirements.name: SEO Metadata
requirements.description: The page SHALL use build-time placeholders for SEO metadata resolved from `site.json`. - **WHEN** the page source file is inspected - **THEN** the `<title>` tag SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` - **AND** `<meta name="description">` SHALL contain `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` - **AND** no hardcoded title or description SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Title and description placeholders
scenarios.when: the page source file is inspected
scenarios.then: the `<title>` tag SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}`

requirements.name: Dark Mode Support
requirements.description: All page-specific components SHALL have complete `[data-theme="dark"]` style overrides. - **WHEN** the page's `<style>` block is inspected - **THEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.quadrant-card`, `.quadrant-center`, `.cost-card`, `.cost-card.best`, `.comparison-table th`, `.comparison-table td`, `.standout-card` icon backgrounds - **AND** all dark mode color values SHALL use `var()`, `color-mix()`, or CSS custom properties - **AND** no raw hex values SHALL appear in dark mode rules

scenarios.name: Dark mode overrides exist
scenarios.then: `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.quadrant-card`, `.quadrant-center`, `.cost-card`, `.cost-card.best`, `.comparison-table th`, `.comparison-table td`, `.standout-card` icon backgrounds

requirements.name: Cross-Links from Existing Pages
requirements.description: The Why OptiFlow and Pricing pages SHALL include cross-links to the Competitive Positioning page. - **WHEN** the Why OptiFlow page renders - **THEN** the CTA section SHALL include a secondary link "Compare OptiFlow vs alternatives" pointing to `/competitive-positioning/` - **WHEN** the Pricing page renders - **THEN** the cost comparison section SHALL include a link "See full competitive comparison" pointing to `/competitive-positioning/`

scenarios.name: Why OptiFlow CTA links
scenarios.then: the CTA section SHALL include a secondary link "Compare OptiFlow vs alternatives" pointing to `/competitive-positioning/`

scenarios.name: Pricing page cross-link
scenarios.then: the cost comparison section SHALL include a link "See full competitive comparison" pointing to `/competitive-positioning/`

requirements.name: Performance Standards
requirements.description: Page-specific scroll listeners SHALL use `{passive: true}`. No mousemove listeners SHALL be used. Animations SHALL pause when the page is hidden. - **WHEN** any page-specific scroll listener is registered - **THEN** the options object SHALL include `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Scroll listeners are passive
scenarios.when: any page-specific scroll listener is registered
scenarios.then: the options object SHALL include `{passive: true}`

requirements.name: Enquiry Form
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL include a lead capture form with 7 fields using the shared `submitForm()` function from core.js for Netlify Forms submission. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<form id="contactForm">` SHALL be present with 7 fields: Full Name (required), Company Name (required), Mobile Number (required), Email Address (required), Team Size (select), Industry (select), Current Operational Challenges (textarea) - **AND** required fields SHALL have `required` attribute and `aria-invalid` support - **AND** the form SHALL have `data-netlify="true"` and `name="contact"` attributes - **GIVEN** the contact form is submitted - **WHEN** the form passes client-side validation - **THEN** the form SHALL be handled by `core.js` `submitForm()` function - **AND** no custom `fetch()`, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handler SHALL exist in page scripts - **GIVEN** the contact form with empty required fields - **WHEN** the form is submitted - **THEN** error messages SHALL display below each invalid field with class `visible` - **AND** invalid inputs SHALL have class `error` and `aria-invalid="true"` - **AND** email field SHALL validate format via regex `/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/` - **AND** phone field SHALL validate format via regex `/^\+?\d{7,15}$/` - **GIVEN** the contact form submits successfully - **WHEN** the form enters success state - **THEN** form fields SHALL be hidden - **AND** a success message with checkmark icon SHALL display - **AND** a "Expect a response within 24 hours" badge SHALL appear with animated pulse dot

scenarios.name: Form fields render
scenarios.then: a `<form id="contactForm">` SHALL be present with 7 fields: Full Name (required), Company Name (required), Mobile Number (required), Email Address (required), Team Size (select), Industry (select), Current Operational Challenges (textarea)

scenarios.name: Form uses shared submission
scenarios.then: the form SHALL be handled by `core.js` `submitForm()` function

scenarios.name: Validation errors display
scenarios.given: the contact form with empty required fields
scenarios.then: error messages SHALL display below each invalid field with class `visible`

scenarios.name: Success state displays
scenarios.then: form fields SHALL be hidden

requirements.name: Sales Contact Channels
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL display 3 sales contact channels with icons, descriptions, and action links. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page, Sales Contact section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 3 `.contact-chan` cards SHALL display: Call Us (with `{{PHONE}}`), Email Us (with `{{EMAIL}}`), WhatsApp (with `{{PHONE}}`) - **AND** each channel SHALL have an accent SVG icon, heading, detail text with hours, and action link text with arrow - **AND** phone channels SHALL show "Mon — Sat, 10 AM — 7 PM IST" - **AND** email channel SHALL show "Response within 4—8 business hours"

scenarios.name: Sales channels render
scenarios.given: the assembled contact page, Sales Contact section
scenarios.then: 3 `.contact-chan` cards SHALL display: Call Us (with `{{PHONE}}`), Email Us (with `{{EMAIL}}`), WhatsApp (with `{{PHONE}}`)

requirements.name: Support Contact Channels
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL display 3 support contact channels with teal icons. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page, Support section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 3 `.contact-chan` cards SHALL display: Support Email, Helpdesk Portal, Knowledge Base - **AND** each channel SHALL have a teal icon (`--teal-soft` background, `--teal` color) - **AND** Support Email SHALL show "support@optiflow.in" with 4—8 hour response time - **AND** Helpdesk Portal SHALL reference dashboard access - **AND** Knowledge Base SHALL reference self-serve guides and tutorials

scenarios.name: Support channels render
scenarios.given: the assembled contact page, Support section
scenarios.then: 3 `.contact-chan` cards SHALL display: Support Email, Helpdesk Portal, Knowledge Base

requirements.name: Office Information
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL display office location, contact details, and operating hours. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page, Office section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** an `.office-card` SHALL display: location ("Surat, Gujarat, India"), phone (`{{PHONE}}`), email (`{{EMAIL}}`), hours ("Mon — Sat, 10 AM — 7 PM IST") - **AND** the location SHALL use `{{LOCATION}}` placeholder - **AND** an "Open for business" badge SHALL be displayed with green styling

scenarios.name: Office card renders
scenarios.given: the assembled contact page, Office section
scenarios.then: an `.office-card` SHALL display: location ("Surat, Gujarat, India"), phone (`{{PHONE}}`), email (`{{EMAIL}}`), hours ("Mon — Sat, 10 AM — 7 PM IST")

requirements.name: Response Time Commitments
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL display 4 response time commitment cards with color-coded icons. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page, Response Time section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.response-grid` SHALL display 4 `.response-card` items: Sales Enquiries (accent icon), Support Requests (teal icon), Critical Issues (red icon), Demo Requests (green icon) - **AND** each card SHALL display a `.response-time` value in monospace font

scenarios.name: Response cards render
scenarios.given: the assembled contact page, Response Time section
scenarios.then: a `.response-grid` SHALL display 4 `.response-card` items: Sales Enquiries (accent icon), Support Requests (teal icon), Critical Issues (red icon), Demo Requests (green icon)

requirements.name: Trust Bar with Counters
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL display a trust bar with 4 animated count-up statistics. - **GIVEN** the trust bar section - **WHEN** the section enters the viewport - **THEN** counters SHALL animate from 0 to their target values: 500, 10000, 250000, 1200 - **AND** counters SHALL use `data-count` attributes processed by core.js

scenarios.name: Counter animation
scenarios.then: counters SHALL animate from 0 to their target values: 500, 10000, 250000, 1200

requirements.name: Contact-Specific FAQ
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL include a FAQ accordion with 7 questions relevant to the contact and support context. - **GIVEN** the FAQ section of the contact page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 7 `.faq-item` elements SHALL be present using the shared FAQ accordion pattern - **AND** questions SHALL cover: response time, demo scheduling, industry support, implementation services, team participation, post-submission process, post-go-live support

scenarios.name: FAQ questions render
scenarios.given: the FAQ section of the contact page
scenarios.then: 7 `.faq-item` elements SHALL be present using the shared FAQ accordion pattern

requirements.name: Hero Section with Illustration
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL feature a hero section with a badge, heading, lead text, dual CTAs, and a visual illustration with stats. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a hero section SHALL display a "LET'S TALK ABOUT YOUR BUSINESS" badge, an H1, lead paragraph, and two CTA buttons: "Contact Our Team" (primary) and "Book Free Demo" (secondary) - **AND** a `.hero-illus` SHALL display a chat SVG illustration with floating animation - **AND** 3 `.hil-stat` items SHALL display: "4hrs Avg Response Time", "500+ Businesses Served", "7 Industries"

scenarios.name: Hero renders
scenarios.then: a hero section SHALL display a "LET'S TALK ABOUT YOUR BUSINESS" badge, an H1, lead paragraph, and two CTA buttons: "Contact Our Team" (primary) and "Book Free Demo" (secondary)

requirements.name: Dual CTA Section
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL include a final CTA section with dual action buttons at the bottom of the page. - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.cta-section` SHALL display with heading "Let's Build A More Accountable And Scalable Business Together" - **AND** two buttons SHALL be present: "Contact Our Team" (primary, links to #contact-form) and "Book Free Demo" (secondary, links to /demo-booking/)

scenarios.name: Final CTA renders
scenarios.then: a `.cta-section` SHALL display with heading "Let's Build A More Accountable And Scalable Business Together"

requirements.name: Placeholder Compliance
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL use `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` variables for all company data resolved from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** the contact page source file - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `{{PHONE}}`, `{{EMAIL}}`, `{{LOCATION}}` placeholders SHALL be used for all company contact information - **AND** no hardcoded phone numbers, email addresses, or locations SHALL be present except `support@optiflow.in` (distinct from main email)

scenarios.name: No hardcoded company data
scenarios.then: `{{PHONE}}`, `{{EMAIL}}`, `{{LOCATION}}` placeholders SHALL be used for all company contact information

requirements.name: Dark Mode Support
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL render correctly in both light and dark themes. - **GIVEN** the page's `<style>` block - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.contact-chan`, `.response-card`, `.office-card`, `.form-input:focus` - **AND** all dark mode color values SHALL use `color-mix()` or `var()` functions

scenarios.name: Dark mode overrides
scenarios.then: `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.contact-chan`, `.response-card`, `.office-card`, `.form-input:focus`

requirements.name: Responsive Layout
requirements.description: The Contact page SHALL adapt to tablet and mobile viewports. - **GIVEN** viewport ≤ 1024px - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** hero grid SHALL collapse to single column - **AND** response grid SHALL display 2 columns - **GIVEN** viewport ≤ 768px - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** form grid SHALL collapse to single column - **AND** hero CTA buttons SHALL stack vertically - **AND** hero illustration stats SHALL stack vertically - **GIVEN** viewport ≤ 480px - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** trust metrics SHALL display 2 columns - **AND** response grid SHALL collapse to single column

scenarios.name: Tablet layout
scenarios.then: hero grid SHALL collapse to single column

scenarios.name: Mobile layout
scenarios.then: form grid SHALL collapse to single column

scenarios.name: Small mobile
scenarios.then: trust metrics SHALL display 2 columns

requirements.name: Theme Attribute
requirements.description: The system SHALL support light and dark themes via the `data-theme` attribute on the `<html>` element. - **GIVEN** a first-time visitor with no saved preference and a light system theme - **WHEN** the page loads - **THEN** `<html>` SHALL have `data-theme="light"` (or no explicit theme) - **AND** the `:root` CSS custom properties SHALL apply - **GIVEN** `<html data-theme="dark">` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** all `[data-theme="dark"]` CSS overrides SHALL apply - **AND** background SHALL switch to deep navy (`oklch(9% 0.02 250)`) - **AND** text SHALL switch to near-white (`oklch(98% 0.002 240)`) - **AND** accent colors SHALL brighten for visibility against dark backgrounds

scenarios.name: Light theme (default)
scenarios.given: a first-time visitor with no saved preference and a light system theme
scenarios.then: `<html>` SHALL have `data-theme="light"` (or no explicit theme)

scenarios.name: Dark theme activation
scenarios.then: all `[data-theme="dark"]` CSS overrides SHALL apply

requirements.name: Preference Persistence
requirements.description: The system SHALL persist theme preference to localStorage. - **GIVEN** a user toggles to dark mode - **WHEN** the user navigates to another page or returns later - **THEN** the theme SHALL remain dark - **AND** the preference SHALL be stored under `localStorage` key `optiflow-theme`

scenarios.name: Theme remembered
scenarios.given: a user toggles to dark mode
scenarios.when: the user navigates to another page or returns later
scenarios.then: the theme SHALL remain dark

requirements.name: System Preference Detection
requirements.description: The system SHALL detect system preference on first visit via `prefers-color-scheme`. - **GIVEN** a first-time visitor with no `localStorage` entry - **WHEN** the page loads and `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` matches - **THEN** the theme SHALL be set to "dark" - **GIVEN** a first-time visitor with no `localStorage` entry - **WHEN** the page loads and the system preference is light - **THEN** the theme SHALL remain at the default (light)

scenarios.name: First visit with dark system preference
scenarios.given: a first-time visitor with no `localStorage` entry
scenarios.when: the page loads and `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` matches
scenarios.then: the theme SHALL be set to "dark"

scenarios.name: First visit with light system preference
scenarios.given: a first-time visitor with no `localStorage` entry
scenarios.when: the page loads and the system preference is light
scenarios.then: the theme SHALL remain at the default (light)

requirements.name: Smooth Transition
requirements.description: The system SHALL smoothly transition between themes with a CSS transition. - **GIVEN** the user toggles between light and dark themes - **WHEN** the `data-theme` attribute changes - **THEN** the `body` SHALL transition `background` and `color` over 0.35s - **AND** the navigation SHALL transition `background`, `border-color`, and `box-shadow` over 0.35s with a cubic-bezier easing

scenarios.name: Theme change animation
scenarios.given: the user toggles between light and dark themes
scenarios.then: the `body` SHALL transition `background` and `color` over 0.35s

requirements.name: Theme Toggle in Navigation
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a theme toggle button in the navigation bar. - **GIVEN** any page with the shared navigation - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<button class="theme-toggle">` SHALL be present in the nav CTA area - **AND** clicking it SHALL toggle the theme

scenarios.name: Nav toggle present
scenarios.given: any page with the shared navigation
scenarios.then: a `<button class="theme-toggle">` SHALL be present in the nav CTA area

requirements.name: Theme Toggle in Footer
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a theme toggle button in the footer. - **GIVEN** any page with the shared footer - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<button class="theme-toggle">` SHALL be present in the footer bottom bar - **AND** it SHALL be styled to match the dark footer background

scenarios.name: Footer toggle present
scenarios.given: any page with the shared footer
scenarios.then: a `<button class="theme-toggle">` SHALL be present in the footer bottom bar

requirements.name: Component Dark Mode Rendering
requirements.description: The system SHALL ensure all shared components render correctly in dark mode. - **GIVEN** the dark theme is active - **WHEN** `.card` elements render - **THEN** they SHALL use dark-mode shadow tokens with a subtle white edge highlight - **AND** hover state SHALL add a teal glow - **GIVEN** the dark theme is active - **WHEN** `.glass-card` elements render - **THEN** they SHALL use a semi-transparent white background (`rgba(255,255,255,.06)`) - **AND** border SHALL be semi-transparent (`rgba(255,255,255,.08)`) - **GIVEN** the dark theme is active - **WHEN** the navigation scrolls - **THEN** `.topnav.scrolled` SHALL use a dark glass background (`rgba(15,23,42,.85)`) - **AND** dropdown menus SHALL use a dark background (`rgba(15,23,42,.94)`) - **GIVEN** the dark theme is active - **WHEN** FAQ items render - **THEN** question hover color SHALL remain teal - **AND** open state icon color SHALL be teal

scenarios.name: Cards in dark mode
scenarios.then: they SHALL use dark-mode shadow tokens with a subtle white edge highlight

scenarios.name: Glass cards in dark mode
scenarios.then: they SHALL use a semi-transparent white background (`rgba(255,255,255,.06)`)

scenarios.name: Navigation in dark mode
scenarios.then: `.topnav.scrolled` SHALL use a dark glass background (`rgba(15,23,42,.85)`)

scenarios.name: FAQ in dark mode
scenarios.then: question hover color SHALL remain teal

requirements.name: Page-Specific Dark Mode Overrides
requirements.description: The system SHALL ensure all page-specific styles override correctly in dark mode. - **GIVEN** a page has custom `<style>` block rules using `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors - **WHEN** the dark theme is active - **THEN** those page-specific overrides SHALL apply correctly - **AND** validation SHALL warn if a page has no dark mode styles

scenarios.name: Page style dark mode
scenarios.given: a page has custom `<style>` block rules using `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors
scenarios.then: those page-specific overrides SHALL apply correctly

requirements.name: KV Key Naming Convention
requirements.description: The system SHALL use structured, date-prefixed key names for all KV records to enable efficient listing and TTL-based lifecycle management. - **WHEN** a valid form submission is stored in KV - **THEN** the key SHALL be `sub:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}` - **AND** the UUID SHALL be a v4 UUID generated by the worker - **WHEN** an email notification log is stored in KV - **THEN** the key SHALL be `notif:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}` - **WHEN** an audit log entry is stored in KV - **THEN** the key SHALL be `audit:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}` - **WHEN** a newsletter subscriber is stored in KV - **THEN** the key SHALL be `subscriber:{sha256(email)}` - **AND** this SHALL provide natural deduplication (same email = same key)

scenarios.name: Submission key format
scenarios.when: a valid form submission is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the key SHALL be `sub:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}`

scenarios.name: Notification log key format
scenarios.when: an email notification log is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the key SHALL be `notif:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}`

scenarios.name: Audit entry key format
scenarios.when: an audit log entry is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the key SHALL be `audit:{YYYY-MM-DD}:{uuid}`

scenarios.name: Newsletter subscriber key format
scenarios.when: a newsletter subscriber is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the key SHALL be `subscriber:{sha256(email)}`

requirements.name: KV Value Schema Versioning
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a `schema` field in every KV value to enable forward-compatible deserialization and migration. - **WHEN** a submission is stored in KV - **THEN** the value SHALL include `"schema": "submission.v1"` - **WHEN** a notification log is stored in KV - **THEN** the value SHALL include `"schema": "notification.v1"` - **WHEN** an audit entry is stored in KV - **THEN** the value SHALL include `"schema": "audit.v1"` - **WHEN** a KV value is read and its schema does not match the expected version - **THEN** the worker SHALL log a warning with the key and actual schema - **AND** the record SHALL be skipped (not returned to the caller)

scenarios.name: Submission value schema
scenarios.when: a submission is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the value SHALL include `"schema": "submission.v1"`

scenarios.name: Notification log value schema
scenarios.when: a notification log is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the value SHALL include `"schema": "notification.v1"`

scenarios.name: Audit entry value schema
scenarios.when: an audit entry is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the value SHALL include `"schema": "audit.v1"`

scenarios.name: Schema mismatch on read
scenarios.when: a KV value is read and its schema does not match the expected version
scenarios.then: the worker SHALL log a warning with the key and actual schema

requirements.name: Submission Value Schema
requirements.description: The system SHALL enforce a consistent JSON schema for submission KV values. - **WHEN** a submission is stored in KV - **THEN** the value SHALL be a JSON object with keys: `schema`, `id`, `timestamp`, `formName`, `fields`, `utm`, `ip`, `emailSent`, `spam` - **AND** `schema` SHALL be `"submission.v1"` - **AND** `id` SHALL be the same UUID used in the key - **AND** `timestamp` SHALL be ISO-8601 format - **AND** `formName` SHALL be one of `"contact"`, `"demo-booking"`, `"newsletter"` - **AND** `fields` SHALL be an object with form-specific field names and values - **AND** `ip` SHALL be a hashed IP (never raw) - **AND** `spam` SHALL be a boolean

scenarios.name: Valid submission value structure
scenarios.when: a submission is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the value SHALL be a JSON object with keys: `schema`, `id`, `timestamp`, `formName`, `fields`, `utm`, `ip`, `emailSent`, `spam`

requirements.name: Notification Log Value Schema
requirements.description: The system SHALL enforce a consistent JSON schema for notification log KV values. - **WHEN** a notification log is stored in KV - **THEN** the value SHALL be a JSON object with keys: `schema`, `id`, `timestamp`, `type`, `to`, `subject`, `success` - **AND** `schema` SHALL be `"notification.v1"` - **AND** `id` SHALL be the same UUID used in the key

scenarios.name: Valid notification log structure
scenarios.when: a notification log is stored in KV
scenarios.then: the value SHALL be a JSON object with keys: `schema`, `id`, `timestamp`, `type`, `to`, `subject`, `success`

requirements.name: Audit Logging
requirements.description: The system SHALL write audit entries to KV for all data mutation operations and admin access events. - **WHEN** a form submission is accepted and stored in KV - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_write"` and the submission key in `resource` - **WHEN** the admin export endpoint is accessed - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_export"` and the actor's hashed IP - **WHEN** a successful admin login occurs - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login"` - **WHEN** an audit entry is stored - **THEN** the value SHALL include: `schema`, `id`, `timestamp`, `action`, `actor`, `resource`, `detail` - **WHEN** audit KV write fails - **THEN** the originating operation SHALL still succeed - **AND** a console warning SHALL be logged

scenarios.name: Audit entry on submission write
scenarios.when: a form submission is accepted and stored in KV
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_write"` and the submission key in `resource`

scenarios.name: Audit entry on submission export
scenarios.when: the admin export endpoint is accessed
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_export"` and the actor's hashed IP

scenarios.name: Audit entry on admin login
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login"`

scenarios.name: Audit entry value structure
scenarios.then: the value SHALL include: `schema`, `id`, `timestamp`, `action`, `actor`, `resource`, `detail`

scenarios.name: KV write failure does not block audit
scenarios.then: the originating operation SHALL still succeed

requirements.name: Admin Pagination
requirements.description: The admin submissions endpoint SHALL support offset-based pagination via query parameters. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions` is called without page params - **THEN** page 1 with 20 items per page SHALL be returned - **AND** the response SHALL include `pagination: { page: 1, perPage: 20, total: N, pages: ceil(N/20) }` - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?page=2&per_page=10` is called - **THEN** 10 items from the second page SHALL be returned - **AND** `pagination.page` SHALL be 2, `pagination.perPage` SHALL be 10 - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?page=999` is called and 999 exceeds total pages - **THEN** an empty submissions array SHALL be returned with correct pagination metadata - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?per_page=200` is called - **THEN** `per_page` SHALL be capped to 100 (the maximum allowed)

scenarios.name: Default page
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions` is called without page params
scenarios.then: page 1 with 20 items per page SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Specific page
scenarios.then: 10 items from the second page SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Out of range page
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions?page=999` is called and 999 exceeds total pages
scenarios.then: an empty submissions array SHALL be returned with correct pagination metadata

scenarios.name: Page capped to max
scenarios.then: `per_page` SHALL be capped to 100 (the maximum allowed)

requirements.name: Admin Data Export
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose an export endpoint at `/api/admin/submissions/export` that returns CSV or JSON formatted submission data. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv` is called with valid Bearer token - **THEN** the response SHALL have Content-Type `text/csv` - **AND** the CSV SHALL include a header row with all field keys - **AND** each submission SHALL be one row - **AND** the Content-Disposition header SHALL be `attachment; filename="optiflow-submissions.csv"` - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=json` is called - **THEN** the response SHALL be a JSON array of submission objects - **AND** Content-Type SHALL be `application/json` - **AND** the Content-Disposition header SHALL be `attachment; filename="optiflow-submissions.json"` - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv&formName=contact` is called - **THEN** only submissions with `formName: "contact"` SHALL be included in the export - **WHEN** the export endpoint is called without a valid Bearer token - **THEN** HTTP 401 SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: CSV export
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv` is called with valid Bearer token
scenarios.then: the response SHALL have Content-Type `text/csv`

scenarios.name: JSON export
scenarios.then: the response SHALL be a JSON array of submission objects

scenarios.name: Filtered export
scenarios.then: only submissions with `formName: "contact"` SHALL be included in the export

scenarios.name: Unauthenticated export
scenarios.when: the export endpoint is called without a valid Bearer token
scenarios.then: HTTP 401 SHALL be returned

requirements.name: Data Lifecycle Management
requirements.description: The system SHALL automatically purge expired records via a scheduled Worker. - **WHEN** the scheduled cleanup worker runs - **THEN** all `sub:` keys with timestamp older than 30 days SHALL be deleted from KV - **WHEN** the scheduled cleanup worker runs - **THEN** all `notif:` keys with timestamp older than 7 days SHALL be deleted from KV - **WHEN** the scheduled cleanup worker runs - **THEN** all `audit:` keys with timestamp older than 90 days SHALL be deleted from KV - **WHEN** the cleanup worker completes a run - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "data_cleanup"` and `detail: { submissions_deleted: N, notifications_deleted: N, audit_deleted: N }`

scenarios.name: Submission TTL enforcement
scenarios.then: all `sub:` keys with timestamp older than 30 days SHALL be deleted from KV

scenarios.name: Notification TTL enforcement
scenarios.then: all `notif:` keys with timestamp older than 7 days SHALL be deleted from KV

scenarios.name: Audit TTL enforcement
scenarios.then: all `audit:` keys with timestamp older than 90 days SHALL be deleted from KV

scenarios.name: Cleanup audit log
scenarios.when: the cleanup worker completes a run
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "data_cleanup"` and `detail: { submissions_deleted: N, notifications_deleted: N, audit_deleted: N }`

requirements.name: Subscriber Deduplication
requirements.description: The system SHALL deduplicate newsletter subscribers by email address. - **WHEN** a newsletter signup is accepted - **THEN** the subscriber SHALL be stored at key `subscriber:{sha256(email)}` - **AND** the value SHALL include `{ email, subscribedAt, source }` - **WHEN** an existing subscriber email is submitted again - **THEN** the existing KV key SHALL be overwritten with updated `subscribedAt` - **AND** the older timestamp SHALL be preserved in `firstSeen`

scenarios.name: New subscriber stored
scenarios.then: the subscriber SHALL be stored at key `subscriber:{sha256(email)}`

scenarios.name: Duplicate subscriber overwrite
scenarios.when: an existing subscriber email is submitted again
scenarios.then: the existing KV key SHALL be overwritten with updated `subscribedAt`

requirements.name: KV Namespace Configuration
requirements.description: The system SHALL use separate Cloudflare KV namespaces for different data domains. - **WHEN** Workers are deployed - **THEN** the `SUBMISSIONS` namespace binding SHALL store form submissions - **AND** the `NOTIFICATIONS` namespace binding SHALL store email notification logs - **AND** the `AUDIT` namespace binding SHALL store audit log entries

scenarios.name: Namespace binding
scenarios.then: the `SUBMISSIONS` namespace binding SHALL store form submissions

requirements.name: CSS Custom Properties
requirements.description: The system SHALL define all colors as CSS custom properties in `:root`. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded on any page - **WHEN** a component references `var(--bg)`, `var(--fg)`, `var(--surface)`, `var(--muted)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--accent)`, `var(--teal)`, `var(--green)`, or `var(--lime)` - **THEN** the correct color value SHALL be applied - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** a component references `var(--accent-soft)`, `var(--teal-soft)`, or `var(--green-soft)` - **THEN** a low-opacity mix of the corresponding brand color SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Color variables available
scenarios.then: the correct color value SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Soft color variants
scenarios.then: a low-opacity mix of the corresponding brand color SHALL be applied

requirements.name: Border Hierarchy Tokens
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide CSS custom properties for the complete border hierarchy defined in the brand color palette. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded on any page - **WHEN** a component references `var(--border-soft)`, `var(--border-default)`, or `var(--border-strong)` - **THEN** the correct border color SHALL be applied - **AND** `--border-soft` SHALL be the lightest (subtle dividers) - **AND** `--border-default` SHALL match the existing `--border` token - **AND** `--border-strong` SHALL be the strongest (focus states, emphasized separators) - **GIVEN** `<html data-theme="dark">` - **WHEN** border tokens are rendered - **THEN** each border token SHALL resolve to its dark-theme equivalent

scenarios.name: Three-level border tokens available
scenarios.then: the correct border color SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Dark theme border tokens
scenarios.then: each border token SHALL resolve to its dark-theme equivalent

requirements.name: Color Palette Completeness
requirements.description: The system SHALL include CSS custom properties for all color roles defined in the brand specification. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** a component references `var(--fg-disabled)` - **THEN** the disabled/inactive text color SHALL be applied - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** a component references `var(--glow-accent)` - **THEN** the decorative glow color SHALL be applied - **AND** the value SHALL match the brand specification's glow token

scenarios.name: Disabled text token
scenarios.then: the disabled/inactive text color SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Glow accent token
scenarios.then: the decorative glow color SHALL be applied

requirements.name: Z-Index Scale
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide named z-index tokens for consistent vertical layering across all pages. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** any component needs a z-index value - **THEN** it SHALL reference a `--z-*` token - **AND** `--z-base` SHALL be the lowest (0) - **AND** `--z-content` SHALL be for content layering (1) - **AND** `--z-sticky` SHALL be for sticky elements (90) - **AND** `--z-nav` SHALL be for navigation (100) - **AND** `--z-dropdown` SHALL be for dropdowns (101) - **AND** `--z-drawer` SHALL be for mobile drawer (101) - **AND** `--z-hamburger` SHALL be for hamburger button (102) - **AND** `--z-scroll-top` SHALL be for scroll-to-top button (199) - **AND** `--z-skip-link` SHALL be the highest for skip-to-content (1000) - **GIVEN** `core.css` has been updated - **WHEN** any component rule sets `z-index` - **THEN** it SHALL use a `var(--z-*)` reference instead of a hardcoded number

scenarios.name: Z-index tokens available
scenarios.when: any component needs a z-index value
scenarios.then: it SHALL reference a `--z-*` token

scenarios.name: Existing z-index values replaced
scenarios.then: it SHALL use a `var(--z-*)` reference instead of a hardcoded number

requirements.name: Transition Tokens
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide transition duration and easing tokens for consistent motion. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** a component references `--transition-fast`, `--transition-base`, or `--transition-slow` - **THEN** the correct duration (150ms, 250ms, 350ms) SHALL be applied - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** a component references `var(--ease-out)`, `var(--ease-in)`, `var(--ease-in-out)`, or `var(--ease-spring)` - **THEN** the correct cubic-bezier curve SHALL be applied - **AND** `--ease-out` SHALL be used for entrances - **AND** `--ease-in` SHALL be used for exits - **AND** `--ease-in-out` SHALL be used for symmetric transitions - **AND** `--ease-spring` SHALL be used for bouncy entrances - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** `.reveal`, `.reveal-left`, or `.reveal-right` elements animate - **THEN** transform distance SHALL use `var(--motion-distance, 28px)`

scenarios.name: Duration tokens available
scenarios.then: the correct duration (150ms, 250ms, 350ms) SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Easing curve tokens available
scenarios.then: the correct cubic-bezier curve SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Motion distance token available
scenarios.then: transform distance SHALL use `var(--motion-distance, 28px)`

requirements.name: Scroll Reveal Variants
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide directional and scale reveal variants. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** an element has `.reveal-left`, `.reveal-right`, or `.reveal-scale` - **THEN** each SHALL have independent opacity and transform transitions using `var(--ease-out)` - **AND** `.reveal-left` SHALL enter from the left - **AND** `.reveal-right` SHALL enter from the right - **AND** `.reveal-scale` SHALL enter via scale-up animation - **AND** all variants SHALL be observed by the IntersectionObserver in core.js - **GIVEN** any `.reveal` or reveal-variant element - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the element SHALL have `will-change: opacity, transform` - **AND** the compositor SHALL promote the element to a GPU layer before the transition fires

scenarios.name: Directional reveal variants
scenarios.then: each SHALL have independent opacity and transform transitions using `var(--ease-out)`

scenarios.name: GPU-accelerated reveals
scenarios.then: the element SHALL have `will-change: opacity, transform`

requirements.name: Noise Texture
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide configurable noise texture overlay via CSS custom properties. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** `.noise-overlay` is used with `var(--noise-opacity)` - **THEN** the noise texture SHALL render at the defined opacity - **AND** dark theme SHALL use a higher opacity value for visibility on dark backgrounds

scenarios.name: Noise opacity token
scenarios.then: the noise texture SHALL render at the defined opacity

requirements.name: Neutral-to-Brand Color Ratio
requirements.description: The system SHALL maintain approximately 88% neutral / 12% brand color ratio. - **GIVEN** any page in the marketing website - **WHEN** the page is visually assessed - **THEN** the majority of surface area SHALL use neutral colors (--bg, --surface, --fg, --muted, --border) - **AND** brand colors (--accent, --teal, --green, --lime) SHALL be reserved for accent elements, CTAs, and highlights

scenarios.name: Neutral-dominant interface
scenarios.given: any page in the marketing website
scenarios.then: the majority of surface area SHALL use neutral colors (--bg, --surface, --fg, --muted, --border)

requirements.name: Light and Dark Themes
requirements.description: The system SHALL support light and dark themes via the `data-theme` attribute on the `<html>` element. - **GIVEN** `<html data-theme="light">` or no `data-theme` attribute - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** light-theme color values from `:root` SHALL be applied - **GIVEN** `<html data-theme="dark">` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** dark-theme color values from `[data-theme="dark"]` SHALL override `:root` values - **AND** shadow tokens SHALL switch to dark-mode shadows - **AND** noise texture opacity SHALL increase

scenarios.name: Light theme (default)
scenarios.then: light-theme color values from `:root` SHALL be applied

scenarios.name: Dark theme
scenarios.then: dark-theme color values from `[data-theme="dark"]` SHALL override `:root` values

requirements.name: Typography
requirements.description: The system SHALL use Inter as the primary font family and JetBrains Mono for monospace. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** headings and body text render - **THEN** `--font-display` and `--font-body` SHALL resolve to Inter with system font fallbacks - **AND** `--font-mono` SHALL resolve to JetBrains Mono with monospace fallbacks

scenarios.name: Display and body text
scenarios.then: `--font-display` and `--font-body` SHALL resolve to Inter with system font fallbacks

requirements.name: Type Scale
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a complete type scale with responsive clamping. - **GIVEN** `<h1>` and `<h2>` elements - **WHEN** the page renders at any viewport width - **THEN** `h1` SHALL use `--fs-h1` (clamped between 36px and 68px) - **AND** `h2` SHALL use `--fs-h2` (clamped between 28px and 46px) - **AND** `h3` SHALL use `--fs-h3` (20px) - **GIVEN** `.lead`, `.body`, `.meta`, and `.eyebrow` elements - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** `.lead` SHALL use 18px, `.body` SHALL use 16px, `.meta` SHALL use 13px - **AND** `.eyebrow` SHALL use 12px uppercase monospace with teal color

scenarios.name: Heading sizes
scenarios.when: the page renders at any viewport width
scenarios.then: `h1` SHALL use `--fs-h1` (clamped between 36px and 68px)

scenarios.name: Body and utility sizes
scenarios.then: `.lead` SHALL use 18px, `.body` SHALL use 16px, `.meta` SHALL use 13px

requirements.name: 8px Baseline Grid
requirements.description: The system SHALL use an 8px baseline grid spacing system. - **GIVEN** `core.css` is loaded - **WHEN** any layout spacing is applied - **THEN** spacing SHALL use `var(--gap-*)` tokens: `--gap-xs` (8px), `--gap-sm` (12px), `--gap-md` (20px), `--gap-lg` (32px), `--gap-xl` (56px), `--gap-2xl` (96px)

scenarios.name: Gap tokens
scenarios.then: spacing SHALL use `var(--gap-*)` tokens: `--gap-xs` (8px), `--gap-sm` (12px), `--gap-md` (20px), `--gap-lg` (32px), `--gap-xl` (56px), `--gap-2xl` (96px)

requirements.name: Shared Components
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide shared component styles via utility classes in `core.css`. - **GIVEN** an element with `.btn-primary`, `.btn-secondary`, or `.btn-ghost` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** `.btn-primary` SHALL render as a gradient blue button with glow effect - **AND** `.btn-secondary` SHALL render as an outlined button with backdrop blur - **AND** `.btn-ghost` SHALL render as a transparent text button - **AND** all buttons SHALL have hover lift, active press, and focus-visible states - **GIVEN** an element with `.card` or `.glass-card` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** `.card` SHALL render with surface background, border, border-radius, and shadow - **AND** `.glass-card` SHALL render with backdrop blur and semi-transparent background - **AND** both SHALL have hover lift and border-color transition - **GIVEN** elements with `.grid-2`, `.grid-3`, `.grid-4`, or `.grid-5` - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** child elements SHALL be arranged in responsive CSS Grid columns matching the class name - **AND** grids SHALL collapse to single column at mobile breakpoints - **GIVEN** FAQ markup with `.faq-item`, `.faq-question`, `.faq-answer` - **WHEN** a question is clicked - **THEN** the answer SHALL expand with a `grid-template-rows` transition - **AND** the icon SHALL rotate 45 degrees

scenarios.name: Button variants
scenarios.then: `.btn-primary` SHALL render as a gradient blue button with glow effect

scenarios.name: Card components
scenarios.then: `.card` SHALL render with surface background, border, border-radius, and shadow

scenarios.name: Grid layouts
scenarios.then: child elements SHALL be arranged in responsive CSS Grid columns matching the class name

scenarios.name: FAQ accordion
scenarios.then: the answer SHALL expand with a `grid-template-rows` transition

requirements.name: No Hardcoded Colors
requirements.description: The system SHALL NOT use hardcoded hex colors in page content outside the design system, except within `<style>` blocks, SVG `fill` attributes, and `core.css` token definitions. - **GIVEN** any page HTML content outside `<style>` blocks and SVG fills - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** hardcoded hex colors SHALL be flagged as warnings - **GIVEN** `core.css` defines CSS custom properties using OKLCH and hex values - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** hex values within `core.css` token definitions SHALL NOT be flagged

scenarios.name: Validation enforces variable usage
scenarios.given: any page HTML content outside `<style>` blocks and SVG fills
scenarios.then: hardcoded hex colors SHALL be flagged as warnings

scenarios.name: core.css is exempt from hex validation
scenarios.given: `core.css` defines CSS custom properties using OKLCH and hex values
scenarios.then: hex values within `core.css` token definitions SHALL NOT be flagged

requirements.name: Email Dispatch Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a POST endpoint at `/api/email` that accepts JSON with notification type, recipient data, and template context, and sends an email via the Resend API. - **WHEN** a POST request is received at `/api/email` with `{ "type": "contact", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com" }, "utm": {} }` - **THEN** an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Contact Form Submission — OptiFlow OS" - **AND** the email body SHALL be HTML with OptiFlow brand styling (logo, accent colors, footer) - **AND** the response SHALL be HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "type": "contact" }` - **WHEN** a POST request is received with `{ "type": "demo-booking", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com", "demoDate": "2026-07-15" } }` - **THEN** an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Demo Booking — OptiFlow OS" - **WHEN** a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }` - **THEN** an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Newsletter Signup — OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: Send team notification for contact form
scenarios.when: a POST request is received at `/api/email` with `{ "type": "contact", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com" }, "utm": {} }`
scenarios.then: an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Contact Form Submission — OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: Send team notification for demo booking
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with `{ "type": "demo-booking", "fields": { "name": "Test", "email": "test@example.com", "demoDate": "2026-07-15" } }`
scenarios.then: an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Demo Booking — OptiFlow OS"

scenarios.name: Send team notification for newsletter
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }`
scenarios.then: an email SHALL be sent to `info@optiflow.co.in` with subject "New Newsletter Signup — OptiFlow OS"

requirements.name: Subscriber Welcome Email
requirements.description: The system SHALL send a welcome confirmation email to the subscriber when a newsletter signup is received. - **WHEN** a POST request is received with `{ "type": "newsletter", "fields": { "email": "sub@example.com" } }` - **THEN** a welcome email SHALL be sent to `sub@example.com` with subject "Welcome to OptiFlow OS" - **AND** the welcome email SHALL include the OptiFlow OS brand logo, a brief introduction, and a link to the website - **AND** the welcome email SHALL include an unsubscribe notice - **WHEN** a POST request is received with type other than "newsletter" - **THEN** no subscriber-facing email SHALL be sent

scenarios.name: Welcome email sent to subscriber
