requirements.description: The engine SHALL link specs to tasks, tasks to git commits, and commits to changed files, producing a full traceability matrix. - **GIVEN** a task from `openspec/changes/update-header/tasks.md` is completed - **WHEN** the task is committed as `feat: update nav header` - **THEN** the traceability matrix SHALL record: `spec.md#header-requirement → tasks.md#T3 → commit abc123 → src/pages/index.html` - **AND** any unlinked commit SHALL be flagged during `/opsx:verify` - **GIVEN** a change is archived via `/opsx:archive` - **WHEN** the archive process completes - **THEN** a traceability report SHALL be generated listing all spec deltas merged - **AND** the report SHALL include the full chain for each delta

scenarios.name: Commit traceability
scenarios.when: the task is committed as `feat: update nav header`
scenarios.then: the traceability matrix SHALL record: `spec.md#header-requirement → tasks.md#T3 → commit abc123 → src/pages/index.html`

scenarios.name: Archive traceability report
scenarios.then: a traceability report SHALL be generated listing all spec deltas merged

requirements.name: Phase Detection
requirements.description: The engine SHALL detect the correct OpenSpec phase from the current change state (explore, propose, apply, verify, archive) and prevent phase-skipping. - **GIVEN** no prior `/opsx:explore` has been run - **WHEN** `/opsx:propose new-feature` is invoked - **THEN** the engine SHALL warn that exploration has not been done - **AND** the engine SHALL auto-run explore before proceeding - **GIVEN** a change has been applied but not verified - **WHEN** `/opsx:archive` is invoked - **THEN** the engine SHALL reject the archive request - **AND** the engine SHALL emit: "Run /opsx:verify first"

scenarios.name: Must explore before propose
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL warn that exploration has not been done

scenarios.name: Must verify before archive
scenarios.given: a change has been applied but not verified
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL reject the archive request

requirements.name: Command Auto-Orchestration
requirements.description: The user SHALL run a single OpenSpec command; the engine SHALL handle all subordinate routing, validation, and quality gating automatically. - **GIVEN** the user runs `/orchestrate "add testimonial section to homepage"` - **WHEN** the orchestration engine processes the request - **THEN** the engine SHALL auto-discover affected specs (marketing-pages, design-system, shared-components) - **AND** the engine SHALL route to planner → tdd-guide → code-reviewer → security-reviewer in sequence - **AND** the engine SHALL run pre-build validation before any code changes - **AND** the engine SHALL run post-build validation after implementation - **AND** the engine SHALL enforce all quality gates before completion - **GIVEN** the `/opsx:apply` command is invoked - **WHEN** tasks.md contains tasks tagged with `@agent:tdd-guide`, `@skill:design-system`, `@mcp:context7` - **THEN** the engine SHALL route each task to the specified resource - **AND** tasks with no explicit routing SHALL be auto-routed based on affected specs - **AND** the engine SHALL run hooks for each file change automatically

scenarios.name: Single command full pipeline
scenarios.given: the user runs `/orchestrate "add testimonial section to homepage"`
scenarios.when: the orchestration engine processes the request
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL auto-discover affected specs (marketing-pages, design-system, shared-components)

scenarios.name: Command chain execution
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL route each task to the specified resource

requirements.name: Preconnect Resource Hints
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject `<link rel="preconnect">` hints for third-party origins into every page's `<head>`. - **WHEN** a page is assembled - **THEN** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>` SHALL be injected - **WHEN** a page is assembled with Plausible analytics - **THEN** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://plausible.io">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Google Fonts preconnect
scenarios.then: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Analytics preconnect
scenarios.when: a page is assembled with Plausible analytics
scenarios.then: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://plausible.io">` SHALL be injected

requirements.name: Lazy Image Loading
requirements.description: Images below the viewport fold SHALL use `loading="lazy"` to defer loading. - **WHEN** an `` tag appears outside the initial viewport on content pages (feature-showcase, product-overview, competitive-positioning) - **THEN** the image SHALL include `loading="lazy"` attribute - **WHEN** an `` tag appears in the hero section or above fold - **THEN** the image SHALL NOT include `loading="lazy"`

scenarios.name: Below-fold images lazily loaded
scenarios.when: an `` tag appears outside the initial viewport on content pages (feature-showcase, product-overview, competitive-positioning)
scenarios.then: the image SHALL include `loading="lazy"` attribute

scenarios.name: Above-fold images remain eager
scenarios.when: an `` tag appears in the hero section or above fold
scenarios.then: the image SHALL NOT include `loading="lazy"`

requirements.name: CSS and JS Minification
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL minify CSS and JavaScript assets during production build. - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** `dist/assets/css/core.css` SHALL be minified (whitespace and comments removed) - **AND** `dist/assets/js/core.js` SHALL be minified - **AND** source files in `assets/` SHALL remain unminified for development - **WHEN** minification is applied to core.css (current ~26KB) and core.js (current ~18KB) - **THEN** the combined output SHALL be at least 20% smaller than unminified versions

scenarios.name: Build-time minification
scenarios.then: `dist/assets/css/core.css` SHALL be minified (whitespace and comments removed)

scenarios.name: Minification reduces size
scenarios.when: minification is applied to core.css (current ~26KB) and core.js (current ~18KB)
scenarios.then: the combined output SHALL be at least 20% smaller than unminified versions

requirements.name: GATE_PERF Quality Gate
requirements.description: The orchestration engine SHALL include a GATE_PERF step that validates performance budgets. - **WHEN** the build pipeline config runs GATE_PERF - **THEN** it SHALL check that CSS + JS combined size does not exceed 100KB - **AND** it SHALL verify Lighthouse Performance score >= 0.8 for all configured pages

scenarios.name: Performance gate in build pipeline
scenarios.when: the build pipeline config runs GATE_PERF
scenarios.then: it SHALL check that CSS + JS combined size does not exceed 100KB

requirements.name: Dependency Vulnerability Monitoring
requirements.description: The platform SHALL include `npm audit` scanning as part of the CI pipeline to detect known vulnerabilities in Node.js dependencies. - **WHEN** the CI pipeline runs on a pull request - **THEN** `npm audit --production` SHALL execute after test steps - **AND** HIGH or CRITICAL vulnerabilities SHALL cause the step to fail - **WHEN** `npm audit --production` completes - **THEN** a summary of vulnerabilities found SHALL be displayed in CI logs

scenarios.name: Audit runs in CI
scenarios.when: the CI pipeline runs on a pull request
scenarios.then: `npm audit --production` SHALL execute after test steps

scenarios.name: Audit report is visible
scenarios.then: a summary of vulnerabilities found SHALL be displayed in CI logs

requirements.name: Unique Title Tag
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have a unique, descriptive `<title>` tag. - **GIVEN** the `site.json` pages array - **WHEN** any page is assembled - **THEN** the `<title>` SHALL match the page's `title` field exactly - **AND** no two pages SHALL share the same title

scenarios.name: Title uniqueness
scenarios.then: the `<title>` SHALL match the page's `title` field exactly

requirements.name: Unique Meta Description
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have a unique meta description between 120 and 160 characters. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** a `<meta name="description" content="...">` tag SHALL be present - **AND** the description SHALL come from the page's `description` field in `site.json` - **AND** it SHALL be between 120 and 160 characters in length - **AND** the validation script SHALL error if any page's description is shorter than 120 or longer than 160 characters

scenarios.name: Description presence and length
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="description" content="...">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Tags
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have complete OpenGraph meta tags. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** `og:title` SHALL be present and populated - **AND** `og:description` SHALL match the page's meta description - **AND** `og:image` SHALL point to the OptiFlow logo (`/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png`) - **AND** `og:type` SHALL be set to "website" (or "article" for the newsletter page) - **AND** `og:url` SHALL be the page's canonical absolute URL - **AND** `og:site_name` SHALL be "OptiFlow OS" - **AND** `og:locale` SHALL be "en_IN" - **AND** `og:image:width` and `og:image:height` SHALL be present with values 512

scenarios.name: OG tag presence
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: `og:title` SHALL be present and populated

requirements.name: Twitter Card
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have a Twitter card meta tag. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on Twitter/X - **THEN** a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Twitter card presence
scenarios.when: the page is shared on Twitter/X
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: Single H1 Element
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have exactly one `<h1>` element. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** exactly one `<h1>` element SHALL exist - **AND** it SHALL contain the primary page heading

scenarios.name: H1 uniqueness
scenarios.then: exactly one `<h1>` element SHALL exist

requirements.name: Clean URLs
requirements.description: The system SHALL produce search-engine-friendly, directory-based URLs without `.html` extensions. - **GIVEN** a page defined in `site.json` as `/features/index.html` - **WHEN** the page is built - **THEN** the accessible URL SHALL be `/features/` - **AND** no `.html` extension SHALL appear in any navigation link

scenarios.name: URL structure
scenarios.then: the accessible URL SHALL be `/features/`

requirements.name: Canonical URL Tag
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include a canonical URL meta tag pointing to its absolute URL. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** a `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>` - **AND** the href SHALL be the absolute URL matching the page's site.json file path

scenarios.name: Canonical URL present
scenarios.then: a `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

requirements.name: Robots Meta Tag
requirements.description: Every public page SHALL include a robots meta tag allowing indexing. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** a `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

scenarios.name: Robots tag present on all pages
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

requirements.name: OpenGraph URL
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include an `og:url` meta tag with its canonical URL. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** a `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present - **AND** the content SHALL match the page's canonical URL

scenarios.name: OG URL present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: a `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Site Name
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include an `og:site_name` meta tag. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** a `<meta property="og:site_name" content="OptiFlow OS">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: OG site name present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: a `<meta property="og:site_name" content="OptiFlow OS">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Locale
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include an `og:locale` meta tag set to `en_IN`. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** a `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_IN">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: OG locale present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: a `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_IN">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Image Dimensions
requirements.description: The OG image meta tag SHALL include width and height attributes for complete social preview rendering. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** `<meta property="og:image:width" content="512">` and `<meta property="og:image:height" content="512">` tags SHALL be present

scenarios.name: OG image dimensions present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: `<meta property="og:image:width" content="512">` and `<meta property="og:image:height" content="512">` tags SHALL be present

requirements.name: Twitter Image Card
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include `twitter:image` and `twitter:image:alt` meta tags. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on Twitter/X - **THEN** a `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` tag SHALL be present - **AND** a `<meta name="twitter:image:alt" content="OptiFlow OS Logo">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Twitter image tags present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on Twitter/X
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: BreadcrumbList Structured Data
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include BreadcrumbList JSON-LD structured data reflecting the page's position in the site hierarchy. - **GIVEN** an assembled page at `/features/` - **WHEN** the page loads in a browser - **THEN** a `<script type="application/ld+json">` block SHALL contain a `BreadcrumbList` schema - **AND** the list SHALL include `{ "@type": "ListItem", "position": 1, "name": "Home", "item": "https://optiflow.in/" }` - **AND** the list SHALL end with an item matching the current page name

scenarios.name: BreadcrumbList present on sub-pages
scenarios.when: the page loads in a browser
scenarios.then: a `<script type="application/ld+json">` block SHALL contain a `BreadcrumbList` schema

requirements.name: Article Structured Data on Newsletter
requirements.description: The newsletter page SHALL include Article JSON-LD structured data. - **GIVEN** the assembled newsletter page at `/newsletter/` - **WHEN** the page loads in a browser - **THEN** a `<script type="application/ld+json">` block SHALL contain an `Article` schema - **AND** the Article SHALL include `headline`, `description`, `publisher` (Organization), and `datePublished` fields

scenarios.name: Article schema on newsletter page
scenarios.when: the page loads in a browser
scenarios.then: a `<script type="application/ld+json">` block SHALL contain an `Article` schema

requirements.name: Favicon
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a favicon on every page. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page loads in a browser - **THEN** a `<link rel="icon" href="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png" sizes="any">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

scenarios.name: Favicon present
scenarios.when: the page loads in a browser
scenarios.then: a `<link rel="icon" href="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png" sizes="any">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

requirements.name: Canonical Page Inventory
requirements.description: The `site.json` pages array SHALL be the canonical page inventory for SEO metadata. - **GIVEN** the `site.json` pages array - **WHEN** the build runs or validation executes - **THEN** all page titles, descriptions, and URL paths SHALL be sourced from this array - **AND** any page not in the array SHALL not be generated - **AND** validation SHALL verify consistency between `site.json` metadata and generated pages

scenarios.name: Single source of truth
scenarios.when: the build runs or validation executes
scenarios.then: all page titles, descriptions, and URL paths SHALL be sourced from this array

requirements.name: Shared Navigation Component
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a shared navigation component with desktop navigation and a mobile drawer. - **GIVEN** a page with viewport width > 1024px - **WHEN** the navigation renders - **THEN** all nav links SHALL be displayed inline via `.desktop-nav` - **AND** the hamburger button SHALL be hidden - **AND** the "Resources" link SHALL open a dropdown on hover - **GIVEN** a page with viewport width <= 1024px - **WHEN** the hamburger button is clicked - **THEN** the mobile drawer SHALL slide in from the right - **AND** a semi-transparent overlay SHALL appear - **AND** clicking the overlay or any drawer link SHALL close the drawer - **GIVEN** the current page has an `active` field in `site.json` (e.g., "Solutions") - **WHEN** the nav is injected during build - **THEN** the corresponding nav link SHALL receive the `.active` class - **AND** the active link SHALL render with accent color and an underline indicator

scenarios.name: Desktop navigation
scenarios.then: all nav links SHALL be displayed inline via `.desktop-nav`

scenarios.name: Mobile drawer
scenarios.then: the mobile drawer SHALL slide in from the right

scenarios.name: Active link indicator
scenarios.given: the current page has an `active` field in `site.json` (e.g., "Solutions")
scenarios.when: the nav is injected during build
scenarios.then: the corresponding nav link SHALL receive the `.active` class

requirements.name: Shared Footer Component
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a shared footer component with branded columns. - **GIVEN** any page with an injected footer - **WHEN** the footer renders - **THEN** it SHALL display a 5-column grid: Brand, Product, Solutions, Resources, Contact - **AND** the Contact column SHALL include phone (tel: link), email (mailto: link), and location text - **AND** the bottom bar SHALL display the copyright notice with the current year and company name

scenarios.name: Footer structure
scenarios.given: any page with an injected footer
scenarios.then: it SHALL display a 5-column grid: Brand, Product, Solutions, Resources, Contact

requirements.name: Theme Toggle
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a theme toggle (light/dark) that persists preference to localStorage. - **GIVEN** the page header - **WHEN** the theme toggle button is clicked - **THEN** the `data-theme` attribute on `<html>` SHALL toggle between "light" and "dark" - **AND** the preference SHALL be saved to `localStorage` under key `optiflow-theme` - **GIVEN** the page footer - **WHEN** the footer theme toggle button is clicked - **THEN** the theme SHALL toggle identically to the nav toggle - **AND** both toggles SHALL remain synchronized - **GIVEN** a theme toggle button - **WHEN** the current theme is "light" - **THEN** the sun icon SHALL be visible and the moon icon SHALL be hidden - **WHEN** the current theme is "dark" - **THEN** the moon icon SHALL be visible and the sun icon SHALL be hidden

scenarios.name: Theme toggle in navigation
scenarios.when: the theme toggle button is clicked
scenarios.then: the `data-theme` attribute on `<html>` SHALL toggle between "light" and "dark"

scenarios.name: Theme toggle in footer
scenarios.when: the footer theme toggle button is clicked
scenarios.then: the theme SHALL toggle identically to the nav toggle

scenarios.name: Icon display per theme
scenarios.when: the current theme is "light" the current theme is "dark"
scenarios.then: the sun icon SHALL be visible and the moon icon SHALL be hidden the moon icon SHALL be visible and the sun icon SHALL be hidden

requirements.name: Scroll-Reveal Animation
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide scroll-reveal animation via IntersectionObserver. - **GIVEN** elements marked with `.reveal` class - **WHEN** an element enters the viewport (threshold 0.1, rootMargin -40px) - **THEN** the `.visible` class SHALL be added - **AND** the element SHALL be unobserved after becoming visible - **AND** staggered children within `.stagger-3`, `.stagger-4`, `.stagger-5` SHALL animate sequentially

scenarios.name: Elements revealed on scroll
scenarios.when: an element enters the viewport (threshold 0.1, rootMargin -40px)
scenarios.then: the `.visible` class SHALL be added

requirements.name: Animated Counters
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide animated counter elements for statistics. - **GIVEN** an element with `data-count` attribute - **WHEN** the element enters the viewport (threshold 0.5) - **THEN** a number animation SHALL run with easeOutExpo easing over the specified duration - **AND** the counter SHALL be unobserved after animation completes - **AND** `data-prefix`, `data-suffix`, and `data-fmt` attributes SHALL format the output

scenarios.name: Counter animation
scenarios.then: a number animation SHALL run with easeOutExpo easing over the specified duration

requirements.name: FAQ Accordion
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide FAQ accordion with expand/collapse functionality. - **GIVEN** FAQ items with `.faq-question` buttons - **WHEN** a question is clicked - **THEN** the parent `.faq-item` SHALL toggle the `.open` class - **AND** `aria-expanded` SHALL update to reflect the state - **AND** the answer area SHALL animate open via CSS grid transition

scenarios.name: Accordion toggle
scenarios.then: the parent `.faq-item` SHALL toggle the `.open` class

requirements.name: Sticky CTA Visibility
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide sticky CTA button visibility control based on scroll position. - **GIVEN** a `.sticky-cta` element - **WHEN** the user scrolls past 70% of the hero section height - **THEN** the `.visible` class SHALL be added, making the CTA visible and clickable

scenarios.name: CTA appears after hero
scenarios.when: the user scrolls past 70% of the hero section height
scenarios.then: the `.visible` class SHALL be added, making the CTA visible and clickable

requirements.name: Scroll-to-Top Button
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a scroll-to-top button with visibility control. - **GIVEN** a `.scroll-top` button - **WHEN** the user scrolls past 600px - **THEN** the `.visible` class SHALL be added - **AND** clicking the button SHALL smoothly scroll to the top of the page

scenarios.name: Button appears after scrolling
scenarios.then: the `.visible` class SHALL be added

requirements.name: Skip-to-Content Link
requirements.description: The navigation partial SHALL include a skip-to-content link as the first focusable element in the page. - **GIVEN** any page with injected navigation - **WHEN** the page renders and the user presses Tab - **THEN** a "Skip to content" link SHALL appear at the top of the viewport - **AND** clicking it SHALL move focus to `<main id="content">`

scenarios.name: Skip link present and functional
scenarios.when: the page renders and the user presses Tab
scenarios.then: a "Skip to content" link SHALL appear at the top of the viewport

requirements.name: Mobile Drawer Active State
requirements.description: The mobile drawer SHALL resolve active link indicators identical to desktop nav. - **GIVEN** the current page has `active: "Features"` in `site.json` - **WHEN** the mobile drawer is opened - **THEN** the Features link in the drawer SHALL have the `.active` class

scenarios.name: Active link in mobile drawer
scenarios.then: the Features link in the drawer SHALL have the `.active` class

requirements.name: Resources Dropdown
requirements.description: The Resources dropdown in both desktop nav and mobile drawer SHALL contain Newsletter, FAQ, and Competitive Positioning. - **GIVEN** the nav is rendered - **WHEN** the Resources dropdown is opened - **THEN** it SHALL display "Newsletter", "FAQ", and "Competitive Positioning" - **AND** the Competitive Positioning link SHALL point to `/competitive-positioning/`

scenarios.name: Resources dropdown content
scenarios.then: it SHALL display "Newsletter", "FAQ", and "Competitive Positioning"

requirements.name: Footer Placeholders
requirements.description: The footer partial SHALL use `{{PHONE}}`, `{{EMAIL}}`, `{{LOCATION}}`, `{{COMPANY}}`, and `{{YEAR}}` placeholders instead of hardcoded values. - **GIVEN** the footer partial source - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** phone SHALL be `{{PHONE}}` (not a hardcoded number) - **AND** email SHALL be `{{EMAIL}}` (not a hardcoded email) - **AND** year SHALL be `{{YEAR}}` (not a hardcoded year)

scenarios.name: Footer uses placeholders
scenarios.then: phone SHALL be `{{PHONE}}` (not a hardcoded number)

requirements.name: Navigation CTA
requirements.description: The nav CTA area SHALL show a single "Book Demo" button linking to `/demo-booking/`. - **GIVEN** the nav header - **WHEN** it renders - **THEN** only one CTA button ("Book Demo") SHALL be visible - **AND** no secondary "Watch Demo" button SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Single CTA in nav
scenarios.then: only one CTA button ("Book Demo") SHALL be visible

requirements.name: Keyboard Drawer Control
requirements.description: The interactive runtime SHALL close the mobile drawer on Escape key press. - **GIVEN** the mobile drawer is open - **WHEN** the user presses the Escape key - **THEN** the drawer SHALL close - **AND** the hamburger button SHALL return to closed state

scenarios.name: Escape closes drawer
scenarios.when: the user presses the Escape key
scenarios.then: the drawer SHALL close

requirements.name: Unified FAQ Accordion
requirements.description: All FAQ toggles SHALL use the `.faq-question` class exclusively. - **GIVEN** any FAQ toggle button with class `.faq-question` - **WHEN** the button is clicked - **THEN** the parent `.faq-item` SHALL toggle `.open` - **AND** `aria-expanded` SHALL update to reflect state

scenarios.name: FAQ toggle via .faq-question
scenarios.given: any FAQ toggle button with class `.faq-question`
scenarios.then: the parent `.faq-item` SHALL toggle `.open`

requirements.name: Unified Counter System
requirements.description: All animated counters SHALL use `data-count` attribute. Incorrect `data-target` usage SHALL be migrated. - **GIVEN** an element with `data-count="1500"` - **WHEN** the element enters the viewport - **THEN** an easeOutExpo animation SHALL count from 0 to 1500 - **AND** `data-prefix`, `data-suffix`, `data-fmt`, `data-duration` attributes SHALL be respected

scenarios.name: Counter via data-count
scenarios.then: an easeOutExpo animation SHALL count from 0 to 1500

requirements.name: Navigation Links
requirements.description: The navigation bar SHALL include a "Showcase" link pointing to `/feature-showcase/`. - **GIVEN** `site.json` nav configuration - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** the `nav.links` array SHALL include `{ "label": "Showcase", "href": "/feature-showcase/" }`

scenarios.name: Showcase nav link present
scenarios.then: the `nav.links` array SHALL include `{ "label": "Showcase", "href": "/feature-showcase/" }`

requirements.name: Shared Form Submission Utility
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `submitForm(formElement)` function in `core.js` that handles form submission with state management, validation, UTM capture, honey-pot injection, and endpoint posting. - **GIVEN** a `<form>` element with `data-endpoint` attribute - **WHEN** `submitForm(formElement)` is called - **THEN** the form SHALL add class `form-submitting` - **AND** all inputs and the submit button SHALL be disabled - **AND** form data SHALL be collected from all named inputs, selects, and textareas (excluding `_hp`) - **AND** UTM parameters from the URL SHALL be appended to the payload - **AND** a honey-pot field (`_hp`) SHALL be injected if not present - **GIVEN** a `<form>` element with no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` - **WHEN** `submitForm(formElement)` is called - **THEN** the form SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` - **AND** the payload SHALL be structured as `{ formName, fields, honeyPot, utm }` - **GIVEN** a form in `form-submitting` state - **WHEN** the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with `{ success: false, error: "..." }` or HTTP 422/429 - **THEN** `form-submitting` class SHALL be removed - **AND** `form-error` class SHALL be added - **AND** the API error message SHALL be displayed to the user - **GIVEN** a form in `form-submitting` state - **WHEN** the endpoint returns a 2xx response with `{ success: true }` - **THEN** `form-submitting` class SHALL be removed - **AND** `form-success` class SHALL be added - **AND** the form's inner content SHALL be replaced with a success message element - **GIVEN** a form in `form-submitting` state - **WHEN** the fetch fails or returns an error response - **THEN** `form-submitting` class SHALL be removed - **AND** `form-error` class SHALL be added - **AND** an error message SHALL be displayed with a retry button - **AND** clicking retry SHALL call `submitForm()` again - **GIVEN** the demo booking or contact page - **WHEN** the form's submit event fires - **THEN** page-level validation (required fields, email/phone format) SHALL run first - **AND** only after validation passes SHALL `submitForm()` from core.js be called

scenarios.name: Form submission with state transitions
scenarios.then: the form SHALL add class `form-submitting`

scenarios.name: Default endpoint routing
scenarios.given: a `<form>` element with no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify`
scenarios.then: the form SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit`

scenarios.name: API response error handling
scenarios.when: the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with `{ success: false, error: "..." }` or HTTP 422/429
scenarios.then: `form-submitting` class SHALL be removed

scenarios.name: Success state rendering
scenarios.when: the endpoint returns a 2xx response with `{ success: true }`
scenarios.then: `form-submitting` class SHALL be removed

scenarios.name: Error state with retry
scenarios.when: the fetch fails or returns an error response
scenarios.then: `form-submitting` class SHALL be removed

scenarios.name: Page-specific validation preserves pre-submit check
scenarios.given: the demo booking or contact page
scenarios.then: page-level validation (required fields, email/phone format) SHALL run first

requirements.name: Skeleton Loading for Content Grids
requirements.description: Content sections that display multiple items (articles, features, resources) SHALL support skeleton loading placeholders using the `.skeleton` CSS classes defined in `core.css`. - **GIVEN** a page with a content grid - **WHEN** skeletons are injected via `showSkeleton(container, 'card', count)` - **THEN** the correct number of skeleton card placeholders SHALL appear - **AND** each placeholder SHALL have the shimmer animation - **AND** `prefers-reduced-motion` SHALL disable the animation - **GIVEN** skeleton placeholders are visible on a page - **WHEN** a screen reader encounters the skeleton elements - **THEN** the skeleton elements SHALL have `aria-hidden="true"`

scenarios.name: Skeleton cards render correctly
scenarios.given: a page with a content grid
scenarios.then: the correct number of skeleton card placeholders SHALL appear

scenarios.name: Skeletons are hidden from screen readers
scenarios.given: skeleton placeholders are visible on a page
scenarios.when: a screen reader encounters the skeleton elements
scenarios.then: the skeleton elements SHALL have `aria-hidden="true"`

requirements.name: Empty State for Filtered Content
requirements.description: When a category filter or search returns no matching content, an empty state SHALL be displayed with a descriptive message. - **GIVEN** a page with a category-filterable content grid - **WHEN** the user selects a category that has no matching items - **THEN** an empty state component SHALL be shown - **AND** the empty state SHALL include an icon, a title, and a descriptive paragraph - **GIVEN** an empty state is displayed due to a filter - **WHEN** the user interacts with a "Clear filter" or "View all" button - **THEN** the full content list SHALL be restored

scenarios.name: Category filter returns no results
scenarios.given: a page with a category-filterable content grid
scenarios.when: the user selects a category that has no matching items
scenarios.then: an empty state component SHALL be shown

scenarios.name: Empty state includes recovery action
scenarios.given: an empty state is displayed due to a filter
scenarios.when: the user interacts with a "Clear filter" or "View all" button
scenarios.then: the full content list SHALL be restored

requirements.name: Form Submission Loading State
requirements.description: All forms on the site SHALL display a loading state during submission using the `.form-submitting` CSS class from `core.css`. - **GIVEN** the newsletter subscription form - **WHEN** the user submits the form - **THEN** the form SHALL enter the `.form-submitting` state - **AND** the submit button SHALL show a loading spinner - **AND** the form fields SHALL be dimmed - **GIVEN** the demo booking form - **WHEN** the user submits the form - **THEN** the form SHALL enter the `.form-submitting` state - **GIVEN** the contact form - **WHEN** the user submits the form - **THEN** the form SHALL enter the `.form-submitting` state

scenarios.name: Newsletter form shows loading state
scenarios.then: the form SHALL enter the `.form-submitting` state

scenarios.name: Demo booking form shows loading state
scenarios.then: the form SHALL enter the `.form-submitting` state

scenarios.name: Contact form shows loading state
scenarios.then: the form SHALL enter the `.form-submitting` state

requirements.name: E2E Test Coverage
requirements.description: The testing suite SHALL cover all published pages in `site.json` across 5 test dimensions. - **GIVEN** the `site.json` pages array defines the published page set - **WHEN** the E2E test suite runs - **THEN** each published page (excluding `noindex` pages) SHALL be covered by at least one test spec - **AND** accessibility tests SHALL scan all pages with axe-core - **GIVEN** the test suite at `tests/e2e/` - **WHEN** any page is modified - **THEN** the following dimensions SHALL be verified: - Accessibility (zero critical/serious WCAG violations) - SEO (title, meta description, OG tags, single h1) - Navigation (200 status, nav visibility, link clicks, mobile drawer) - Responsive (no horizontal overflow at 6 breakpoints) - Core assets (core.css, core.js, footer loaded on every page)

scenarios.name: All pages covered
scenarios.given: the `site.json` pages array defines the published page set
scenarios.then: each published page (excluding `noindex` pages) SHALL be covered by at least one test spec

scenarios.name: Test dimensions
scenarios.then: the following dimensions SHALL be verified:

requirements.name: Browser Coverage
requirements.description: The test suite SHALL run across Chromium, Firefox, WebKit desktop, and Chromium mobile. - **GIVEN** Playwright is configured with 4 projects - **WHEN** `npm test` is executed - **THEN** all test specs SHALL run on chromium-desktop, firefox-desktop, webkit-desktop, and chromium-mobile

scenarios.name: Multi-browser execution
scenarios.then: all test specs SHALL run on chromium-desktop, firefox-desktop, webkit-desktop, and chromium-mobile

requirements.name: CI Integration
requirements.description: The test suite SHALL be integrated into the CI/CD pipeline and quality gates via a GitHub Actions workflow. - **GIVEN** a pull request is opened against `main` - **WHEN** the CI workflow triggers - **THEN** `npm test` SHALL run as a required step - **AND** the PR SHALL be blocked if any test fails - **GIVEN** the orchestration engine runs quality gates - **WHEN** GATE_TEST is executed - **THEN** the gate SHALL pass only when all E2E tests pass across all browser projects - **GIVEN** the project repository - **WHEN** a CI pipeline is required - **THEN** `.github/workflows/ci.yml` SHALL exist - **AND** it SHALL run on pull requests to `main`

scenarios.name: PR validation includes tests
scenarios.given: a pull request is opened against `main`
scenarios.then: `npm test` SHALL run as a required step

scenarios.name: GATE_TEST enforcement
scenarios.given: the orchestration engine runs quality gates
scenarios.then: the gate SHALL pass only when all E2E tests pass across all browser projects

scenarios.name: CI workflow exists
scenarios.then: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` SHALL exist

requirements.name: Accessibility Gate
requirements.description: Accessibility tests SHALL block deployment on critical or serious axe-core violations. - **GIVEN** the accessibility test spec - **WHEN** axe-core identifies a critical or serious WCAG violation on any page - **THEN** the test SHALL fail - **AND** the violation details SHALL be reported in the test output

scenarios.name: A11y violations block deploy
scenarios.when: axe-core identifies a critical or serious WCAG violation on any page
scenarios.then: the test SHALL fail

requirements.name: Test Independence
requirements.description: Each test spec SHALL be independently runnable and not depend on state from other specs. - **GIVEN** `fullyParallel: true` is configured in Playwright - **WHEN** multiple test files execute concurrently - **THEN** no test SHALL depend on cookies, localStorage, or DOM state set by another test

scenarios.name: Fully parallel execution
scenarios.then: no test SHALL depend on cookies, localStorage, or DOM state set by another test

skillMetadata.name: openspec-verify
skillMetadata.description: Verify that an implementation matches spec deltas — run build, validation, tests, quality gates, and design compliance checks. Use when the user wants to verify a change before archiving or after implementation.

skillMetadata.name: e2e-testing
skillMetadata.description: Playwright E2E testing patterns, Page Object Model, configuration, CI/CD integration, artifact management, and flaky test strategies.

skillMetadata.name: react-testing
skillMetadata.description: React component testing with React Testing Library, Vitest/Jest, MSW for network mocking, accessibility assertions with axe, and the decision boundary between component tests and Playwright/Cypress end-to-end runs. Use when writing or fixing tests for React components, hooks, or pages.

skillMetadata.name: rust-testing
skillMetadata.description: Rust testing patterns including unit tests, integration tests, async testing, property-based testing, mocking, and coverage. Follows TDD methodology.

skillMetadata.name: tdd-workflow
skillMetadata.description: Use this skill when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. Enforces test-driven development with 80%+ coverage including unit, integration, and E2E tests.

skillMetadata.name: tinystruct-patterns
skillMetadata.description: Expert guidance for developing with the tinystruct Java framework. Use when working on the tinystruct codebase or any project built on tinystruct — including creating Application classes, @Action-mapped routes, unit tests, ActionRegistry, HTTP/CLI dual-mode handling, the built-in HTTP server, the event system, JSON with Builder/Builders, database persistence with AbstractData, POJO generation, Server-Sent Events (SSE), file uploads, and outbound HTTP networking.
triggers: for heavy tasks triggered by events

skillMetadata.name: windows-desktop-e2e
skillMetadata.description: E2E testing for Windows native desktop apps (WPF, WinForms, Win32/MFC, Qt) using pywinauto and Windows UI Automation.

skillMetadata.name: ui-demo
skillMetadata.description: Record polished UI demo videos using Playwright. Use when the user asks to create a demo, walkthrough, screen recording, or tutorial video of a web application. Produces WebM videos with visible cursor, natural pacing, and professional feel.

requirements.name: WCAG 2.2 Level AA Compliance
requirements.description: The system SHALL meet WCAG 2.2 Level AA standards across all pages. - **GIVEN** any page on the website - **WHEN** the page is tested against WCAG 2.2 Level AA criteria - **THEN** all success criteria SHALL be satisfied

scenarios.name: Standards conformance
scenarios.when: the page is tested against WCAG 2.2 Level AA criteria
scenarios.then: all success criteria SHALL be satisfied

requirements.name: Skip-to-Content Link
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a skip-to-content link on every page. - **GIVEN** a keyboard user navigates to a page - **WHEN** the Tab key is pressed - **THEN** a "Skip to content" link SHALL become visible at the top of the page - **AND** activating the link SHALL move focus to the `<main>` content area

scenarios.name: Skip link visibility
scenarios.given: a keyboard user navigates to a page
scenarios.then: a "Skip to content" link SHALL become visible at the top of the page

requirements.name: Visible Focus Indicators
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide visible `:focus-visible` outlines on all interactive elements. - **GIVEN** a keyboard user navigates interactive elements - **WHEN** any element receives focus via keyboard - **THEN** a 2px solid teal (`var(--teal)`) outline with 2px offset SHALL appear - **AND** the outline SHALL have a 4px border-radius

scenarios.name: Keyboard focus visible
scenarios.given: a keyboard user navigates interactive elements
scenarios.when: any element receives focus via keyboard
scenarios.then: a 2px solid teal (`var(--teal)`) outline with 2px offset SHALL appear

requirements.name: Minimum Contrast Ratio
requirements.description: The system SHALL maintain a minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for all text content. - **GIVEN** any text content on any page - **WHEN** the foreground and background colors are measured - **THEN** the contrast ratio SHALL be at least 4.5:1 - **AND** large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold) SHALL meet at least 3:1 - **GIVEN** the build validation pipeline runs - **WHEN** `npm run validate` executes - **THEN** all oklch color variable pairs defined in `core.css` SHALL be checked for WCAG 2.2 AA contrast compliance - **AND** any pair below 4.5:1 (normal text) or 3:1 (large text) SHALL produce a warning

scenarios.name: Text contrast sufficient
scenarios.given: any text content on any page
scenarios.when: the foreground and background colors are measured
scenarios.then: the contrast ratio SHALL be at least 4.5:1

scenarios.name: Automated contrast validation
scenarios.then: all oklch color variable pairs defined in `core.css` SHALL be checked for WCAG 2.2 AA contrast compliance

requirements.name: Semantic HTML Landmarks
requirements.description: The system SHALL use semantic HTML landmarks on every page. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** navigation SHALL be wrapped in `<header role="navigation">` - **AND** main content SHALL be wrapped in `<main>` - **AND** footer SHALL be wrapped in `<footer>`

scenarios.name: Landmark structure
scenarios.then: navigation SHALL be wrapped in `<header role="navigation">`

requirements.name: ARIA Labels
requirements.description: The system SHALL include `aria-label` attributes on interactive elements. - **GIVEN** interactive elements without visible text labels - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** each SHALL have an `aria-label` attribute (e.g., hamburger button: "Toggle menu", theme toggle: "Toggle theme") - **AND** FAQ question buttons SHALL have `aria-expanded` reflecting open/closed state - **AND** form validation errors SHALL set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields

scenarios.name: Interactive element labeling
scenarios.given: interactive elements without visible text labels
scenarios.then: each SHALL have an `aria-label` attribute (e.g., hamburger button: "Toggle menu", theme toggle: "Toggle theme")

requirements.name: Reduced Motion
requirements.description: The system SHALL respect the `prefers-reduced-motion` media query. - **GIVEN** the user's system has `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` enabled - **WHEN** any page renders - **THEN** all CSS animations and transitions SHALL be disabled (duration 0.01ms) - **AND** all `.reveal` and staggered elements SHALL be fully visible (opacity: 1, transform: none) - **AND** glow and CTA gradient animations SHALL be disabled

scenarios.name: Animations disabled
scenarios.given: the user's system has `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` enabled
scenarios.then: all CSS animations and transitions SHALL be disabled (duration 0.01ms)

requirements.name: Keyboard Navigation
requirements.description: The system SHALL ensure all navigation and interactive elements are keyboard-accessible. - **GIVEN** a keyboard-only user navigates the website - **WHEN** the Tab key is used to move through interactive elements - **THEN** all nav links, buttons, theme toggles, FAQ accordion items, and form fields SHALL be focusable and operable - **AND** the mobile drawer SHALL not trap focus when closed - **AND** the mobile drawer SHALL close on Escape key press when open

scenarios.name: Full keyboard operability
scenarios.given: a keyboard-only user navigates the website
scenarios.when: the Tab key is used to move through interactive elements
scenarios.then: all nav links, buttons, theme toggles, FAQ accordion items, and form fields SHALL be focusable and operable

requirements.name: Calendar Keyboard Accessibility
requirements.description: The demo-booking calendar SHALL be keyboard accessible with all date cells as `<button>` elements. - **GIVEN** the calendar widget on the demo-booking page - **WHEN** the user focuses a date cell and presses Enter or Space - **THEN** the date SHALL be selected - **AND** `aria-label` SHALL describe the full date

scenarios.name: Calendar date via keyboard
scenarios.given: the calendar widget on the demo-booking page
scenarios.when: the user focuses a date cell and presses Enter or Space
scenarios.then: the date SHALL be selected

requirements.name: Form Validation Accessibility
requirements.description: Form validation errors SHALL be communicated to screen readers via `aria-invalid`. - **GIVEN** a form with validation on demo-booking or contact page - **WHEN** a required field is empty and the form is submitted - **THEN** the field SHALL receive `aria-invalid="true"`

scenarios.name: Invalid field announced
scenarios.given: a form with validation on demo-booking or contact page
scenarios.when: a required field is empty and the form is submitted
scenarios.then: the field SHALL receive `aria-invalid="true"`

requirements.name: Animation Pause on Visibility Change
requirements.description: Animations SHALL pause when the page is hidden (tab switch, minimize). - **GIVEN** a typewriter animation is running - **WHEN** the user switches to a different browser tab - **THEN** the typewriter SHALL pause - **AND** it SHALL resume when the tab becomes visible again

scenarios.name: Typewriter pauses on blur
scenarios.when: the user switches to a different browser tab
scenarios.then: the typewriter SHALL pause

requirements.name: E2E Accessibility Testing
requirements.description: The system SHALL include automated accessibility scans for all public pages in the E2E test suite. - **GIVEN** the E2E test suite runs - **WHEN** any public page is loaded - **THEN** an `axe-core` scan SHALL run at `wcag2aa` standard - **AND** zero violations SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Page-level axe scan
scenarios.then: an `axe-core` scan SHALL run at `wcag2aa` standard

requirements.name: Admin Login Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a POST endpoint at `/api/admin/login` that accepts admin credentials and returns a JWT token. - **WHEN** a POST request is received at `/api/admin/login` with `{ "username": "<ADMIN_USERNAME>", "password": "<ADMIN_PASSWORD>" }` - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "token": "<JWT>" }` - **AND** the JWT SHALL be signed with the JWT_SECRET environment variable - **AND** the JWT SHALL expire after 24 hours - **WHEN** a POST request is received with incorrect username or password - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 401 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Invalid credentials" }` - **WHEN** a POST request is received without username or password fields - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Missing credentials" }`

scenarios.name: Valid credentials
scenarios.when: a POST request is received at `/api/admin/login` with `{ "username": "<ADMIN_USERNAME>", "password": "<ADMIN_PASSWORD>" }`
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "token": "<JWT>" }`

scenarios.name: Invalid credentials
scenarios.when: a POST request is received with incorrect username or password
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 401 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Invalid credentials" }`

scenarios.name: Missing credentials
scenarios.when: a POST request is received without username or password fields
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 400 with `{ "success": false, "error": "Missing credentials" }`

requirements.name: Admin Token Verification Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a POST endpoint at `/api/admin/verify` that validates an existing JWT token. - **WHEN** a POST request with `{ "token": "<valid-jwt>" }` is received - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "valid": true }` - **WHEN** a POST request with an expired or malformed token is received - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "valid": false }`

scenarios.name: Valid token
scenarios.when: a POST request with `{ "token": "<valid-jwt>" }` is received
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "valid": true }`

scenarios.name: Invalid or expired token
scenarios.when: a POST request with an expired or malformed token is received
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "valid": false }`

requirements.name: Admin Submissions Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a GET endpoint at `/api/admin/submissions` that returns recent form submissions with field values, secured by Bearer token authentication, sourced from Cloudflare KV. - **WHEN** a GET request with `Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>` is received - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "submissions": [...] }` - **AND** each submission SHALL include `timestamp`, `formName`, `fields` (key-value pairs), `utm`, `emailSent` - **AND** submissions SHALL be sorted by timestamp descending (most recent first) - **AND** field values SHALL be included (not just keys) since admin is authenticated - **WHEN** a GET request without a valid Bearer token is received - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 401

scenarios.name: Authenticated request returns submissions with values
scenarios.when: a GET request with `Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>` is received
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "submissions": [...] }`

scenarios.name: Unauthenticated request
scenarios.when: a GET request without a valid Bearer token is received
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 401

requirements.name: Admin Stats Endpoint
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose a GET endpoint at `/api/admin/stats` that returns aggregated submission statistics. - **WHEN** a GET request with `Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>` is received - **THEN** the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "stats": { "total": <N>, "today": <N>, "byForm": { "contact": <N>, "demo-booking": <N>, "newsletter": <N> } } }`

scenarios.name: Authenticated stats request
scenarios.when: a GET request with `Authorization: Bearer <valid-jwt>` is received
scenarios.then: the endpoint SHALL return HTTP 200 with `{ "success": true, "stats": { "total": <N>, "today": <N>, "byForm": { "contact": <N>, "demo-booking": <N>, "newsletter": <N> } } }`

requirements.name: Submission KV Storage
requirements.description: The form processing endpoint SHALL store each valid submission in Cloudflare KV namespace `SUBMISSIONS`. - **WHEN** a valid form submission is accepted - **THEN** the submission SHALL be stored in KV with key `sub:<ISO-timestamp>-<random-6chars>` - **AND** the value SHALL be JSON with `{ timestamp, formName, fields, utm, ip, emailSent, spam }` - **AND** KV write failure SHALL NOT fail the submission (logged as warning)

scenarios.name: Submission stored in KV
scenarios.when: a valid form submission is accepted
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL be stored in KV with key `sub:<ISO-timestamp>-<random-6chars>`

requirements.name: Admin Credential Storage
requirements.description: Admin credentials SHALL be stored as Cloudflare Worker environment variables (secrets). - **WHEN** the Worker starts - **THEN** ADMIN_USERNAME, ADMIN_PASSWORD, and JWT_SECRET SHALL be accessible via `env`

scenarios.name: Credentials available
scenarios.then: ADMIN_USERNAME, ADMIN_PASSWORD, and JWT_SECRET SHALL be accessible via `env`

requirements.name: Admin Page
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide an admin page at `/admin/` with login form and dashboard UI toggled by auth state. - **WHEN** a user navigates to `/admin/` without a valid session - **THEN** the login form SHALL be displayed - **AND** the dashboard UI SHALL be hidden - **WHEN** a user navigates to `/admin/` with a valid stored token - **THEN** the token SHALL be verified via `/api/admin/verify` - **AND** on success, the dashboard SHALL be shown and the login form hidden - **AND** recent submissions SHALL be loaded from `/api/admin/submissions`

scenarios.name: Unauthenticated visitor
scenarios.when: a user navigates to `/admin/` without a valid session
scenarios.then: the login form SHALL be displayed

scenarios.name: Authenticated visitor
scenarios.when: a user navigates to `/admin/` with a valid stored token
scenarios.then: the token SHALL be verified via `/api/admin/verify`

requirements.name: Admin Navigation Isolation
requirements.description: The admin page SHALL NOT be linked from the public navigation or footer. - **WHEN** the public site navigation and footer are rendered - **THEN** no link to `/admin/` SHALL be present - **AND** the admin page SHALL have `<meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow">`

scenarios.name: No public links to admin
scenarios.when: the public site navigation and footer are rendered
scenarios.then: no link to `/admin/` SHALL be present

requirements.name: Admin Design Components
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide admin-specific CSS component classes in core.css. - **WHEN** the admin page renders the login form - **THEN** `.admin-login`, `.admin-login-card`, `.admin-login-header` classes SHALL style the login UI - **AND** the design SHALL follow the existing design system tokens (colors, spacing, typography) - **WHEN** the admin dashboard renders - **THEN** `.admin-dashboard`, `.admin-submission-list`, `.admin-submission-item` classes SHALL style the dashboard UI - **AND** `.admin-stats-grid` and `.admin-stat-card` SHALL display submission statistics - **AND** `.admin-filter-bar` SHALL provide search input and form-type filter dropdown

scenarios.name: Admin login form
scenarios.when: the admin page renders the login form
scenarios.then: `.admin-login`, `.admin-login-card`, `.admin-login-header` classes SHALL style the login UI

scenarios.name: Admin dashboard
scenarios.then: `.admin-dashboard`, `.admin-submission-list`, `.admin-submission-item` classes SHALL style the dashboard UI

requirements.name: Dashboard Stats Display
requirements.description: The admin dashboard SHALL display aggregate submission statistics upon authentication. - **WHEN** the dashboard becomes visible after auth - **THEN** stats SHALL be fetched from `/api/admin/stats` - **AND** total, today, and by-form counts SHALL be displayed in stat cards

scenarios.name: Stats loaded on dashboard open
scenarios.when: the dashboard becomes visible after auth
scenarios.then: stats SHALL be fetched from `/api/admin/stats`

requirements.name: Submission Search/Filter
requirements.description: The admin dashboard SHALL provide client-side filtering of submissions. - **WHEN** user selects a form type from the filter dropdown - **THEN** only submissions matching that form type SHALL be shown - **WHEN** user types in the search input - **THEN** submissions whose field values contain the search text SHALL be shown (case-insensitive)

scenarios.name: Filter by form type
scenarios.when: user selects a form type from the filter dropdown
scenarios.then: only submissions matching that form type SHALL be shown

scenarios.name: Search submissions
scenarios.when: user types in the search input
scenarios.then: submissions whose field values contain the search text SHALL be shown (case-insensitive)

requirements.name: Auto-Refresh
requirements.description: The admin dashboard SHALL auto-refresh submissions every 30 seconds. - **WHEN** the dashboard is visible - **THEN** a 30-second interval SHALL poll `/api/admin/submissions` - **AND** the submission list SHALL update with any new entries - **AND** on logout the interval SHALL be cleared

scenarios.name: Auto-polling
scenarios.then: a 30-second interval SHALL poll `/api/admin/submissions`

requirements.name: Submission Detail Toggle
requirements.description: Each submission item SHALL support expand/collapse to show full field values. - **WHEN** user clicks a submission item - **THEN** the item SHALL expand to show all field key-value pairs - **AND** clicking again SHALL collapse the detail

scenarios.name: Expand submission detail
scenarios.then: the item SHALL expand to show all field key-value pairs

requirements.name: Admin Submissions Pagination
requirements.description: The admin submissions endpoint SHALL support offset-based pagination via query parameters. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions` is called without page params - **THEN** page 1 with 20 items per page SHALL be returned - **AND** the response SHALL include `pagination: { page: 1, perPage: 20, total: N, pages: ceil(N/20) }` - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?per_page=200` is called - **THEN** `per_page` SHALL be capped to 100 (the maximum allowed) - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions?formName=contact` is called - **THEN** only submissions with matching formName SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Default page
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions` is called without page params
scenarios.then: page 1 with 20 items per page SHALL be returned

scenarios.name: Per-page capped to max
scenarios.then: `per_page` SHALL be capped to 100 (the maximum allowed)

scenarios.name: Form type filter
scenarios.then: only submissions with matching formName SHALL be returned

requirements.name: Admin Data Export
requirements.description: The system SHALL expose an export endpoint at `/api/admin/submissions/export` with CSV and JSON format support, secured by Bearer token. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv` is called with valid Bearer token - **THEN** the response SHALL be `text/csv` with `Content-Disposition: attachment` header - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=json` is called - **THEN** the response SHALL be a JSON array of submission objects - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv&formName=contact` is called - **THEN** only matching submissions SHALL be included - **WHEN** an export is performed - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_export"`

scenarios.name: CSV export
scenarios.when: GET `/api/admin/submissions/export?format=csv` is called with valid Bearer token
scenarios.then: the response SHALL be `text/csv` with `Content-Disposition: attachment` header

scenarios.name: JSON export
scenarios.then: the response SHALL be a JSON array of submission objects

scenarios.name: Filtered export
scenarios.then: only matching submissions SHALL be included

scenarios.name: Audit on export
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "submission_export"`

requirements.name: Subscriber Metrics
requirements.description: The admin stats endpoint SHALL include newsletter subscriber count. - **WHEN** GET `/api/admin/stats` is called - **THEN** the response SHALL include `stats.subscribers` with count of `subscriber:*` KV keys

scenarios.name: Subscribers in stats
scenarios.then: the response SHALL include `stats.subscribers` with count of `subscriber:*` KV keys

requirements.name: Admin Audit Logging
requirements.description: The system SHALL write audit entries on admin authentication events. - **WHEN** admin login succeeds - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login"` - **WHEN** admin login fails - **THEN** an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login_failed"`

scenarios.name: Successful login audit
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login"`

scenarios.name: Failed login audit
scenarios.then: an audit entry SHALL be written with `action: "admin_login_failed"`

requirements.name: Admin Tab Accessibility
requirements.description: The admin dashboard tablist SHALL follow the WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with `aria-controls` attributes. - **WHEN** the admin dashboard renders the tab bar - **THEN** each tab button SHALL have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the corresponding `role="tabpanel"` element - **AND** each `<div role="tabpanel">` SHALL have an `id` matching the `aria-controls` value

scenarios.name: Tab aria-controls
scenarios.when: the admin dashboard renders the tab bar
scenarios.then: each tab button SHALL have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the corresponding `role="tabpanel"` element

requirements.name: Page Assembly
requirements.description: The system SHALL assemble all pages from `src/pages/`, `src/partials/`, and shared assets into the `dist/` directory via `npm run build`. - **GIVEN** a clean project with no `dist/` directory - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** all 12 pages SHALL be generated under `dist/` with clean URLs (directory-based, `/page-name/index.html`) - **AND** the home page SHALL be generated at `dist/index.html` - **GIVEN** an existing `dist/` directory - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** the existing `dist/` SHALL be removed and recreated from scratch

scenarios.name: Full build from clean state
scenarios.given: a clean project with no `dist/` directory
scenarios.then: all 12 pages SHALL be generated under `dist/` with clean URLs (directory-based, `/page-name/index.html`)

scenarios.name: Rebuild overwrites previous output
scenarios.then: the existing `dist/` SHALL be removed and recreated from scratch

requirements.name: SEO Meta Injection
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject canonical URLs, OpenGraph URL/site name/locale/dimensions, Twitter image, and robots meta tags into every assembled page. - **GIVEN** a page with file path `features/index.html` in site.json - **WHEN** the build pipeline assembles the page - **THEN** `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/features/">` SHALL be injected into `<head>` - **GIVEN** any page being assembled - **WHEN** the build pipeline processes the page - **THEN** `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:site_name" content="OptiFlow OS">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_IN">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:image:width" content="512">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta property="og:image:height" content="512">` SHALL be injected - **GIVEN** any page being assembled - **WHEN** the build pipeline processes the page - **THEN** `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` SHALL be injected into `<head>` - **GIVEN** any page being assembled - **WHEN** the build pipeline processes the page - **THEN** `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<meta name="twitter:image:alt" content="OptiFlow OS Logo">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Canonical URL injection
scenarios.given: a page with file path `features/index.html` in site.json
scenarios.when: the build pipeline assembles the page
scenarios.then: `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/features/">` SHALL be injected into `<head>`

scenarios.name: OG URL and site name injection
scenarios.when: the build pipeline processes the page
scenarios.then: `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Robots meta injection
scenarios.when: the build pipeline processes the page
scenarios.then: `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` SHALL be injected into `<head>`

scenarios.name: Twitter image injection
scenarios.when: the build pipeline processes the page
scenarios.then: `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` SHALL be injected

requirements.name: BreadcrumbList Structured Data Injection
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject BreadcrumbList JSON-LD structured data into every assembled page. - **GIVEN** a page at `/problem-solutions/` - **WHEN** the build pipeline injects JSON-LD - **THEN** a BreadcrumbList schema SHALL be present with items for Home and Solutions

scenarios.name: BreadcrumbList generation
scenarios.then: a BreadcrumbList schema SHALL be present with items for Home and Solutions

requirements.name: Article Structured Data on Newsletter
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject Article JSON-LD structured data into the newsletter page. - **GIVEN** the newsletter page - **WHEN** the build pipeline injects JSON-LD - **THEN** an Article schema SHALL be present with headline, description, publisher, and datePublished fields

scenarios.name: Article schema on newsletter
scenarios.then: an Article schema SHALL be present with headline, description, publisher, and datePublished fields

requirements.name: Placeholder Variable Replacement
requirements.description: The system SHALL replace `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` variables in source pages with values from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** a source page contains `{{PHONE}}`, `{{EMAIL}}`, `{{YEAR}}`, `{{SITE_NAME}}` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** each placeholder SHALL be replaced with its corresponding value from `site.json` - **AND** no `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` variables SHALL remain in the output - **GIVEN** a source page contains `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` and `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** these SHALL be replaced with the page's title and description from the `site.json` pages array

scenarios.name: Canonical data injection
scenarios.then: each placeholder SHALL be replaced with its corresponding value from `site.json`

scenarios.name: Page-specific metadata injection
scenarios.then: these SHALL be replaced with the page's title and description from the `site.json` pages array

requirements.name: Partial Injection
requirements.description: The system SHALL inject navigation and footer partials via `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->` and `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->` comments. - **GIVEN** a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/nav.html` - **AND** the active page indicator SHALL be resolved based on the page's `active` field in `site.json` - **AND** active state SHALL be resolved for both desktop nav AND mobile drawer links - **GIVEN** a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->` - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/footer.html`

scenarios.name: Navigation injection
scenarios.given: a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: nav -->`
scenarios.then: the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/nav.html`

scenarios.name: Footer injection
scenarios.given: a source page contains `<!-- INCLUDE: footer -->`
scenarios.then: the comment SHALL be replaced with the contents of `src/partials/footer.html`

requirements.name: Asset Copying
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL copy shared assets (CSS, JS, images) from `assets/` into `dist/assets/`, and SHALL minify CSS and JS during production build. - **GIVEN** the `assets/` directory contains `css/core.css`, `js/core.js`, and image files - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** all files and subdirectories from `assets/` SHALL be recursively copied to `dist/assets/` - **AND** each page SHALL reference assets via `/assets/css/core.css` and `/assets/js/core.js` - **GIVEN** a design specification directory exists in the project root - **WHEN** the build runs - **THEN** PNG logo files SHALL be copied into `dist/assets/img/` - **WHEN** assets are copied in production build mode - **THEN** `core.css` and `core.js` SHALL be minified before writing to `dist/assets/` - **AND** source files in `assets/` SHALL remain unminified

scenarios.name: Shared assets copy
scenarios.given: the `assets/` directory contains `css/core.css`, `js/core.js`, and image files
scenarios.then: all files and subdirectories from `assets/` SHALL be recursively copied to `dist/assets/`

scenarios.name: Logo image detection
scenarios.given: a design specification directory exists in the project root
scenarios.then: PNG logo files SHALL be copied into `dist/assets/img/`

scenarios.name: CSS and JS minification
scenarios.when: assets are copied in production build mode
scenarios.then: `core.css` and `core.js` SHALL be minified before writing to `dist/assets/`

requirements.name: Validation
requirements.description: The system SHALL validate all generated pages via `npm run validate`, checking internal links, hardcoded hex colors, dark mode coverage, SEO tags, and data consistency. - **GIVEN** a generated page contains internal links (`href="/path/"`) - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** each internal link SHALL be verified as resolvable against the `dist/` directory - **AND** broken links SHALL be reported as errors - **GIVEN** a generated page contains inline hex colors outside `<style>` blocks and SVG `fill` attributes - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** each hardcoded hex color SHALL be reported as a warning - **GIVEN** a generated page does NOT contain `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** a warning SHALL be reported for that page - **GIVEN** a generated page - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** the system SHALL verify `<title>`, meta description, `<h1>`, and OG tags exist - **AND** missing tags SHALL be reported as errors or warnings - **GIVEN** a generated page - **WHEN** `npm run validate` is executed - **THEN** the system SHALL check for stale phone numbers and locations - **AND** inconsistencies SHALL be reported as errors

scenarios.name: Internal link validation
scenarios.given: a generated page contains internal links (`href="/path/"`)
scenarios.then: each internal link SHALL be verified as resolvable against the `dist/` directory

scenarios.name: Hardcoded color detection
scenarios.given: a generated page contains inline hex colors outside `<style>` blocks and SVG `fill` attributes
scenarios.then: each hardcoded hex color SHALL be reported as a warning

scenarios.name: Dark mode coverage check
scenarios.given: a generated page does NOT contain `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors
scenarios.then: a warning SHALL be reported for that page

scenarios.name: SEO tag presence check
scenarios.then: the system SHALL verify `<title>`, meta description, `<h1>`, and OG tags exist

scenarios.name: Data consistency check
scenarios.then: the system SHALL check for stale phone numbers and locations

requirements.name: Validation Exit Code
requirements.description: The system SHALL exit with a non-zero code when validation errors are present, and the post-build hook SHALL propagate this exit code. - **GIVEN** validation finds one or more errors - **WHEN** `npm run validate` completes - **THEN** the process SHALL exit with code 1 - **GIVEN** one or more E2E tests fail - **WHEN** `npm test` completes - **THEN** the process SHALL exit with code 1 - **GIVEN** validation finds only warnings and no errors - **WHEN** `npm run validate` completes - **THEN** the process SHALL exit with code 0 - **GIVEN** validation exits with code 1 (errors found) - **WHEN** the post-build hook runs - **THEN** the build SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Errors cause failure
scenarios.given: validation finds one or more errors
scenarios.then: the process SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Test failures cause CI failure
scenarios.given: one or more E2E tests fail
scenarios.then: the process SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Warnings-only success
scenarios.given: validation finds only warnings and no errors
scenarios.then: the process SHALL exit with code 0

scenarios.name: Post-build propagates validate exit code
scenarios.then: the build SHALL exit with code 1

requirements.name: Clean URLs
requirements.description: The system SHALL produce clean, directory-based URLs without `.html` extensions. - **GIVEN** a page mapped to `features/index.html` in the page map - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the output SHALL be written to `dist/features/index.html` - **AND** the URL path SHALL be `/features/`

scenarios.name: Directory-based output
scenarios.given: a page mapped to `features/index.html` in the page map
scenarios.then: the output SHALL be written to `dist/features/index.html`

requirements.name: CI/CD Pipeline
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on pull requests and pushes to main. - **GIVEN** a pull request is opened against `main` - **WHEN** the CI workflow triggers - **THEN** the build SHALL run via `npm run build` - **AND** validation SHALL run via `npm run validate` - **AND** lint checks SHALL run via `npm run lint:all` - **AND** E2E tests SHALL run via `npm test` - **AND** all steps SHALL complete within 10 minutes - **GIVEN** the project is configured for deployment - **WHEN** `npm run deploy:netlify` is executed - **THEN** the site SHALL be deployed to Netlify - **WHEN** `npm run deploy:cloudflare` is executed - **THEN** the site SHALL be deployed to Cloudflare Pages

scenarios.name: PR validation
scenarios.given: a pull request is opened against `main`
scenarios.then: the build SHALL run via `npm run build`

scenarios.name: Deploy scripts
scenarios.given: the project is configured for deployment
scenarios.when: `npm run deploy:netlify` is executed `npm run deploy:cloudflare` is executed
scenarios.then: the site SHALL be deployed to Netlify the site SHALL be deployed to Cloudflare Pages

requirements.name: Pipeline Configuration
requirements.description: The system SHALL have a deploy step in the build pipeline config that invokes the actual deploy script. - **GIVEN** the build pipeline config at `orchestrate/pipeline-config/build.yaml` - **WHEN** the deploy step is executed - **THEN** it SHALL run `npm run deploy` - **AND** it SHALL NOT be a placeholder echo command

scenarios.name: Pipeline deploy step
scenarios.then: it SHALL run `npm run deploy`

requirements.name: Development Server
requirements.description: The system SHALL support `npm run dev` for local development (build + serve). - **GIVEN** the project source files - **WHEN** `npm run dev` is executed - **THEN** the build SHALL run first - **AND** a local HTTP server SHALL serve the `dist/` directory

scenarios.name: Dev workflow
scenarios.then: the build SHALL run first

requirements.name: Test Command
requirements.description: The system SHALL include an `npm test` script that runs the full Playwright E2E test suite. - **GIVEN** the project has test files in `tests/e2e/` - **WHEN** `npm test` is executed - **THEN** Playwright SHALL run all test specs across configured browser projects - **AND** the dev server SHALL be started automatically via `webServer` config

scenarios.name: Test command execution
scenarios.given: the project has test files in `tests/e2e/`
scenarios.then: Playwright SHALL run all test specs across configured browser projects

requirements.name: Post-Merge Hook
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a post-merge hook that runs after `git merge` or `git pull` to detect dependency drift and warn of rebuild-requiring changes. - **WHEN** a merge changes `package-lock.json` - **THEN** `npm install` SHALL run automatically via the post-merge hook - **WHEN** a merge changes `site.json` or `assets/css/core.css` - **THEN** the hook SHALL warn the user to run `npm run build`

scenarios.name: Package lockfile change triggers install
scenarios.then: `npm install` SHALL run automatically via the post-merge hook

scenarios.name: Site data change warns about rebuild
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL warn the user to run `npm run build`

requirements.name: Pre-Push Hook
requirements.description: The system SHALL include a pre-push hook that runs lint and validate checks before allowing a `git push`. - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and linting returns errors - **THEN** the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1 - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and validation returns errors - **THEN** the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1 - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and both lint and validate pass - **THEN** the push SHALL proceed with exit code 0

scenarios.name: Push blocked on lint failure
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and linting returns errors
scenarios.then: the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1

scenarios.name: Push blocked on validate failure
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and validation returns errors
scenarios.then: the push SHALL be blocked with exit code 1

scenarios.name: Clean push proceeds
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and both lint and validate pass
scenarios.then: the push SHALL proceed with exit code 0

requirements.name: Preconnect Resource Hints Injection
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject `<link rel="preconnect">` hints into every assembled page's `<head>` for Google Fonts and Plausible analytics. - **WHEN** a page is assembled - **THEN** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be present in `<head>` - **AND** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>` SHALL be present - **AND** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://plausible.io">` SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Preconnect hints present
scenarios.then: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be present in `<head>`

requirements.name: GitHub Actions CI Workflow
