requirements.description: The system SHALL include a pre-push hook at `hooks/pre-push.mjs` that runs lint and validate checks before allowing a push to proceed. - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and `npm run lint:all && npm run validate` both pass - **THEN** the push SHALL proceed - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and linting finds errors - **THEN** the hook SHALL exit with code 1 - **AND** the push SHALL be blocked - **WHEN** `git push` is executed and validation finds errors - **THEN** the hook SHALL exit with code 1 - **AND** the push SHALL be blocked

scenarios.name: Clean lint and validate allows push
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and `npm run lint:all && npm run validate` both pass
scenarios.then: the push SHALL proceed

scenarios.name: Lint failure blocks push
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and linting finds errors
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL exit with code 1

scenarios.name: Validate failure blocks push
scenarios.when: `git push` is executed and validation finds errors
scenarios.then: the hook SHALL exit with code 1

requirements.name: Hook lifecycle integration
requirements.description: The orchestration engine's `hook-engine.mjs` SHALL include post-merge and pre-push events in the lifecycle phase mappings. - **WHEN** the orchestration engine runs the `apply` phase - **THEN** the lifecycle SHALL include `post-merge` in addition to existing `pre-build`, `post-build`, and `theme-change` - **WHEN** `executeLifecycle` processes global hooks - **THEN** the `globalResults` array SHALL contain results in deterministic order matching project hooks behavior

scenarios.name: Apply phase includes post-merge
scenarios.when: the orchestration engine runs the `apply` phase
scenarios.then: the lifecycle SHALL include `post-merge` in addition to existing `pre-build`, `post-build`, and `theme-change`

scenarios.name: globalResults ordering is fixed
scenarios.then: the `globalResults` array SHALL contain results in deterministic order matching project hooks behavior

requirements.name: Shared Form Submission
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `submitForm()` function in `core.js` that handles form submission with a state machine (idle → submitting → success → error → idle). - **WHEN** a user submits a validated form with a configured endpoint - **THEN** the form SHALL enter `submitting` state showing a loading indicator - **AND** the form data SHALL be POSTed to the endpoint - **AND** a honey-pot field SHALL be injected if not present - **AND** on 2xx response, if the response body contains `success: true`, the form SHALL transition to `success` state - **AND** if the response body contains `success: false`, the form SHALL transition to `error` state with the API error message - **WHEN** a form submission fails (network error or non-2xx response) - **THEN** the form SHALL transition to `error` state showing the error message - **AND** a retry button SHALL be displayed - **AND** clicking retry SHALL re-enter `submitting` state and re-POST - **WHEN** a form is in `error` state and the user clicks retry - **THEN** the form SHALL re-submit - **AND** on failure, the form SHALL return to `error` state with the new error message

scenarios.name: Successful submission
scenarios.when: a user submits a validated form with a configured endpoint
scenarios.then: the form SHALL enter `submitting` state showing a loading indicator

scenarios.name: Submission error
scenarios.when: a form submission fails (network error or non-2xx response)
scenarios.then: the form SHALL transition to `error` state showing the error message

scenarios.name: Form returns to idle on retry
scenarios.when: a form is in `error` state and the user clicks retry
scenarios.then: the form SHALL re-submit

requirements.name: Endpoint Configuration
requirements.description: Forms SHALL configure their submission target via `data-endpoint` attribute on the `<form>` element. The `data-netlify` attribute provides a Netlify Forms fallback. - **WHEN** a form has `data-netlify="true"` attribute - **THEN** the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` - **AND** `form-name` SHALL be sent as a hidden field with the form's `name` attribute value - **AND** the honey-pot field value SHALL be included in the payload - **WHEN** a form has `data-endpoint` set to a URL (not Netlify Forms) - **THEN** the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/json` - **AND** form data SHALL be sent as a JSON object with `formName`, `fields`, `honeyPot`, and `utm` keys - **WHEN** a form has no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` attribute - **THEN** the submission SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with JSON content type - **AND** the form SHALL NOT log a warning or show an error

scenarios.name: Netlify Forms endpoint
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`

scenarios.name: JSON API endpoint
scenarios.when: a form has `data-endpoint` set to a URL (not Netlify Forms)
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL use `Content-Type: application/json`

scenarios.name: Missing endpoint defaults to API
scenarios.when: a form has no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` attribute
scenarios.then: the submission SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with JSON content type

requirements.name: UTM Parameter Capture
requirements.description: The system SHALL capture UTM parameters (`utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`, `utm_content`, `utm_term`) from the current URL and include them in form submission payloads. - **WHEN** the URL contains `?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=launch` - **THEN** the form payload SHALL include `utm_source: "linkedin"`, `utm_medium: "cpc"`, `utm_campaign: "launch"` - **WHEN** the URL contains no UTM parameters - **THEN** the form payload SHALL NOT include any `utm_*` fields

scenarios.name: UTM params present
scenarios.then: the form payload SHALL include `utm_source: "linkedin"`, `utm_medium: "cpc"`, `utm_campaign: "launch"`

scenarios.name: No UTM params
scenarios.when: the URL contains no UTM parameters
scenarios.then: the form payload SHALL NOT include any `utm_*` fields

requirements.name: Form State Styling
requirements.description: The system SHALL provide CSS classes for form submission states applied to the form element. - **WHEN** a form has class `form-submitting` - **THEN** the submit button SHALL be disabled - **AND** the submit button SHALL display a loading indicator (CSS spinner) - **AND** all form inputs SHALL be disabled - **WHEN** a form has class `form-success` - **THEN** form fields SHALL be hidden - **AND** a success message SHALL be displayed in their place - **WHEN** a form has class `form-error` - **THEN** the error message SHALL be displayed above the submit button - **AND** a retry button SHALL be shown

scenarios.name: Submitting state
scenarios.then: the submit button SHALL be disabled

scenarios.name: Success state
scenarios.then: form fields SHALL be hidden

scenarios.name: Error state
scenarios.then: the error message SHALL be displayed above the submit button

requirements.name: Honey-Pot Spam Detection
requirements.description: All forms using `submitForm()` SHALL include an invisible honey-pot field that bots auto-fill but humans never see or interact with. - **WHEN** a form with `data-endpoint` or `data-netlify` is submitted - **THEN** `submitForm()` SHALL append a hidden `<input>` with name `_hp`, `tabindex="-1"`, and `autocomplete="off"` if one does not already exist - **AND** the honey-pot field SHALL be visually hidden via CSS (`position: absolute; left: -9999px`) - **WHEN** a form already contains `<input name="_hp" ...>` in the HTML source - **THEN** `submitForm()` SHALL NOT inject a duplicate honey-pot field

scenarios.name: Honey-pot field injected
scenarios.when: a form with `data-endpoint` or `data-netlify` is submitted
scenarios.then: `submitForm()` SHALL append a hidden `<input>` with name `_hp`, `tabindex="-1"`, and `autocomplete="off"` if one does not already exist

scenarios.name: Honey-pot field present in source
scenarios.when: a form already contains `<input name="_hp" ...>` in the HTML source
scenarios.then: `submitForm()` SHALL NOT inject a duplicate honey-pot field

requirements.name: Default API Endpoint
requirements.description: When no `data-endpoint` and no `data-netlify` attribute are present, `submitForm()` SHALL default to POSTing to `/api/form-submit`. - **WHEN** a form has neither `data-endpoint` nor `data-netlify` - **THEN** `submitForm()` SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with `Content-Type: application/json` - **AND** the payload SHALL include `formName` (from the form's `name` attribute), `fields` (object of all named inputs), `honeyPot` (value of `_hp` field), and `utm` (captured UTM params)

scenarios.name: Default endpoint used
scenarios.then: `submitForm()` SHALL POST to `/api/form-submit` with `Content-Type: application/json`

requirements.name: Privacy Policy Page
requirements.description: The system SHALL render a Privacy Policy page at `/privacy-policy/` with a hero section, sticky side navigation, and 8 content sections presented as bordered content cards. - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/privacy-policy.html` source file - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** `dist/privacy-policy/index.html` SHALL be generated - **AND** the page SHALL contain a `<title>` matching the `site.json` entry - **AND** the page SHALL contain the shared navigation and footer includes - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a hero section SHALL display an "LEGAL & COMPLIANCE" eyebrow, the page title "Privacy Policy", a lead paragraph, and version/effective/updated metadata - **AND** the hero SHALL have a centered layout with a radial gradient background effect - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** 9 content sections SHALL be present: Introduction, Information Collection, Data Usage, Data Storage, Cookies, Third-Party Services, Your Rights, Security, Contact - **AND** each section SHALL have a unique `id` attribute for side navigation linking - **AND** each section SHALL contain an inline SVG icon in the heading

scenarios.name: Page assembles correctly
scenarios.then: `dist/privacy-policy/index.html` SHALL be generated

scenarios.name: Hero section renders
scenarios.then: a hero section SHALL display an "LEGAL & COMPLIANCE" eyebrow, the page title "Privacy Policy", a lead paragraph, and version/effective/updated metadata

scenarios.name: Content sections render
scenarios.then: 9 content sections SHALL be present: Introduction, Information Collection, Data Usage, Data Storage, Cookies, Third-Party Services, Your Rights, Security, Contact

requirements.name: Side Navigation
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL include a sticky side navigation that highlights the currently visible section based on scroll position. - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy or terms page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<aside class="side-nav">` element SHALL be present - **AND** it SHALL contain links to all content sections on the page - **AND** it SHALL have a "On this page" header in monospace uppercase - **GIVEN** the side navigation is rendered - **WHEN** the user scrolls to a content section - **THEN** the corresponding side nav link SHALL have class `active` - **AND** only one link SHALL be active at any time - **AND** the active link SHALL use `--accent-soft` background - **GIVEN** the side navigation is rendered - **WHEN** the page scrolls - **THEN** the side nav SHALL remain visible at `top: calc(var(--nav-h) + 24px)` - **GIVEN** the viewport is 1024px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the side nav SHALL be hidden via `display: none`

scenarios.name: Side nav renders
scenarios.given: the assembled privacy-policy or terms page
scenarios.then: a `<aside class="side-nav">` element SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Active section tracking
scenarios.when: the user scrolls to a content section
scenarios.then: the corresponding side nav link SHALL have class `active`

scenarios.name: Sticky positioning
scenarios.then: the side nav SHALL remain visible at `top: calc(var(--nav-h) + 24px)`

scenarios.name: Hidden on tablet
scenarios.then: the side nav SHALL be hidden via `display: none`

requirements.name: Reading Progress Bar
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL display a fixed reading progress bar at the top of the viewport. - **GIVEN** the assembled privacy-policy or terms page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<div class="progress-bar">` SHALL be present with `position: fixed` and `z-index: 101` - **AND** the bar SHALL have a gradient background from `--accent` to `--teal` - **GIVEN** the page is scrollable - **WHEN** the user scrolls - **THEN** the progress bar width SHALL be `(scrollTop / scrollableHeight) * 100` percent - **AND** the scroll listener SHALL use `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Progress bar renders
scenarios.given: the assembled privacy-policy or terms page
scenarios.then: a `<div class="progress-bar">` SHALL be present with `position: fixed` and `z-index: 101`

scenarios.name: Progress updates on scroll
scenarios.then: the progress bar width SHALL be `(scrollTop / scrollableHeight) * 100` percent

requirements.name: Content Cards
requirements.description: Legal page sections SHALL be rendered as content cards with border, background surface, and consistent internal spacing. - **GIVEN** any content section on a legal page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the section SHALL have class `content-card` with background `var(--surface)`, border `var(--border)`, and border-radius `var(--radius-lg)` - **AND** the heading SHALL have a bottom border separating it from the body text - **AND** paragraph text SHALL use `line-height: 1.7` - **GIVEN** a content card containing informational sub-sections - **WHEN** the card renders - **THEN** `.info-block` elements SHALL render with a subtle background and border - **AND** each info block SHALL have a bold heading and muted body text

scenarios.name: Card styling
scenarios.given: any content section on a legal page
scenarios.then: the section SHALL have class `content-card` with background `var(--surface)`, border `var(--border)`, and border-radius `var(--radius-lg)`

scenarios.name: Info blocks within cards
scenarios.given: a content card containing informational sub-sections
scenarios.then: `.info-block` elements SHALL render with a subtle background and border

requirements.name: Cookie Classification Grid
requirements.description: The Privacy Policy page SHALL classify cookies used by the Platform in a 4-column grid of cards. - **GIVEN** the Cookies section of the privacy policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.cookie-grid` SHALL display exactly 4 `.cookie-card` items: Essential, Analytics, Performance, Preferences - **AND** each card SHALL contain an SVG icon, heading, and description

scenarios.name: Four cookie types displayed
scenarios.given: the Cookies section of the privacy policy page
scenarios.then: a `.cookie-grid` SHALL display exactly 4 `.cookie-card` items: Essential, Analytics, Performance, Preferences

requirements.name: User Rights Grid
requirements.description: The Privacy Policy page SHALL display 6 user data rights as a grid of cards. - **GIVEN** the Your Rights section of the privacy policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.rights-grid` SHALL display exactly 6 cards: Access your data, Correct or update, Request deletion, Export your data, Withdraw consent, Communication preferences - **AND** each card SHALL have a green SVG icon (`--green`), heading, and description

scenarios.name: Six rights displayed
scenarios.given: the Your Rights section of the privacy policy page
scenarios.then: a `.rights-grid` SHALL display exactly 6 cards: Access your data, Correct or update, Request deletion, Export your data, Withdraw consent, Communication preferences

requirements.name: Security Features Grid
requirements.description: The Privacy Policy page SHALL display 6 security features as a grid of cards. - **GIVEN** the Security section of the privacy policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.security-grid` SHALL display exactly 6 cards: Role-based access, Encryption, Audit logs, Monitoring, Regular backups, Access restrictions - **AND** each card SHALL have an accent SVG icon (`--accent`), heading, and description

scenarios.name: Six security features displayed
scenarios.given: the Security section of the privacy policy page
scenarios.then: a `.security-grid` SHALL display exactly 6 cards: Role-based access, Encryption, Audit logs, Monitoring, Regular backups, Access restrictions

requirements.name: Contact Section
requirements.description: The Privacy Policy page SHALL include a contact section with email and contact form links for privacy-related inquiries. - **GIVEN** the Contact section of the privacy policy page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `.contact-card` grid SHALL display 3 `.contact-item` elements: Privacy email, Support email, Contact form - **AND** email addresses SHALL use `{{EMAIL}}` placeholder resolved from `site.json` - **AND** the contact form link SHALL point to `/contact/`

scenarios.name: Contact methods displayed
scenarios.given: the Contact section of the privacy policy page
scenarios.then: a `.contact-card` grid SHALL display 3 `.contact-item` elements: Privacy email, Support email, Contact form

requirements.name: Company Data Placeholders
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL use `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` variables for all company information, resolved at build time from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** the privacy-policy and terms page source files - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** no hardcoded phone numbers, email addresses, company names, or years SHALL be present - **AND** all company data SHALL use `{{PHONE}}`, `{{EMAIL}}`, `{{COMPANY}}`, `{{YEAR}}`, `{{LOCATION}}`, etc.

scenarios.name: No hardcoded company data
scenarios.given: the privacy-policy and terms page source files
scenarios.then: no hardcoded phone numbers, email addresses, company names, or years SHALL be present

requirements.name: Print Styles
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL include print-specific CSS to produce clean printed output. - **GIVEN** the page is printed via `@media print` - **WHEN** the print stylesheet applies - **THEN** navigation, mobile drawer, hamburger, progress bar, side nav, footer, breadcrumbs, and skip link SHALL be hidden - **AND** content cards SHALL render without borders or shadows - **AND** links SHALL render in black with underline - **AND** paragraph and list items SHALL enforce orphans:3 and widows:3

scenarios.name: Print hides interactive elements
scenarios.given: the page is printed via `@media print`
scenarios.then: navigation, mobile drawer, hamburger, progress bar, side nav, footer, breadcrumbs, and skip link SHALL be hidden

requirements.name: Dark Mode Support
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL render correctly in both light and dark themes. - **GIVEN** the page's `<style>` block - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.content-card`, `.side-nav a`, `.info-block`, `.hero-section::before`, `.rights-grid .card` - **AND** all color values in dark mode rules SHALL use `color-mix()`, `var()`, or CSS custom properties

scenarios.name: Dark mode overrides
scenarios.then: `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for: `.content-card`, `.side-nav a`, `.info-block`, `.hero-section::before`, `.rights-grid .card`

requirements.name: Responsive Layout
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL adapt layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile viewports. - **GIVEN** viewport is 1024px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the content layout SHALL collapse to single column - **AND** the side nav SHALL be hidden - **AND** cookie, rights, and security grids SHALL display 2 columns - **GIVEN** viewport is 768px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** content cards SHALL reduce padding to 28px - **AND** cookie, rights, security grids SHALL collapse to single column - **AND** data flow arrows SHALL rotate 90 degrees to vertical - **GIVEN** viewport is 480px or narrower - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** hero metadata SHALL stack vertically - **AND** content cards SHALL reduce padding to 20px 16px - **AND** headings SHALL reduce font size - **AND** the sticky CTA SHALL expand to full width

scenarios.name: Tablet layout (≤1024px)
scenarios.then: the content layout SHALL collapse to single column

scenarios.name: Mobile layout (≤768px)
scenarios.then: content cards SHALL reduce padding to 28px

scenarios.name: Small mobile (≤480px)
scenarios.then: hero metadata SHALL stack vertically

requirements.name: Shared Component Compliance
requirements.description: Legal pages SHALL use the project's shared components and SHALL NOT duplicate shared logic. - **GIVEN** the privacy-policy or terms page has a form (if applicable) - **WHEN** the page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no duplicate `fetch()`, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL exist for form submission - **GIVEN** the privacy-policy page scripts - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** no duplicate sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **AND** scroll event listeners SHALL use `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Uses shared form logic
scenarios.given: the privacy-policy or terms page has a form (if applicable)
scenarios.then: no duplicate `fetch()`, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL exist for form submission

scenarios.name: Uses shared scroll/animation logic
scenarios.then: no duplicate sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

requirements.name: Page Inventory
requirements.description: The system SHALL render exactly 14 marketing pages as defined in the `site.json` pages array. The FAQ page SHALL now include interactive self-service components beyond its original static Q&A format. The demo booking and contact pages SHALL use the shared `submitForm()` function from core.js for real form submission instead of `alert()`. - **GIVEN** `site.json` contains entries for Home, Problem-Solutions, Product-Overview, Features, Feature-Showcase, Why-OptiFlow, Pricing, Newsletter, FAQ, Contact, Demo-Booking, Privacy-Policy, Terms, and Competitive-Positioning - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** all 14 pages SHALL be generated in `dist/` with corresponding directory structures - **AND** only pages defined in `site.json` SHALL be generated - **AND** the FAQ page SHALL include self-service toolbar, feedback buttons, troubleshooting widget, and escalation section - **GIVEN** the assembled demo-booking page - **WHEN** a user fills and submits the booking form - **THEN** the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()` - **AND** the form SHALL display submitting/success/error states - **AND** the calendar slot data SHALL be included in the payload - **GIVEN** the assembled contact page - **WHEN** a user fills and submits the contact form - **THEN** the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()` - **AND** the form SHALL display submitting/success/error states

scenarios.name: All pages present
scenarios.given: `site.json` contains entries for Home, Problem-Solutions, Product-Overview, Features, Feature-Showcase, Why-OptiFlow, Pricing, Newsletter, FAQ, Contact, Demo-Booking, Privacy-Policy, Terms, and Competitive-Positioning
scenarios.then: all 14 pages SHALL be generated in `dist/` with corresponding directory structures

scenarios.name: Demo booking form submits to endpoint
scenarios.when: a user fills and submits the booking form
scenarios.then: the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()`

scenarios.name: Contact form submits to endpoint
scenarios.when: a user fills and submits the contact form
scenarios.then: the form data SHALL be posted to the configured endpoint via core.js `submitForm()`

requirements.name: Unique Metadata
requirements.description: Each page SHALL have a unique `<title>`, `<meta name="description">`, OpenGraph tags, and canonical URL. All SEO metadata in page source files SHALL use build-time placeholders that resolve from `site.json`. - **GIVEN** any page source file in `src/pages/` - **WHEN** the file is inspected - **THEN** the `<title>` tag SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` as its content - **AND** the title SHALL NOT be hardcoded in the source file - **GIVEN** any page source file in `src/pages/` - **WHEN** the file is inspected - **THEN** the `<meta name="description">` tag SHALL use `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` as its content attribute - **AND** the `<meta property="og:description">` tag SHALL use `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` as its content attribute - **AND** the description SHALL NOT be hardcoded in the source file - **GIVEN** a page entry in `site.json` with `title` and `description` fields - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** the `<title>` tag SHALL match the page's `title` value - **AND** the `<meta name="description">` SHALL match the page's `description` value (120-160 characters) - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL contain `og:title` and `og:description` meta tags - **AND** it SHALL contain `og:image` pointing to the OptiFlow logo - **AND** it SHALL contain `og:type` set to "website" - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL contain a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag

scenarios.name: Title uses placeholder
scenarios.then: the `<title>` tag SHALL contain `{{PAGE_TITLE}}` as its content

scenarios.name: Description uses placeholder
scenarios.then: the `<meta name="description">` tag SHALL use `{{PAGE_DESCRIPTION}}` as its content attribute

scenarios.name: Title and description
scenarios.given: a page entry in `site.json` with `title` and `description` fields
scenarios.then: the `<title>` tag SHALL match the page's `title` value

scenarios.name: OpenGraph tags
scenarios.then: it SHALL contain `og:title` and `og:description` meta tags

scenarios.name: Twitter card
scenarios.then: it SHALL contain a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag

requirements.name: Sticky Navigation
requirements.description: Each page SHALL include a sticky navigation bar with glass effect. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a fixed-position `<header class="topnav">` SHALL be present - **AND** it SHALL contain the OptiFlow logo, desktop nav links, theme toggle, and CTA buttons - **AND** the nav SHALL acquire a glass blur background on scroll past 20px

scenarios.name: Navigation bar present
scenarios.then: a fixed-position `<header class="topnav">` SHALL be present

requirements.name: Shared Footer
requirements.description: Each page SHALL include a shared footer with company info, product links, and contact details. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** a `<footer class="pagefoot">` SHALL be present - **AND** it SHALL contain branded columns (Product, Solutions, Resources, Contact) - **AND** it SHALL display company info: phone, email, and location from `site.json`

scenarios.name: Footer present

requirements.name: Scroll-Reveal Animations
requirements.description: Each page SHALL use scroll-reveal animations for content sections. - **GIVEN** page sections marked with `.reveal` class - **WHEN** a section scrolls into the viewport - **THEN** it SHALL transition from translateY(28px) and opacity 0 to visible state - **AND** staggered children SHALL animate sequentially via `--delay` values

scenarios.name: Reveal on scroll
scenarios.when: a section scrolls into the viewport
scenarios.then: it SHALL transition from translateY(28px) and opacity 0 to visible state

requirements.name: Source File Naming
requirements.description: Source page files SHALL be named to match their purpose. The newsletter page source SHALL be `newsletter.html` (not `blog.html`). The feature showcase page source SHALL be `feature-showcase.html`. - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/` directory - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `feature-showcase.html` SHALL exist - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/` directory - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `newsletter.html` SHALL exist - **AND** `blog.html` SHALL NOT exist - **GIVEN** the SRC_MAP in `assemble.mjs` - **WHEN** the newsletter entry is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL map `newsletter/index.html` → `newsletter.html` - **GIVEN** the SRC_MAP in `assemble.mjs` - **WHEN** the feature showcase entry is inspected - **THEN** it SHALL map `feature-showcase/index.html` → `feature-showcase.html`

scenarios.name: Feature Showcase source file
scenarios.then: `feature-showcase.html` SHALL exist

scenarios.name: Newsletter source file
scenarios.then: `newsletter.html` SHALL exist

scenarios.name: Build mapping for newsletter
scenarios.then: it SHALL map `newsletter/index.html` → `newsletter.html`

scenarios.name: Build mapping for feature showcase
scenarios.when: the feature showcase entry is inspected
scenarios.then: it SHALL map `feature-showcase/index.html` → `feature-showcase.html`

requirements.name: Page Structure
requirements.description: The system SHALL maintain a consistent page structure on every page. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** the content SHALL follow the order: navigation (`<header>`) → main content (`<main>`) → footer (`<footer>`) - **AND** a skip-to-content link SHALL precede the navigation - **AND** the main content area SHALL have `id="content"`

scenarios.name: Structural ordering
scenarios.then: the content SHALL follow the order: navigation (`<header>`) → main content (`<main>`) → footer (`<footer>`)

requirements.name: Shared Logic Deduplication
requirements.description: Page-specific scripts SHALL NOT duplicate logic already handled by `core.js`. Sticky CTA visibility, scroll-to-top, scroll reveal, stagger animation, FAQ accordion, counter animation, AND form submission SHALL be handled exclusively by core.js. Page-specific CSS SHALL NOT redefine stagger animation keyframes or transition rules already provided by core.css `.stagger-3`/`.stagger-4`/`.stagger-5` utility classes. - **GIVEN** the newsletter page source at `src/pages/newsletter.html` - **WHEN** the `<style>` block is inspected - **THEN** no `.stagger > *` or `.stagger.visible > *:nth-child()` animation rules SHALL be present - **AND** all stagger animations SHALL use core.css `.stagger-N` utility classes - **GIVEN** the home, pricing, privacy-policy, and terms pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **GIVEN** the home and pricing pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no scroll-to-top scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **GIVEN** the product-overview and newsletter pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no reveal or stagger IntersectionObserver SHALL be present (core.js handles it) - **GIVEN** the demo-booking and contact pages - **WHEN** page-specific scripts are inspected - **THEN** no `fetch()` calls, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL be present for form submission - **AND** form submission SHALL be handled exclusively by core.js `submitForm()`

scenarios.name: No duplicate stagger CSS in newsletter page
scenarios.then: no `.stagger > *` or `.stagger.visible > *:nth-child()` animation rules SHALL be present

scenarios.name: No duplicate sticky CTA
scenarios.given: the home, pricing, privacy-policy, and terms pages
scenarios.then: no sticky CTA scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

scenarios.name: No duplicate scroll-to-top
scenarios.then: no scroll-to-top scroll listener SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

scenarios.name: No duplicate reveal or stagger
scenarios.then: no reveal or stagger IntersectionObserver SHALL be present (core.js handles it)

scenarios.name: No duplicate form submission logic
scenarios.then: no `fetch()` calls, `XMLHttpRequest`, or form `onsubmit` handlers SHALL be present for form submission

requirements.name: Performance Standards
requirements.description: All page-specific scroll listeners SHALL use `{passive: true}`. Mousemove listeners SHALL be throttled via `requestAnimationFrame`. Animations SHALL pause when the page is hidden. - **GIVEN** any page-specific scroll listener - **WHEN** the listener is registered - **THEN** the options object SHALL include `{passive: true}` - **GIVEN** mousemove listeners on home or why-optiflow pages - **WHEN** the mouse moves rapidly - **THEN** the callback SHALL fire at most once per animation frame

scenarios.name: Scroll listeners are passive
scenarios.then: the options object SHALL include `{passive: true}`

scenarios.name: Mousemove listeners are throttled
scenarios.given: mousemove listeners on home or why-optiflow pages
scenarios.then: the callback SHALL fire at most once per animation frame

requirements.name: Problem & Solutions Narrative Page
requirements.description: The system SHALL render a Problem & Solutions page with a complete narrative flow: problem diagnosis → cost quantification → solution introduction → transformation proof. - **GIVEN** the `src/pages/problem-solutions.html` source file - **WHEN** `npm run build` assembles the page - **THEN** `dist/problem-solutions/index.html` SHALL contain exactly 10 `<section>` elements labeled via `data-screen-label` attributes: 01 Hero, 02 Pain Points, 02B Chaos Map, 03 Trust, 04 Why Growth Feels Hard, 05 WhatsApp & Excel, 06 The Cost of Doing Nothing, 07 The Solution, 08 People vs Process, 09 Before & After, 10 CTA - **GIVEN** the assembled problem-solutions page - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `<header class="topnav">` navigation SHALL be present with Solutions marked as the active nav link - **AND** the `<footer class="pagefoot">` SHALL be present with company info, product links, and contact details - **GIVEN** the `site.json` entry for `problem-solutions/index.html` with `title` and `description` fields - **WHEN** the page is assembled - **THEN** `<title>` SHALL match the entry's title value - **AND** `<meta name="description">` SHALL match the entry's description value

scenarios.name: All 10 sections render
scenarios.then: `dist/problem-solutions/index.html` SHALL contain exactly 10 `<section>` elements labeled via `data-screen-label` attributes: 01 Hero, 02 Pain Points, 02B Chaos Map, 03 Trust, 04 Why Growth Feels Hard, 05 WhatsApp & Excel, 06 The Cost of Doing Nothing, 07 The Solution, 08 People vs Process, 09 Before & After, 10 CTA

scenarios.name: Navigation and footer included
scenarios.then: the `<header class="topnav">` navigation SHALL be present with Solutions marked as the active nav link

scenarios.name: SEO metadata from site.json
scenarios.given: the `site.json` entry for `problem-solutions/index.html` with `title` and `description` fields
scenarios.then: `<title>` SHALL match the entry's title value

requirements.name: Pain-Point Rotator
requirements.description: The page SHALL include an auto-cycling carousel of pain-point statements that rotates every 3 seconds and pauses when the page is hidden. - **GIVEN** the pain-points section with 8 `.pp-item` elements - **WHEN** the page loads - **THEN** exactly one `.pp-item` SHALL have class `active` at any time - **AND** the active item SHALL cycle to the next every `3000ms` - **GIVEN** the pain-point rotator interval is running - **WHEN** the browser tab is hidden (`visibilitychange` event fires with `document.hidden === true`) - **THEN** the rotator SHALL skip the current cycle (no class change occurs) - **AND** when the tab becomes visible again, the rotator SHALL resume normally - **GIVEN** the user has `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` - **WHEN** the pain-points section renders - **THEN** all `.pp-item` elements SHALL be visible (opacity 1, no animation) - **AND** only the element with class `active` SHALL be displayed; others SHALL have `display: none`

scenarios.name: Carousel cycles on timer
scenarios.then: exactly one `.pp-item` SHALL have class `active` at any time

scenarios.name: Pauses when page hidden
scenarios.given: the pain-point rotator interval is running
scenarios.when: the browser tab is hidden (`visibilitychange` event fires with `document.hidden === true`)
scenarios.then: the rotator SHALL skip the current cycle (no class change occurs)

scenarios.name: Reduced motion disables carousel
scenarios.then: all `.pp-item` elements SHALL be visible (opacity 1, no animation)

requirements.name: Operational Chaos Map
requirements.description: The page SHALL include a visual flow diagram showing operational chaos sources funneling into OptiFlow OS as the single source of truth. - **GIVEN** the chaos map section (data-screen-label="02B Chaos Map") - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `.chaos-sources` grid SHALL contain exactly 6 `.chaos-src` items: WhatsApp, Excel Sheets, Phone Calls, Manual Registers, Verbal Instructions, Sticky Notes - **AND** each source SHALL animate with the `floatChaos` keyframe - **GIVEN** the chaos map flow - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the flow SHALL visually connect sources → CHAOS state → OptiFlow OS node - **AND** the resolve tag SHALL display "OptiFlow OS" with subtitle "Single Source of Truth"

scenarios.name: Chaos map renders 6 sources
scenarios.given: the chaos map section (data-screen-label="02B Chaos Map")
scenarios.then: the `.chaos-sources` grid SHALL contain exactly 6 `.chaos-src` items: WhatsApp, Excel Sheets, Phone Calls, Manual Registers, Verbal Instructions, Sticky Notes

scenarios.name: Chaos resolves to OptiFlow OS
scenarios.then: the flow SHALL visually connect sources → CHAOS state → OptiFlow OS node

requirements.name: Industry-Specific Problem Cards
requirements.description: The page SHALL display 6 problem scenario cards across textile, manufacturing, trading, and logistics verticals with quantified ₹ impact. - **GIVEN** the section labeled "04 Why Growth Feels Hard" - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `.grid-3` SHALL contain exactly 6 `.card` elements - **AND** cards SHALL cover Textile (2 cards), Manufacturing (2 cards), Trading (1 card), and Logistics (1 card) - **GIVEN** any problem card in the section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the card SHALL contain an `.impact-tag` with a specific ₹ amount or operational cost - **AND** the card SHALL contain a `.roi-line` describing how OptiFlow OS fixes the problem

scenarios.name: Cards cover 4 industries
scenarios.given: the section labeled "04 Why Growth Feels Hard"
scenarios.then: the `.grid-3` SHALL contain exactly 6 `.card` elements

scenarios.name: Each card shows ₹ impact
scenarios.given: any problem card in the section
scenarios.then: the card SHALL contain an `.impact-tag` with a specific ₹ amount or operational cost

requirements.name: WhatsApp Chat Mockup
requirements.description: The page SHALL include a simulated WhatsApp operations group chat mockup to illustrate chat-based chaos. - **GIVEN** the WhatsApp & Excel section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `.chat-mock` SHALL display at least 5 chat messages from multiple senders (Owner, Supervisor, QC Manager, HR, Finance) - **AND** messages SHALL alternate between `.sent` and `.received` styles - **GIVEN** the chat mockup is rendered - **WHEN** the page is viewed - **THEN** 3 cost-of-chaos cards SHALL appear alongside the mockup: Tasks Get Lost, No Audit Trail, Zero Visibility - **AND** each cost card SHALL contain an `.impact-tag`

scenarios.name: Chat mockup shows realistic messages
scenarios.then: the `.chat-mock` SHALL display at least 5 chat messages from multiple senders (Owner, Supervisor, QC Manager, HR, Finance)

scenarios.name: Cost analysis accompanies mockup
scenarios.then: 3 cost-of-chaos cards SHALL appear alongside the mockup: Tasks Get Lost, No Audit Trail, Zero Visibility

requirements.name: Cost of Doing Nothing Stats
requirements.description: The page SHALL quantify the cost of inaction with 3 stat blocks on a dark section background. - **GIVEN** the section labeled "06 The Cost of Doing Nothing" - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `.stats-panel` SHALL display exactly 3 `.stat-block` elements showing: annual revenue loss (₹5-20L), monthly hours wasted (60-100), and owner time on follow-ups (40%) - **GIVEN** the Cost of Doing Nothing section - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the section SHALL use class `section-dark` for inverted background treatment - **AND** stat numbers SHALL remain visible in dark mode via `[data-theme="dark"]` overrides

scenarios.then: the `.stats-panel` SHALL display exactly 3 `.stat-block` elements showing: annual revenue loss (₹5-20L), monthly hours wasted (60-100), and owner time on follow-ups (40%)

scenarios.then: the section SHALL use class `section-dark` for inverted background treatment

requirements.name: People-Driven vs Process-Driven Comparison
requirements.description: The page SHALL contrast 7 operational attributes between people-driven and process-driven businesses. - **GIVEN** the section labeled "08 People vs Process" - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `.pvp-grid` SHALL contain two `.pvp-col` columns: people-driven (danger border) and process-driven (green border) - **AND** each column SHALL display exactly 7 `.pvp-row` entries comparing the same operational dimensions

requirements.description: The page SHALL display a compact before-OptiFlow / after-OptiFlow comparison grid. - **GIVEN** the section labeled "09 Before & After" - **WHEN** the page renders - **THEN** the `.compact-compare` SHALL contain two `.compact-col` columns: before (red) and after (green) - **AND** each column SHALL display exactly 7 `.compact-row` entries with dot indicators

requirements.name: Full Dark Mode Support
requirements.description: All page-specific components SHALL have complete `[data-theme="dark"]` style overrides using CSS custom properties. - **GIVEN** the page's `<style>` block - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for at minimum: `.hook-badge`, `.pain-points`, `.chat-mock`, `.chaos-bubble` variants, `.section-dark`, `.pvp-label`, `.pvp-row`, `.compact-row`, `.module-tag`, `.industry-tag`, `.impact-tag`, `.roi-line`, `.compare-col`, `.compare-item`, `.sf-node` variants, `.chaos-src`, `.stat-block`, `.compact-col` - **GIVEN** the `[data-theme="dark"]` block - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** all color values SHALL use `color-mix()`, `var()`, or CSS custom properties - **AND** no raw hex values SHALL appear in dark mode rules

scenarios.name: Dark mode overrides exist
scenarios.then: `[data-theme="dark"]` rules SHALL exist for at minimum: `.hook-badge`, `.pain-points`, `.chat-mock`, `.chaos-bubble` variants, `.section-dark`, `.pvp-label`, `.pvp-row`, `.compact-row`, `.module-tag`, `.industry-tag`, `.impact-tag`, `.roi-line`, `.compare-col`, `.compare-item`, `.sf-node` variants, `.chaos-src`, `.stat-block`, `.compact-col`

scenarios.name: No hardcoded dark hex colors
scenarios.then: all color values SHALL use `color-mix()`, `var()`, or CSS custom properties

requirements.name: Resources Dropdown Includes Competitive Positioning
requirements.description: The Resources dropdown in both desktop nav and mobile drawer SHALL contain Newsletter, FAQ, and Competitive Positioning. - **GIVEN** the nav is rendered - **WHEN** the Resources dropdown is opened - **THEN** it SHALL display "Newsletter", "FAQ", and "Competitive Positioning" - **AND** the Competitive Positioning link SHALL point to `/competitive-positioning/`

scenarios.name: Resources dropdown content updated

requirements.name: Footer Includes Competitive Positioning Link
requirements.description: The footer Resource links SHALL include a link to the Competitive Positioning page. - **GIVEN** the footer partial - **WHEN** inspected - **THEN** the Resources column SHALL include "Competitive Positioning" link pointing to `/competitive-positioning/`

scenarios.name: Footer resource links
scenarios.then: the Resources column SHALL include "Competitive Positioning" link pointing to `/competitive-positioning/`

requirements.name: Auto-Discovery
requirements.description: The engine SHALL auto-discover all global repository resources at startup, including skills, agents, MCP servers, hooks, rules, and capability specs. - **GIVEN** the `/opsx:explore` command is invoked - **WHEN** the engine initializes - **THEN** all 67 agents, 271+ skills, 33 MCP servers, 28 hooks, and 113 rules SHALL be indexed - **AND** each resource SHALL be tagged with its domain (design, frontend, build, accessibility, seo, security) - **AND** all 7 capability specs SHALL be loaded into the routing matrix - **GIVEN** the current git branch is `feature/new-page` - **WHEN** the engine auto-discovers resources - **THEN** the pipeline SHALL select the feature-branch pipeline configuration (skip E2E, run full unit + integration) - **AND** the production guard SHALL be enforced (block release.yaml on non-main branches)

scenarios.name: Resource index on explore
scenarios.then: all 67 agents, 271+ skills, 33 MCP servers, 28 hooks, and 113 rules SHALL be indexed

scenarios.name: Branch-aware context
scenarios.then: the pipeline SHALL select the feature-branch pipeline configuration (skip E2E, run full unit + integration)

requirements.name: Spec-Driven Routing
requirements.description: The engine SHALL route skills, agents, and MCPs to tasks based on which capability specs are affected by the change. - **GIVEN** a change affects `openspec/specs/design-system/spec.md` - **WHEN** `/opsx:propose design-update` is executed - **THEN** the engine SHALL route to the design-system skill and the planner agent - **AND** the design-system MCP server SHALL be activated for validation - **GIVEN** a change affects both `seo` and `accessibility` specs - **WHEN** `/opsx:apply` is executed - **THEN** the engine SHALL route to seo + accessibility skills in parallel - **AND** the code-reviewer and security-reviewer agents SHALL both review the output

scenarios.name: Design system change routing
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL route to the design-system skill and the planner agent

scenarios.name: Multi-spec change routing
scenarios.given: a change affects both `seo` and `accessibility` specs
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL route to seo + accessibility skills in parallel

requirements.name: Pipeline Execution
requirements.description: The engine SHALL execute YAML pipeline definitions with DAG (directed acyclic graph) support, resolving dependencies and running independent stages in parallel. - **GIVEN** the `build.yaml` pipeline defines stages: build → validate → test - **WHEN** the pipeline is executed - **THEN** validate SHALL wait for build to complete - **AND** test SHALL wait for validate to complete - **AND** each stage failure SHALL halt subsequent stages - **GIVEN** the `audit.yaml` pipeline defines parallel stages: a11y-audit, seo-audit, design-audit - **WHEN** the pipeline is executed - **THEN** all three audit stages SHALL run simultaneously - **AND** the pipeline SHALL complete only when all stages finish - **AND** any stage failure SHALL be reported with the stage name and error detail

scenarios.name: Build pipeline DAG
scenarios.given: the `build.yaml` pipeline defines stages: build → validate → test
scenarios.then: validate SHALL wait for build to complete

scenarios.name: Audit pipeline parallelism
scenarios.given: the `audit.yaml` pipeline defines parallel stages: a11y-audit, seo-audit, design-audit
scenarios.then: all three audit stages SHALL run simultaneously

requirements.name: Validation Pipeline
requirements.description: The engine SHALL support L1 through L7 validation levels, each gating on the previous level's success. - **GIVEN** the `/validate-full` command is invoked - **WHEN** the validation pipeline runs - **THEN** L1 (lint) SHALL run first; failure halts the pipeline - **AND** L2 (build) SHALL run after L1 passes - **AND** L3 (unit tests) SHALL run after L2 passes - **AND** L4 (integration) SHALL run after L3 passes - **AND** L5 (a11y audit) SHALL run after L4 passes - **AND** L6 (SEO audit) SHALL run after L5 passes - **AND** L7 (E2E tests) SHALL run after L6 passes - **GIVEN** validation level L4 is requested - **WHEN** the validation pipeline runs - **THEN** only L1 through L4 SHALL execute - **AND** L5-L7 SHALL be skipped

scenarios.name: Full L1-L7 validation
scenarios.then: L1 (lint) SHALL run first; failure halts the pipeline

scenarios.name: Partial validation
scenarios.then: only L1 through L4 SHALL execute

requirements.name: Quality Gates
requirements.description: The engine SHALL enforce multi-gate quality enforcement: GATE_SPEC → GATE_BUILD → GATE_VALIDATE → GATE_TEST → GATE_A11Y → GATE_PERF → GATE_SECURITY → GATE_HUMAN. - **GIVEN** a change passes GATE_SPEC but fails GATE_BUILD - **WHEN** the quality pipeline runs - **THEN** the change SHALL stop at GATE_BUILD - **AND** the failure reason SHALL be emitted - **AND** no subsequent gates SHALL execute - **GIVEN** all automated gates pass except GATE_HUMAN - **WHEN** the human reviewer is pending - **THEN** the pipeline SHALL emit a review-required notice - **AND** execution SHALL resume only after human approval

scenarios.name: Gate chain enforcement
scenarios.given: a change passes GATE_SPEC but fails GATE_BUILD
scenarios.then: the change SHALL stop at GATE_BUILD

scenarios.name: Human gate override
scenarios.given: all automated gates pass except GATE_HUMAN
scenarios.then: the pipeline SHALL emit a review-required notice

requirements.name: Traceability
requirements.description: The engine SHALL link specs to tasks, tasks to git commits, and commits to changed files, producing a full traceability matrix. - **GIVEN** a task from `openspec/changes/update-header/tasks.md` is completed - **WHEN** the task is committed as `feat: update nav header` - **THEN** the traceability matrix SHALL record: `spec.md#header-requirement → tasks.md#T3 → commit abc123 → src/pages/index.html` - **AND** any unlinked commit SHALL be flagged during `/opsx:verify` - **GIVEN** a change is archived via `/opsx:archive` - **WHEN** the archive process completes - **THEN** a traceability report SHALL be generated listing all spec deltas merged - **AND** the report SHALL include the full chain for each delta

scenarios.name: Commit traceability
scenarios.when: the task is committed as `feat: update nav header`
scenarios.then: the traceability matrix SHALL record: `spec.md#header-requirement → tasks.md#T3 → commit abc123 → src/pages/index.html`

scenarios.name: Archive traceability report
scenarios.then: a traceability report SHALL be generated listing all spec deltas merged

requirements.name: Phase Detection
requirements.description: The engine SHALL detect the correct OpenSpec phase from the current change state (explore, propose, apply, verify, archive) and prevent phase-skipping. - **GIVEN** no prior `/opsx:explore` has been run - **WHEN** `/opsx:propose new-feature` is invoked - **THEN** the engine SHALL warn that exploration has not been done - **AND** the engine SHALL auto-run explore before proceeding - **GIVEN** a change has been applied but not verified - **WHEN** `/opsx:archive` is invoked - **THEN** the engine SHALL reject the archive request - **AND** the engine SHALL emit: "Run /opsx:verify first"

scenarios.name: Must explore before propose
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL warn that exploration has not been done

scenarios.name: Must verify before archive
scenarios.given: a change has been applied but not verified
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL reject the archive request

requirements.name: Command Auto-Orchestration
requirements.description: The user SHALL run a single OpenSpec command; the engine SHALL handle all subordinate routing, validation, and quality gating automatically. - **GIVEN** the user runs `/orchestrate "add testimonial section to homepage"` - **WHEN** the orchestration engine processes the request - **THEN** the engine SHALL auto-discover affected specs (marketing-pages, design-system, shared-components) - **AND** the engine SHALL route to planner → tdd-guide → code-reviewer → security-reviewer in sequence - **AND** the engine SHALL run pre-build validation before any code changes - **AND** the engine SHALL run post-build validation after implementation - **AND** the engine SHALL enforce all quality gates before completion - **GIVEN** the `/opsx:apply` command is invoked - **WHEN** tasks.md contains tasks tagged with `@agent:tdd-guide`, `@skill:design-system`, `@mcp:context7` - **THEN** the engine SHALL route each task to the specified resource - **AND** tasks with no explicit routing SHALL be auto-routed based on affected specs - **AND** the engine SHALL run hooks for each file change automatically

scenarios.name: Single command full pipeline
scenarios.given: the user runs `/orchestrate "add testimonial section to homepage"`
scenarios.when: the orchestration engine processes the request
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL auto-discover affected specs (marketing-pages, design-system, shared-components)

scenarios.name: Command chain execution
scenarios.then: the engine SHALL route each task to the specified resource

requirements.name: Preconnect Resource Hints
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL inject `<link rel="preconnect">` hints for third-party origins into every page's `<head>`. - **WHEN** a page is assembled - **THEN** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be injected - **AND** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>` SHALL be injected - **WHEN** a page is assembled with Plausible analytics - **THEN** `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://plausible.io">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Google Fonts preconnect
scenarios.then: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">` SHALL be injected

scenarios.name: Analytics preconnect
scenarios.when: a page is assembled with Plausible analytics
scenarios.then: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://plausible.io">` SHALL be injected

requirements.name: Lazy Image Loading
requirements.description: Images below the viewport fold SHALL use `loading="lazy"` to defer loading. - **WHEN** an `` tag appears outside the initial viewport on content pages (feature-showcase, product-overview, competitive-positioning) - **THEN** the image SHALL include `loading="lazy"` attribute - **WHEN** an `` tag appears in the hero section or above fold - **THEN** the image SHALL NOT include `loading="lazy"`

scenarios.name: Below-fold images lazily loaded
scenarios.when: an `` tag appears outside the initial viewport on content pages (feature-showcase, product-overview, competitive-positioning)
scenarios.then: the image SHALL include `loading="lazy"` attribute

scenarios.name: Above-fold images remain eager
scenarios.when: an `` tag appears in the hero section or above fold
scenarios.then: the image SHALL NOT include `loading="lazy"`

requirements.name: CSS and JS Minification
requirements.description: The build pipeline SHALL minify CSS and JavaScript assets during production build. - **WHEN** `npm run build` is executed - **THEN** `dist/assets/css/core.css` SHALL be minified (whitespace and comments removed) - **AND** `dist/assets/js/core.js` SHALL be minified - **AND** source files in `assets/` SHALL remain unminified for development - **WHEN** minification is applied to core.css (current ~26KB) and core.js (current ~18KB) - **THEN** the combined output SHALL be at least 20% smaller than unminified versions

scenarios.name: Build-time minification
scenarios.then: `dist/assets/css/core.css` SHALL be minified (whitespace and comments removed)

scenarios.name: Minification reduces size
scenarios.when: minification is applied to core.css (current ~26KB) and core.js (current ~18KB)
scenarios.then: the combined output SHALL be at least 20% smaller than unminified versions

requirements.name: GATE_PERF Quality Gate
requirements.description: The orchestration engine SHALL include a GATE_PERF step that validates performance budgets. - **WHEN** the build pipeline config runs GATE_PERF - **THEN** it SHALL check that CSS + JS combined size does not exceed 100KB - **AND** it SHALL verify Lighthouse Performance score >= 0.8 for all configured pages

scenarios.name: Performance gate in build pipeline
scenarios.when: the build pipeline config runs GATE_PERF
scenarios.then: it SHALL check that CSS + JS combined size does not exceed 100KB

requirements.name: Dependency Vulnerability Monitoring
requirements.description: The platform SHALL include `npm audit` scanning as part of the CI pipeline to detect known vulnerabilities in Node.js dependencies. - **WHEN** the CI pipeline runs on a pull request - **THEN** `npm audit --production` SHALL execute after test steps - **AND** HIGH or CRITICAL vulnerabilities SHALL cause the step to fail - **WHEN** `npm audit --production` completes - **THEN** a summary of vulnerabilities found SHALL be displayed in CI logs

scenarios.name: Audit runs in CI
scenarios.when: the CI pipeline runs on a pull request
scenarios.then: `npm audit --production` SHALL execute after test steps

scenarios.name: Audit report is visible
scenarios.then: a summary of vulnerabilities found SHALL be displayed in CI logs

requirements.name: Unique Title Tag
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have a unique, descriptive `<title>` tag. - **GIVEN** the `site.json` pages array - **WHEN** any page is assembled - **THEN** the `<title>` SHALL match the page's `title` field exactly - **AND** no two pages SHALL share the same title

scenarios.name: Title uniqueness
scenarios.then: the `<title>` SHALL match the page's `title` field exactly

requirements.name: Unique Meta Description
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have a unique meta description between 120 and 160 characters. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** a `<meta name="description" content="...">` tag SHALL be present - **AND** the description SHALL come from the page's `description` field in `site.json` - **AND** it SHALL be between 120 and 160 characters in length - **AND** the validation script SHALL error if any page's description is shorter than 120 or longer than 160 characters

scenarios.name: Description presence and length
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="description" content="...">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Tags
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have complete OpenGraph meta tags. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** `og:title` SHALL be present and populated - **AND** `og:description` SHALL match the page's meta description - **AND** `og:image` SHALL point to the OptiFlow logo (`/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png`) - **AND** `og:type` SHALL be set to "website" (or "article" for the newsletter page) - **AND** `og:url` SHALL be the page's canonical absolute URL - **AND** `og:site_name` SHALL be "OptiFlow OS" - **AND** `og:locale` SHALL be "en_IN" - **AND** `og:image:width` and `og:image:height` SHALL be present with values 512

scenarios.name: OG tag presence
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: `og:title` SHALL be present and populated

requirements.name: Twitter Card
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have a Twitter card meta tag. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on Twitter/X - **THEN** a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: Twitter card presence
scenarios.when: the page is shared on Twitter/X
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: Single H1 Element
requirements.description: Every page SHALL have exactly one `<h1>` element. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** exactly one `<h1>` element SHALL exist - **AND** it SHALL contain the primary page heading

scenarios.name: H1 uniqueness
scenarios.then: exactly one `<h1>` element SHALL exist

requirements.name: Clean URLs
requirements.description: The system SHALL produce search-engine-friendly, directory-based URLs without `.html` extensions. - **GIVEN** a page defined in `site.json` as `/features/index.html` - **WHEN** the page is built - **THEN** the accessible URL SHALL be `/features/` - **AND** no `.html` extension SHALL appear in any navigation link

scenarios.name: URL structure
scenarios.then: the accessible URL SHALL be `/features/`

requirements.name: Canonical URL Tag
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include a canonical URL meta tag pointing to its absolute URL. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** a `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>` - **AND** the href SHALL be the absolute URL matching the page's site.json file path

scenarios.name: Canonical URL present
scenarios.then: a `<link rel="canonical" href="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

requirements.name: Robots Meta Tag
requirements.description: Every public page SHALL include a robots meta tag allowing indexing. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is inspected - **THEN** a `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

scenarios.name: Robots tag present on all pages
scenarios.then: a `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` tag SHALL be present in the `<head>`

requirements.name: OpenGraph URL
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include an `og:url` meta tag with its canonical URL. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** a `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present - **AND** the content SHALL match the page's canonical URL

scenarios.name: OG URL present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: a `<meta property="og:url" content="https://optiflow.in/<path>/">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Site Name
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include an `og:site_name` meta tag. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** a `<meta property="og:site_name" content="OptiFlow OS">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: OG site name present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: a `<meta property="og:site_name" content="OptiFlow OS">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Locale
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include an `og:locale` meta tag set to `en_IN`. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** a `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_IN">` tag SHALL be present

scenarios.name: OG locale present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: a `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_IN">` tag SHALL be present

requirements.name: OpenGraph Image Dimensions
requirements.description: The OG image meta tag SHALL include width and height attributes for complete social preview rendering. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on social media - **THEN** `<meta property="og:image:width" content="512">` and `<meta property="og:image:height" content="512">` tags SHALL be present

scenarios.name: OG image dimensions present
scenarios.when: the page is shared on social media
scenarios.then: `<meta property="og:image:width" content="512">` and `<meta property="og:image:height" content="512">` tags SHALL be present

requirements.name: Twitter Image Card
requirements.description: Every page SHALL include `twitter:image` and `twitter:image:alt` meta tags. - **GIVEN** any assembled page - **WHEN** the page is shared on Twitter/X - **THEN** a `<meta name="twitter:image" content="/assets/img/OptiFlow.Logo.png">` tag SHALL be present - **AND** a `<meta name="twitter:image:alt" content="OptiFlow OS Logo">` tag SHALL be present
